axis_descriptions.entity: Extract every important person/character in the range once each, merging recurrences into one canonical entry. Include foreground people/characters, speakers, addressed people/characters, partially visible but important interactors, and entities needed to understand actions, conflicts, portrayals, or relationships. Do not skip an unknown-name entity if it is distinguishable or narratively important. Skip background extras with no interaction or story function. person = real individual, character = fictional/performed role; if both a real person and their portrayed character are identifiable, emit them separately. Appearance segmentation — entities are extracted segment by segment, so a lead who recurs in every segment can crowd out the people around them. Treat each NEW person/character's first appearance as the start of a new entity segment. When several people appear at once, split that window into shorter appearance intervals and emit EVERY person in it — including secondary, briefly-seen, or interacting-but-not-central people — not only the most prominent one.
axis_descriptions.shot: This is the 1st of 5 parts of the video, and it is 500 seconds long. Segment this part into granular, contiguous shots using visual and audio cues: cuts, major camera-motion changes, event/action beats, dialogue or speaker turns, subject/framing/setting changes, and entity entries/exits. When unsure, prefer shorter coherent shots. Cover this part end to end with no gaps: the FIRST shot must start at 0 and the LAST shot must end at 500 (the full length of this part). Do not omit or trim static or silent spans at the head or tail.
axis_descriptions.video: This is the 1st of 5 parts of the video. Emit exactly ONE segment covering this whole part, using chunk-relative times: `start_time=0`, `end_time=500`.

segment_definitions.description: Extract every important person/character in the range once each, merging recurrences into one canonical entry. Include foreground people/characters, speakers, addressed people/characters, partially visible but important interactors, and entities needed to understand actions, conflicts, portrayals, or relationships. Do not skip an unknown-name entity if it is distinguishable or narratively important. Skip background extras with no interaction or story function. person = real individual, character = fictional/performed role; if both a real person and their portrayed character are identifiable, emit them separately. Appearance segmentation — entities are extracted segment by segment, so a lead who recurs in every segment can crowd out the people around them. Treat each NEW person/character's first appearance as the start of a new entity segment. When several people appear at once, split that window into shorter appearance intervals and emit EVERY person in it — including secondary, briefly-seen, or interacting-but-not-central people — not only the most prominent one.

fields.name: canonical_name
fields.description: Name, in order of preference: (1) the entity's full name (a real person's real name, or a character's in-story name); (2) a name spoken or shown on screen; (3) a descriptive label built from stable, distinguishing traits (e.g. 'bald older guard', 'red-haired teen') — avoid unstable traits like clothing. These entities are later merged across videos by matching name, so the name must be specific enough that two DIFFERENT people never collide on the same name: never a bare generic ('man', 'woman', 'person'), which would wrongly merge unrelated people. One name per entity; the description elaborates further.

fields.name: entity_type
fields.description: Whether this entity is a person or a character.

fields.name: description
fields.description: Identifying description of this entity. Elaborate the entity to support its name and future cross-video merging: stable appearance, role, key relationships/interactions, and any grounded aliases or names heard. Prefer visible, spoken, OCR, or credited evidence over guesses.

fields.name: relationships
fields.description: Each relationship starting FROM this entity, output exactly as `dst_name;dst_type;rel_type;desc` (empty array if none). dst_name / dst_type must copy another emitted entity verbatim. rel_type is `actor_portrays_character` (person -> character only; desc must be `other`) or `has_social_relationship` (same type only). desc must be exactly one of family / friend / colleague / acquaintance / rival / other. Do not add evidence text, labels, colons, extra semicolons, or any value outside the enum in this field. Put relationship evidence in the entity description instead. Emit only clearly evidenced ties, not mere co-presence; emit symmetric social ties once.

fields.name: best_thumbnail
fields.description: Best, clearest, most front-facing frame of the entity. Output a list of 5 numbers `[time_sec, y_min, x_min, y_max, x_max]`: time_sec is seconds from the chunk start; the box is normalized to [0,1000] (top-left, bottom-right) and tightly encloses the entity. Output [] if not clearly visible.

fields.name: second_best_thumbnail
fields.description: A frame as different as possible from best_thumbnail — a different time, ideally a different shot, and a different pose / angle / expression. Output a list of 5 numbers `[time_sec, y_min, x_min, y_max, x_max]`: time_sec is seconds from the chunk start; the box is normalized to [0,1000] (top-left, bottom-right) and tightly encloses the entity. Output [] if not clearly visible.

fields.name: third_best_thumbnail
fields.description: A frame distinct from and far in time from both other thumbnails, so the three span the entity's varied appearances. Output a list of 5 numbers `[time_sec, y_min, x_min, y_max, x_max]`: time_sec is seconds from the chunk start; the box is normalized to [0,1000] (top-left, bottom-right) and tightly encloses the entity. Output [] if not clearly visible.

segment_definitions.description: This is the 1st of 5 parts of the video, and it is 500 seconds long. Segment this part into granular, contiguous shots using visual and audio cues: cuts, major camera-motion changes, event/action beats, dialogue or speaker turns, subject/framing/setting changes, and entity entries/exits. When unsure, prefer shorter coherent shots. Cover this part end to end with no gaps: the FIRST shot must start at 0 and the LAST shot must end at 500 (the full length of this part). Do not omit or trim static or silent spans at the head or tail.

fields.name: description
fields.description: Describe this shot in rich, verbose detail — cover every meaningful visual and non-visual detail: the actions and interactions, objects, setting, on-screen text, and any spoken dialogue quoted verbatim (with the speaker when certain). Refer to each person or character by the EXACT same name you give it in the `entities` field. Write enough that the shot stands on its own and, read in order, the shots reconstruct the whole story.

fields.name: shot_type
fields.description: The type of the shot.

fields.name: camera_motion
fields.description: The camera motion of the shot.

fields.name: camera_angle
fields.description: The camera angle of the shot.

fields.name: entities
fields.description: Each entity evidenced in this shot, output exactly as `name;entity_type;description`. Include only entities visible, audible, or OCR-referenced in this shot: foreground or speaking persons/characters, important addressed or interacted-with people/characters, and entities needed to understand the action, conflict, portrayal, or relationship; also central places, objects, concepts, or things when they drive the shot's meaning. entity_type is one of person / character / place / object / concept / thing, lowercase. Use exactly two semicolons as separators; do not put semicolons inside name, entity_type, or description (use commas or dashes). For the `name` element (person/character): Name, in order of preference: (1) the entity's full name (a real person's real name, or a character's in-story name); (2) a name spoken or shown on screen; (3) a descriptive label built from stable, distinguishing traits (e.g. 'bald older guard', 'red-haired teen') — avoid unstable traits like clothing. Decide from all available context, not this shot alone. The name must be specific enough that two DIFFERENT people never collide on the same name: never a bare generic ('man', 'woman', 'person'). **Use that entity's exact same name in every shot.** For the `description` element: Describe the entity for IDENTIFICATION — so that, from descriptions alone, one can tell which mentions across shots are the SAME person. Write SEVERAL sentences and pack in AS MANY stable identifying properties as you can: any name or alias heard, role, gender, approximate age, ethnicity, hair (color/length/style), facial features, build/height, distinctive marks, and especially their relationship or recurring interaction with other identified entities (e.g. 'the protagonist's mother', 'the man who repeatedly argues with the bald guard'), which strongly anchors identity. Do NOT describe clothing, location, or momentary physical action — they change and do not help identification.

segment_definitions.description: This is the 1st of 5 parts of the video. Emit exactly ONE segment covering this whole part, using chunk-relative times: `start_time=0`, `end_time=500`.

fields.name: title
fields.description: Title of the whole video; best guess from in-chunk evidence (titles / credits / dialogue).

fields.name: description
fields.description: Summarize this chunk using only evidence from the emitted shot segments — cover essential story beats, key people/characters, important relationships/conflicts, locations, objects, and short grounded dialogue/OCR facts; do not introduce unsupported facts. Integrate events, objects, story context, visual detail, and audio/OCR/transcription facts into one account: names, relationships, intentions, conflicts, interaction evidence, and production cues like 'Action!'/'Cut!' when evidenced. Quote only short exact phrases; do not reproduce the transcript or assert an uncertain speaker.

fields.name: work
fields.description: Optional work title inferred from this chunk (for root candidate materialization).

metadata.title: Arcane: League of Legends
metadata.description: The video opens with a dark, red-hued flashback depicting a catastrophic event on a bridge, where a masked enforcer walks through destruction and fire. Two young girls, Vi and Powder, witness the aftermath and are comforted by a bearded man, Vander, who embraces them as they cry. The scene transitions to the show's title sequence, 'Arcane: League of Legends,' featuring claymation-style statues of characters and credits for Riot Games and Fortiche. The narrative then shifts to a bright, sunny day in the city of Piltover, where Vi, Powder, and their friends Ekko and Mylo are on a rooftop. They admire the cityscape and airships before deciding to break into a building. Vi leads the group across rooftops, and after Powder nearly falls, Vi saves her. They reach a balcony where Ekko picks the lock, allowing them to enter the building as they discuss the risks of their heist.

gemini_result.title: Arcane Opening Sequence and First Scene
gemini_result.description: The video begins with a record player, then transitions to a dark, red-lit scene on a bridge where a battle has taken place. Two young girls, Vi and Powder, are found amidst the wreckage by a large man named Vander. He carries them away from the carnage. The scene then transitions to the opening credits sequence of the show Arcane. After the credits, Vi, Powder, and two other boys climb up to a rooftop overlooking the bright city of Piltover.

axis_descriptions.entity: Extract every important person/character in the range once each, merging recurrences into one canonical entry. Include foreground people/characters, speakers, addressed people/characters, partially visible but important interactors, and entities needed to understand actions, conflicts, portrayals, or relationships. Do not skip an unknown-name entity if it is distinguishable or narratively important. Skip background extras with no interaction or story function. person = real individual, character = fictional/performed role; if both a real person and their portrayed character are identifiable, emit them separately. Appearance segmentation — entities are extracted segment by segment, so a lead who recurs in every segment can crowd out the people around them. Treat each NEW person/character's first appearance as the start of a new entity segment. When several people appear at once, split that window into shorter appearance intervals and emit EVERY person in it — including secondary, briefly-seen, or interacting-but-not-central people — not only the most prominent one.
axis_descriptions.shot: This is the 2nd of 5 parts of the video, and it is 500 seconds long. Segment this part into granular, contiguous shots using visual and audio cues: cuts, major camera-motion changes, event/action beats, dialogue or speaker turns, subject/framing/setting changes, and entity entries/exits. When unsure, prefer shorter coherent shots. Cover this part end to end with no gaps: the FIRST shot must start at 0 and the LAST shot must end at 500 (the full length of this part). Do not omit or trim static or silent spans at the head or tail.
axis_descriptions.video: This is the 2nd of 5 parts of the video. Emit exactly ONE segment covering this whole part, using chunk-relative times: `start_time=0`, `end_time=500`.

segment_definitions.description: Extract every important person/character in the range once each, merging recurrences into one canonical entry. Include foreground people/characters, speakers, addressed people/characters, partially visible but important interactors, and entities needed to understand actions, conflicts, portrayals, or relationships. Do not skip an unknown-name entity if it is distinguishable or narratively important. Skip background extras with no interaction or story function. person = real individual, character = fictional/performed role; if both a real person and their portrayed character are identifiable, emit them separately. Appearance segmentation — entities are extracted segment by segment, so a lead who recurs in every segment can crowd out the people around them. Treat each NEW person/character's first appearance as the start of a new entity segment. When several people appear at once, split that window into shorter appearance intervals and emit EVERY person in it — including secondary, briefly-seen, or interacting-but-not-central people — not only the most prominent one.

fields.name: canonical_name
fields.description: Name, in order of preference: (1) the entity's full name (a real person's real name, or a character's in-story name); (2) a name spoken or shown on screen; (3) a descriptive label built from stable, distinguishing traits (e.g. 'bald older guard', 'red-haired teen') — avoid unstable traits like clothing. These entities are later merged across videos by matching name, so the name must be specific enough that two DIFFERENT people never collide on the same name: never a bare generic ('man', 'woman', 'person'), which would wrongly merge unrelated people. One name per entity; the description elaborates further.

fields.name: entity_type
fields.description: Whether this entity is a person or a character.

fields.name: description
fields.description: Identifying description of this entity. Elaborate the entity to support its name and future cross-video merging: stable appearance, role, key relationships/interactions, and any grounded aliases or names heard. Prefer visible, spoken, OCR, or credited evidence over guesses.

fields.name: relationships
fields.description: Each relationship starting FROM this entity, output exactly as `dst_name;dst_type;rel_type;desc` (empty array if none). dst_name / dst_type must copy another emitted entity verbatim. rel_type is `actor_portrays_character` (person -> character only; desc must be `other`) or `has_social_relationship` (same type only). desc must be exactly one of family / friend / colleague / acquaintance / rival / other. Do not add evidence text, labels, colons, extra semicolons, or any value outside the enum in this field. Put relationship evidence in the entity description instead. Emit only clearly evidenced ties, not mere co-presence; emit symmetric social ties once.

fields.name: best_thumbnail
fields.description: Best, clearest, most front-facing frame of the entity. Output a list of 5 numbers `[time_sec, y_min, x_min, y_max, x_max]`: time_sec is seconds from the chunk start; the box is normalized to [0,1000] (top-left, bottom-right) and tightly encloses the entity. Output [] if not clearly visible.

fields.name: second_best_thumbnail
fields.description: A frame as different as possible from best_thumbnail — a different time, ideally a different shot, and a different pose / angle / expression. Output a list of 5 numbers `[time_sec, y_min, x_min, y_max, x_max]`: time_sec is seconds from the chunk start; the box is normalized to [0,1000] (top-left, bottom-right) and tightly encloses the entity. Output [] if not clearly visible.

fields.name: third_best_thumbnail
fields.description: A frame distinct from and far in time from both other thumbnails, so the three span the entity's varied appearances. Output a list of 5 numbers `[time_sec, y_min, x_min, y_max, x_max]`: time_sec is seconds from the chunk start; the box is normalized to [0,1000] (top-left, bottom-right) and tightly encloses the entity. Output [] if not clearly visible.

segment_definitions.description: This is the 2nd of 5 parts of the video, and it is 500 seconds long. Segment this part into granular, contiguous shots using visual and audio cues: cuts, major camera-motion changes, event/action beats, dialogue or speaker turns, subject/framing/setting changes, and entity entries/exits. When unsure, prefer shorter coherent shots. Cover this part end to end with no gaps: the FIRST shot must start at 0 and the LAST shot must end at 500 (the full length of this part). Do not omit or trim static or silent spans at the head or tail.

fields.name: description
fields.description: Describe this shot in rich, verbose detail — cover every meaningful visual and non-visual detail: the actions and interactions, objects, setting, on-screen text, and any spoken dialogue quoted verbatim (with the speaker when certain). Refer to each person or character by the EXACT same name you give it in the `entities` field. Write enough that the shot stands on its own and, read in order, the shots reconstruct the whole story.

fields.name: shot_type
fields.description: The type of the shot.

fields.name: camera_motion
fields.description: The camera motion of the shot.

fields.name: camera_angle
fields.description: The camera angle of the shot.

fields.name: entities
fields.description: Each entity evidenced in this shot, output exactly as `name;entity_type;description`. Include only entities visible, audible, or OCR-referenced in this shot: foreground or speaking persons/characters, important addressed or interacted-with people/characters, and entities needed to understand the action, conflict, portrayal, or relationship; also central places, objects, concepts, or things when they drive the shot's meaning. entity_type is one of person / character / place / object / concept / thing, lowercase. Use exactly two semicolons as separators; do not put semicolons inside name, entity_type, or description (use commas or dashes). For the `name` element (person/character): Name, in order of preference: (1) the entity's full name (a real person's real name, or a character's in-story name); (2) a name spoken or shown on screen; (3) a descriptive label built from stable, distinguishing traits (e.g. 'bald older guard', 'red-haired teen') — avoid unstable traits like clothing. Decide from all available context, not this shot alone. The name must be specific enough that two DIFFERENT people never collide on the same name: never a bare generic ('man', 'woman', 'person'). **Use that entity's exact same name in every shot.** For the `description` element: Describe the entity for IDENTIFICATION — so that, from descriptions alone, one can tell which mentions across shots are the SAME person. Write SEVERAL sentences and pack in AS MANY stable identifying properties as you can: any name or alias heard, role, gender, approximate age, ethnicity, hair (color/length/style), facial features, build/height, distinctive marks, and especially their relationship or recurring interaction with other identified entities (e.g. 'the protagonist's mother', 'the man who repeatedly argues with the bald guard'), which strongly anchors identity. Do NOT describe clothing, location, or momentary physical action — they change and do not help identification.

segment_definitions.description: This is the 2nd of 5 parts of the video. Emit exactly ONE segment covering this whole part, using chunk-relative times: `start_time=0`, `end_time=500`.

fields.name: title
fields.description: Title of the whole video; best guess from in-chunk evidence (titles / credits / dialogue).

fields.name: description
fields.description: Summarize this chunk using only evidence from the emitted shot segments — cover essential story beats, key people/characters, important relationships/conflicts, locations, objects, and short grounded dialogue/OCR facts; do not introduce unsupported facts. Integrate events, objects, story context, visual detail, and audio/OCR/transcription facts into one account: names, relationships, intentions, conflicts, interaction evidence, and production cues like 'Action!'/'Cut!' when evidenced. Quote only short exact phrases; do not reproduce the transcript or assert an uncertain speaker.

fields.name: work
fields.description: Optional work title inferred from this chunk (for root candidate materialization).

params.summary_prompt: Summarize the video for segmentation context.

metadata.description: A young woman with short pink hair, wearing a maroon vest over a light shirt, and dark pants. She is a member of the group of thieves.
A uniformed guard in a blue and gold uniform with a tall hat. They are pursuing the group of thieves.
A young man with blond hair, wearing a dark vest and pants. He is a gang leader in the Undercity.

The scene opens with a close-up of a young man with dark hair and a bandaged arm, looking out a window. The camera then cuts to a wide shot of a room filled with scientific equipment, where four young people stand looking around in awe. The group consists of a girl with pink hair, a boy with goggles, a girl with blue hair, and the boy with the bandaged arm.

metadata.title: The Heist and the Chase
metadata.description: The video follows a group of four young thieves—Caitlyn, Vi, Jinx, and a fourth member—as they break into a lavish inventor's workshop in Piltover. They gather valuable items, including a 'real Valdaini' and a 'nose hair trimmer,' while Jinx discovers a chest of glowing blue crystals. Her tampering with a crystal triggers a massive explosion, forcing the group to flee from pursuing enforcers. After a chaotic chase through the city and a fall into a sewer, they escape to the Undercity. There, they are confronted by a local gang led by a blonde woman who demands a share of their loot, leading to a violent brawl where the group fights back to protect their haul.

gemini_result.title: Arcane - The Heist and Explosion
gemini_result.description: Vi, Powder, Mylo, and Claggor break into a wealthy apartment in Piltover to steal valuables. While the others gather loot, Powder finds a chest of glowing blue crystals. When someone tries to enter the apartment, Powder drops a crystal, causing a massive explosion that blows out the side of the building. The group flees across the rooftops and streets of Piltover, pursued by Enforcers.

axis_descriptions.entity: Extract every important person/character in the range once each, merging recurrences into one canonical entry. Include foreground people/characters, speakers, addressed people/characters, partially visible but important interactors, and entities needed to understand actions, conflicts, portrayals, or relationships. Do not skip an unknown-name entity if it is distinguishable or narratively important. Skip background extras with no interaction or story function. person = real individual, character = fictional/performed role; if both a real person and their portrayed character are identifiable, emit them separately. Appearance segmentation — entities are extracted segment by segment, so a lead who recurs in every segment can crowd out the people around them. Treat each NEW person/character's first appearance as the start of a new entity segment. When several people appear at once, split that window into shorter appearance intervals and emit EVERY person in it — including secondary, briefly-seen, or interacting-but-not-central people — not only the most prominent one.
axis_descriptions.shot: This is the 3rd of 5 parts of the video, and it is 499 seconds long. Segment this part into granular, contiguous shots using visual and audio cues: cuts, major camera-motion changes, event/action beats, dialogue or speaker turns, subject/framing/setting changes, and entity entries/exits. When unsure, prefer shorter coherent shots. Cover this part end to end with no gaps: the FIRST shot must start at 0 and the LAST shot must end at 499 (the full length of this part). Do not omit or trim static or silent spans at the head or tail.
axis_descriptions.video: This is the 3rd of 5 parts of the video. Emit exactly ONE segment covering this whole part, using chunk-relative times: `start_time=0`, `end_time=499`.

Do not skip an unknown-name entity if it is distinguishable or narratively important. Skip background extras with no interaction or story function. person = real individual, character = fictional/performed role; if both a real person and their portrayed character are identifiable, emit them separately. Appearance segmentation — entities are extracted segment by segment, so a lead who recurs in every segment can crowd out the people around them. Treat each NEW person/character's first appearance as the start of a new entity segment. When several people appear at once, split that window into shorter appearance intervals and emit EVERY person in it — including secondary, briefly-seen, or interacting-but-not-central people — not only the most prominent one.

fields.name: canonical_name
fields.description: Name, in order of preference: (1) the entity's full name (a real person's real name, or a character's in-story name); (2) a name spoken or shown on screen; (3) a descriptive label built from stable, distinguishing traits (e.g. 'bald older guard', 'red-haired teen') — avoid unstable traits like clothing. These entities are later merged across videos by matching name, so the name must be specific enough that two DIFFERENT people never collide on the same name: never a bare generic ('man', 'woman', 'person'), which would wrongly merge unrelated people. One name per entity; the description elaborates further.

fields.name: entity_type
fields.description: Whether this entity is a person or a character.

fields.name: description
fields.description: Identifying description of this entity. Elaborate the entity to support its name and future cross-video merging: stable appearance, role, key relationships/interactions, and any grounded aliases or names heard. Prefer visible, spoken, OCR, or credited evidence over guesses.

fields.name: relationships
fields.description: Each relationship starting FROM this entity, output exactly as `dst_name;dst_type;rel_type;desc` (empty array if none). dst_name / dst_type must copy another emitted entity verbatim. rel_type is `actor_portrays_character` (person -> character only; desc must be `other`) or `has_social_relationship` (same type only). desc must be exactly one of family / friend / colleague / acquaintance / rival / other. Do not add evidence text, labels, colons, extra semicolons, or any value outside the enum in this field. Put relationship evidence in the entity description instead. Emit only clearly evidenced ties, not mere co-presence; emit symmetric social ties once.

fields.name: best_thumbnail
fields.description: Best, clearest, most front-facing frame of the entity. Output a list of 5 numbers `[time_sec, y_min, x_min, y_max, x_max]`: time_sec is seconds from the chunk start; the box is normalized to [0,1000] (top-left, bottom-right) and tightly encloses the entity. Output [] if not clearly visible.

fields.name: second_best_thumbnail
fields.description: A frame as different as possible from best_thumbnail — a different time, ideally a different shot, and a different pose / angle / expression. Output a list of 5 numbers `[time_sec, y_min, x_min, y_max, x_max]`: time_sec is seconds from the chunk start; the box is normalized to [0,1000] (top-left, bottom-right) and tightly encloses the entity. Output [] if not clearly visible.

fields.name: third_best_thumbnail
fields.description: A frame distinct from and far in time from both other thumbnails, so the three span the entity's varied appearances. Output a list of 5 numbers `[time_sec, y_min, x_min, y_max, x_max]`: time_sec is seconds from the chunk start; the box is normalized to [0,1000] (top-left, bottom-right) and tightly encloses the entity. Output [] if not clearly visible.

fields.name: description
fields.description: Describe this shot in rich, verbose detail — cover every meaningful visual and non-visual detail: the actions and interactions, objects, setting, on-screen text, and any spoken dialogue quoted verbatim (with the speaker when certain). Refer to each person or character by the EXACT same name you give it in the `entities` field. Write enough that the shot stands on its own and, read in order, the shots reconstruct the whole story.

fields.name: shot_type
fields.description: The type of the shot.

fields.name: camera_motion
fields.description: The camera motion of the shot.

fields.name: camera_angle
fields.description: The camera angle of the shot.

fields.name: entities
fields.description: Each entity evidenced in this shot, output exactly as `name;entity_type;description`. Include only entities visible, audible, or OCR-referenced in this shot: foreground or speaking persons/characters, important addressed or interacted-with people/characters, and entities needed to understand the action, conflict, portrayal, or relationship; also central places, objects, concepts, or things when they drive the shot's meaning. entity_type is one of person / character / place / object / concept / thing, lowercase. Use exactly two semicolons as separators; do not put semicolons inside name, entity_type, or description (use commas or dashes). For the `name` element (person/character): Name, in order of preference: (1) the entity's full name (a real person's real name, or a character's in-story name); (2) a name spoken or shown on screen; (3) a descriptive label built from stable, distinguishing traits (e.g. 'bald older guard', 'red-haired teen') — avoid unstable traits like clothing. Decide from all available context, not this shot alone. The name must be specific enough that two DIFFERENT people never collide on the same name: never a bare generic ('man', 'woman', 'person'). **Use that entity's exact same name in every shot.** For the `description` element: Describe the entity for IDENTIFICATION — so that, from descriptions alone, one can tell which mentions across shots are the SAME person. Write SEVERAL sentences and pack in AS MANY stable identifying properties as you can: any name or alias heard, role, gender, approximate age, ethnicity, hair (color/length/style), facial features, build/height, distinctive marks, and especially their relationship or recurring interaction with other identified entities (e.g. 'the protagonist's mother', 'the man who repeatedly argues with the bald guard'), which strongly anchors identity. Do NOT describe clothing, location, or momentary physical action — they change and do not help identification.

metadata.title: The Heist and the Confrontation
metadata.description: This segment follows a group of four children—Vi, Powder, Mylo, and Claggor—after a botched heist in the industrial city of Zaun. The sequence begins with a violent confrontation where Vi defeats a blonde attacker, followed by a frantic chase through the city's docks and alleys. Powder accidentally triggers an explosion with a small robot, leading to a foot chase and a fall into the water. The group returns to their home, where they are confronted by their older brother, Vander. The narrative then shifts to a tense negotiation in a bar called 'The Last Drop,' where a merchant is cheated out of his payment by a group of thugs led by a man named Silco. The segment concludes with Vander reprimanding the children for their reckless actions, highlighting the class divide between the wealthy Piltover and the struggling Zaun.

gemini_result.title: Arcane - Fight and Escape
gemini_result.description: Vi, Claggor, and Mylo fight a group of thugs in an alley. Vi faces off against Deckard, who eventually backs down and runs away. Meanwhile, Powder is chased by another thug onto the docks. She tries to use her homemade bomb, Mouser, but it only produces pink smoke. In a panic, she throws the bag of stolen loot into the water to distract the thug, who then leaves. The group regroups, and Mylo berates Powder for losing the loot. They descend into the undercity of Zaun and walk through the bustling streets, eventually arriving at a bar called The Last Drop. Inside, Vander serves a drink to Huck, who then sits at a table to negotiate a deal with some unsavory characters.

axis_descriptions.entity: Extract every important person/character in the range once each, merging recurrences into one canonical entry. Include foreground people/characters, speakers, addressed people/characters, partially visible but important interactors, and entities needed to understand actions, conflicts, portrayals, or relationships. Do not skip an unknown-name entity if it is distinguishable or narratively important. Skip background extras with no interaction or story function. person = real individual, character = fictional/performed role; if both a real person and their portrayed character are identifiable, emit them separately. Appearance segmentation — entities are extracted segment by segment, so a lead who recurs in every segment can crowd out the people around them. Treat each NEW person/character's first appearance as the start of a new entity segment. When several people appear at once, split that window into shorter appearance intervals and emit EVERY person in it — including secondary, briefly-seen, or interacting-but-not-central people — not only the most prominent one.
axis_descriptions.shot: This is the 4th of 5 parts of the video, and it is 500 seconds long. Segment this part into granular, contiguous shots using visual and audio cues: cuts, major camera-motion changes, event/action beats, dialogue or speaker turns, subject/framing/setting changes, and entity entries/exits. When unsure, prefer shorter coherent shots. Cover this part end to end with no gaps: the FIRST shot must start at 0 and the LAST shot must end at 500 (the full length of this part). Do not omit or trim static or silent spans at the head or tail.
axis_descriptions.video: This is the 4th of 5 parts of the video. Emit exactly ONE segment covering this whole part, using chunk-relative times: `start_time=0`, `end_time=500`.

segment_definitions.description: Extract every important person/character in the range once each, merging recurrences into one canonical entry. Include foreground people/characters, speakers, addressed people/characters, partially visible but important interactors, and entities needed to understand actions, conflicts, portrayals, or relationships. Do not skip an unknown-name entity if it is distinguishable or narratively important. Skip background extras with no interaction or story function. person = real individual, character = fictional/performed role; if both a real person and their portrayed character are identifiable, emit them separately. Appearance segmentation — entities are extracted segment by segment, so a lead who recurs in every segment can crowd out the people around them. Treat each NEW person/character's first appearance as the start of a new entity segment. When several people appear at once, split that window into shorter appearance intervals and emit EVERY person in it — including secondary, briefly-seen, or interacting-but-not-central people — not only the most prominent one.

fields.name: canonical_name
fields.description: Name, in order of preference: (1) the entity's full name (a real person's real name, or a character's in-story name); (2) a name spoken or shown on screen; (3) a descriptive label built from stable, distinguishing traits (e.g. 'bald older guard', 'red-haired teen') — avoid unstable traits like clothing. These entities are later merged across videos by matching name, so the name must be specific enough that two DIFFERENT people never collide on the same name: never a bare generic ('man', 'woman', 'person'), which would wrongly merge unrelated people. One name per entity; the description elaborates further.

fields.name: entity_type
fields.description: Whether this entity is a person or a character.

fields.name: description
fields.description: Identifying description of this entity. Elaborate the entity to support its name and future cross-video merging: stable appearance, role, key relationships/interactions, and any grounded aliases or names heard. Prefer visible, spoken, OCR, or credited evidence over guesses.

fields.name: relationships
fields.description: Each relationship starting FROM this entity, output exactly as `dst_name;dst_type;rel_type;desc` (empty array if none). dst_name / dst_type must copy another emitted entity verbatim. rel_type is `actor_portrays_character` (person -> character only; desc must be `other`) or `has_social_relationship` (same type only). desc must be exactly one of family / friend / colleague / acquaintance / rival / other. Do not add evidence text, labels, colons, extra semicolons, or any value outside the enum in this field. Put relationship evidence in the entity description instead. Emit only clearly evidenced ties, not mere co-presence; emit symmetric social ties once.

fields.name: best_thumbnail
fields.description: Best, clearest, most front-facing frame of the entity. Output a list of 5 numbers `[time_sec, y_min, x_min, y_max, x_max]`: time_sec is seconds from the chunk start; the box is normalized to [0,1000] (top-left, bottom-right) and tightly encloses the entity. Output [] if not clearly visible.

fields.name: second_best_thumbnail
fields.description: A frame as different as possible from best_thumbnail — a different time, ideally a different shot, and a different pose / angle / expression. Output a list of 5 numbers `[time_sec, y_min, x_min, y_max, x_max]`: time_sec is seconds from the chunk start; the box is normalized to [0,1000] (top-left, bottom-right) and tightly encloses the entity. Output [] if not clearly visible.

fields.name: third_best_thumbnail
fields.description: A frame distinct from and far in time from both other thumbnails, so the three span the entity's varied appearances. Output a list of 5 numbers `[time_sec, y_min, x_min, y_max, x_max]`: time_sec is seconds from the chunk start; the box is normalized to [0,1000] (top-left, bottom-right) and tightly encloses the entity. Output [] if not clearly visible.

segment_definitions.description: This is the 4th of 5 parts of the video, and it is 500 seconds long. Segment this part into granular, contiguous shots using visual and audio cues: cuts, major camera-motion changes, event/action beats, dialogue or speaker turns, subject/framing/setting changes, and entity entries/exits. When unsure, prefer shorter coherent shots. Cover this part end to end with no gaps: the FIRST shot must start at 0 and the LAST shot must end at 500 (the full length of this part). Do not omit or trim static or silent spans at the head or tail.

fields.name: description
fields.description: Describe this shot in rich, verbose detail — cover every meaningful visual and non-visual detail: the actions and interactions, objects, setting, on-screen text, and any spoken dialogue quoted verbatim (with the speaker when certain). Refer to each person or character by the EXACT same name you give it in the `entities` field. Write enough that the shot stands on its own and, read in order, the shots reconstruct the whole story.

fields.name: shot_type
fields.description: The type of the shot.

fields.name: camera_motion
fields.description: The camera motion of the shot.

fields.name: camera_angle
fields.description: The camera angle of the shot.

fields.name: entities
fields.description: Each entity evidenced in this shot, output exactly as `name;entity_type;description`. Include only entities visible, audible, or OCR-referenced in this shot: foreground or speaking persons/characters, important addressed or interacted-with people/characters, and entities needed to understand the action, conflict, portrayal, or relationship; also central places, objects, concepts, or things when they drive the shot's meaning. entity_type is one of person / character / place / object / concept / thing, lowercase. Use exactly two semicolons as separators; do not put semicolons inside name, entity_type, or description (use commas or dashes). For the `name` element (person/character): Name, in order of preference: (1) the entity's full name (a real person's real name, or a character's in-story name); (2) a name spoken or shown on screen; (3) a descriptive label built from stable, distinguishing traits (e.g. 'bald older guard', 'red-haired teen') — avoid unstable traits like clothing. Decide from all available context, not this shot alone. The name must be specific enough that two DIFFERENT people never collide on the same name: never a bare generic ('man', 'woman', 'person'). **Use that entity's exact same name in every shot.** For the `description` element: Describe the entity for IDENTIFICATION — so that, from descriptions alone, one can tell which mentions across shots are the SAME person. Write SEVERAL sentences and pack in AS MANY stable identifying properties as you can: any name or alias heard, role, gender, approximate age, ethnicity, hair (color/length/style), facial features, build/height, distinctive marks, and especially their relationship or recurring interaction with other identified entities (e.g. 'the protagonist's mother', 'the man who repeatedly argues with the bald guard'), which strongly anchors identity. Do NOT describe clothing, location, or momentary physical action — they change and do not help identification.

segment_definitions.description: This is the 4th of 5 parts of the video. Emit exactly ONE segment covering this whole part, using chunk-relative times: `start_time=0`, `end_time=500`.

fields.name: title
fields.description: Title of the whole video; best guess from in-chunk evidence (titles / credits / dialogue).

fields.name: description
fields.description: Summarize this chunk using only evidence from the emitted shot segments — cover essential story beats, key people/characters, important relationships/conflicts, locations, objects, and short grounded dialogue/OCR facts; do not introduce unsupported facts. Integrate events, objects, story context, visual detail, and audio/OCR/transcription facts into one account: names, relationships, intentions, conflicts, interaction evidence, and production cues like 'Action!'/'Cut!' when evidenced. Quote only short exact phrases; do not reproduce the transcript or assert an uncertain speaker.

fields.name: work
fields.description: Optional work title inferred from this chunk (for root candidate materialization).

params.summary_prompt: Summarize the video for segmentation context.

metadata.title: Z-Squad's Reckoning
metadata.description: In a dimly lit Z-Squad hideout, Vander sternly lectures Vi about her reckless leadership, emphasizing that 'when people look up to you, you don't get to be selfish.' He tends to her bandaged hand and warns her to lay low after a botched job. Meanwhile, Powder secretly retrieves a glowing blue Hextech gem from a bag and hides it. Vi and Mylo argue about Powder's involvement, with Vi asserting, 'Powder's my problem.' Vander visits Benzo's shop to discuss the situation, where they learn the attackers were not Enforcers but 'someone on our side.' The scene shifts to the streets where Ekko teaches Mylo fighting moves before Enforcers arrive. Inside Benzo's, Enforcer Marcus confronts Vander about a recent attack, demanding to know who the 'ruffians' are. Vander maintains his neutrality, stating, 'We had a deal,' while Ekko secretly listens from a vent above.

gemini_result.title: Arcane - Vander Lectures Vi and Visits Benzo
gemini_result.description: Vander scolds Vi for her reckless actions in Piltover, warning her of the consequences and the threat of the Enforcers. He tends to her wounds and learns they lost the stolen loot, which he considers a good thing as it severs their ties to the crime. Vander then leaves with Claggor to speak with their informant, Benzo. Meanwhile, Powder secretly keeps a glowing blue crystal from the heist and overhears Mylo calling her a jinx, prompting Vi to fiercely defend her. Vander arrives at Benzo's shop, where they discuss the younger generation's dangerous ambitions.

Mylo, a young man with spiky hair, and Claggor, a plump young man with goggles, quickly get up from a worn couch and leave the room. Vi, a young woman with pink hair and a red jacket, remains standing, looking defiantly at Vander.

shots.description: Vander looks down at Vi and firmly says, 'Sit down.' Vi, looking away with a stubborn expression, replies, 'I'm fine.' Vander, his expression hardening and his voice dropping lower, repeats, 'Sit. Down.'

shots.narrative_descriptor: Vander commands Vi to sit down. Vi initially refuses, saying 'I'm fine,' but Vander sternly repeats his order: 'Sit. Down.'

shots.description: Vi sighs, walks over to a large, worn armchair, and sits down heavily, leaning back and crossing her arms. Vander walks over and sits down on a stool across from her, resting his arms on the wooden table between them. The room is lit by a warm, glowing lamp on the table.

character_entities.description: A large, muscular man with a beard, wearing a brown jacket and gauntlets. He acts as a father figure to Vi, Powder, Mylo, and Claggor.
A young woman with pink hair, wearing a red jacket and goggles on her head. She is rebellious and protective of her sister, Powder.

shots.narrative_descriptor: Vi reluctantly sits down in an armchair. Vander sits across from her at a table.

shots.description: Vander leans in and talks to Vi. He says, 'Those kids look up to you.' Vi replies, 'Yeah, I know.' Vander continues, 'You know, but you don't know. When people look up to you, you don't get to be selfish. You say run, they run. You say swim, they dive in. You say light a fire, they show up with oil. But whatever happens, it's on you.' Vi looks down, absorbing his words. Vander adds, 'Just like it's on me what happens to us down here. We make ourselves a problem for Piltover, and they will send the Enforcers.' Vi argues, 'So? Why answer to them? These are our streets. Someone should remind them of that.' Vander looks disappointed and says, 'You're not hearing me. That path... this... it's not gonna solve your problems. Just makes more of them. We clear?' Vi looks away, frustrated.

place_entities.description: The bar and basement area where Vander and the kids live.

shots.narrative_descriptor: Vander asks Vi how she got injured. Vi explains that someone was following them for the stolen goods, but admits that they lost all of the loot.

shots.description: Vander looks relieved. 'Good,' he says. 'Nothing can tie you to what happened up there. You're gonna have to lay low for a bit. Understand?' Vi nods slightly. 'Okay,' she says. 'We're gonna be fine, right?' Vander reassures her, 'I'll take care of it.'

character_entities.description: A large, muscular man with a beard, wearing a brown jacket and gauntlets. He acts as a father figure to Vi, Powder, Mylo, and Claggor.
A young woman with pink hair, wearing a red jacket and goggles on her head. She is rebellious and protective of her sister, Powder.

place_entities.description: The bar and basement area where Vander and the kids live.

shots.narrative_descriptor: Vander expresses relief that the loot is gone, as it means there is no evidence tying them to the crime in Piltover. He tells Vi to lay low and promises to handle the situation.

shots.description: Vander stands up, walks over to a shelf, and grabs a large sack. As he prepares to leave, Vi looks up and says, 'You did put that idiot on his ass though, right?' Vander pauses, a small smile appearing on his face, and he walks out the door.

character_entities.description: A large, muscular man with a beard, wearing a brown jacket and gauntlets. He acts as a father figure to Vi, Powder, Mylo, and Claggor.
A young woman with pink hair, wearing a red jacket and goggles on her head. She is rebellious and protective of her sister, Powder.

place_entities.description: The bar and basement area where Vander and the kids live.

shots.narrative_descriptor: Vander grabs a sack and heads for the door. Vi asks if he beat up the person following them, and Vander smiles as he leaves.

shots.description: Outside the room, Mylo is pressing a makeshift listening device against the door. Suddenly, the door opens, and Vander steps out, hitting Mylo in the head with the door. Mylo stumbles back, rubbing his head. Vander looks at Claggor, who is sitting on the stairs, and says, 'Get up, Claggor. We're going out.' Mylo complains, 'Wait, now? Hey, hey, that's mine! You want to be treated like adults, right? Then you should know better than to come back from a job empty-handed. I'm gonna have a little word with your informant.' Vander and Claggor walk away.

character_entities.description: A large, muscular man with a beard, wearing a brown jacket and gauntlets. He acts as a father figure to Vi, Powder, Mylo, and Claggor.
A young man with spiky hair, wearing a white shirt and suspenders. He is critical of Powder and often complains.
A plump young man wearing goggles. He is quiet and follows Vander's orders.

place_entities.description: The bar and basement area where Vander and the kids live.

shots.narrative_descriptor: Vander opens the door, accidentally hitting Mylo who was eavesdropping. Vander tells Claggor they are leaving and scolds Mylo for returning empty-handed, stating he is going to speak with their informant.

shots.description: Vander and Claggor walk out of the building and down a dark, neon-lit alleyway in the Undercity. Above them, hidden inside a large metal pipe, Powder, a young girl with blue hair, watches them leave.

character_entities.description: A large, muscular man with a beard, wearing a brown jacket and gauntlets. He acts as a father figure to Vi, Powder, Mylo, and Claggor.
A plump young man wearing goggles. He is quiet and follows Vander's orders.
A young girl with blue hair. She is Vi's younger sister and struggles to prove her worth to the group.

place_entities.description: The dark, polluted, and impoverished city beneath Piltover.

shots.narrative_descriptor: Vander and Claggor walk down an alleyway while Powder secretly watches them from a hiding spot inside a pipe.

shots.description: Powder watches Vander and Claggor walk away. She then opens her hand, revealing a glowing blue crystal. She stares at it for a moment, her eyes reflecting the blue light, then closes her hand, climbs out of the pipe, and heads back inside the building.

character_entities.description: A young girl with blue hair. She is Vi's younger sister and struggles to prove her worth to the group.

place_entities.description: The dark, polluted, and impoverished city beneath Piltover.

object_entities.description: A glowing blue orb that Powder secretly kept from the heist in Piltover.

shots.narrative_descriptor: Powder looks at a glowing blue crystal she kept from the heist, then climbs out of her hiding spot and goes back inside.

shots.description: Powder sneaks back inside and stands outside the door to the room where Vi and Mylo are. She listens through the crack in the door. Inside, Mylo is complaining to Vi. 'She's a problem, Milo. I'm really not. Do you remember what was in that bag? Biggest payout we've ever seen, and she just lost it.' Vi defends Powder, 'She made a mistake. Name one time she hasn't. She's young. Don't bullshit me. You were twice the person at half her age. You know what, Milo? You're right. There's a bunch of things Powder just can't do. You don't have to tell me twice.' Powder looks sad as she listens.

character_entities.description: A young girl with blue hair. She is Vi's younger sister and struggles to prove her worth to the group.
A young woman with pink hair, wearing a red jacket and goggles on her head. She is rebellious and protective of her sister, Powder.
A young man with spiky hair, wearing a white shirt and suspenders. He is critical of Powder and often complains.

place_entities.description: The bar and basement area where Vander and the kids live.

shots.narrative_descriptor: Powder eavesdrops at the door as Mylo complains to Vi about Powder losing the loot, calling her a jinx. Vi defends Powder, arguing that she is young and still learning.

shots.description: Vi gets angry. 'Like complain about everything. What? And brag nonstop. Okay, okay, I see where this is going. Pick fights with the group when we need to focus. Fine. And tell strangers on the street that we got a nice haul. I didn't mean to... Powder's my problem. Okay? Your problem is never knowing when to shut up. But I'm gonna help you with that. Ready? You see this look on my face? This will always mean it's time to shut up.' Mylo looks intimidated and stammers, 'But I...' Vi glares at him.

character_entities.description: A young woman with pink hair, wearing a red jacket and goggles on her head. She is rebellious and protective of her sister, Powder.
A young man with spiky hair, wearing a white shirt and suspenders. He is critical of Powder and often complains.

place_entities.description: The bar and basement area where Vander and the kids live.

shots.narrative_descriptor: Vi loses her temper with Mylo, listing his flaws and telling him his main problem is not knowing when to shut up. She threatens him, pointing to her face as a warning sign.

shots.description: Vander and Claggor walk down a busy street in the Undercity and arrive outside a shop with a neon sign that reads 'Benzo's'.

character_entities.description: A large, muscular man with a beard, wearing a brown jacket and gauntlets. He acts as a father figure to Vi, Powder, Mylo, and Claggor.
A plump young man wearing goggles. He is quiet and follows Vander's orders.

place_entities.description: A shop in the Undercity run by Benzo, filled with various mechanical parts and antiques.
The dark, polluted, and impoverished city beneath Piltover.

shots.narrative_descriptor: Vander and Claggor arrive at Benzo's shop in the Undercity.

shots.description: Vander enters the shop, carrying the sack. Benzo, a large man with mutton chops, is sitting at the counter, examining a mechanical turtle with a magnifying glass. Vander says, 'We're closed.' Benzo replies, 'We'll open up. For good. You can take your worthless junk elsewhere.' Vander smiles and says, 'Just as well. Owner's the shittiest businessman I know.' They both laugh.

character_entities.description: A large, muscular man with a beard, wearing a brown jacket and gauntlets. He acts as a father figure to Vi, Powder, Mylo, and Claggor.
A large man with mutton chops who runs a shop in the Undercity. He is a friend and informant to Vander.

place_entities.description: A shop in the Undercity run by Benzo, filled with various mechanical parts and antiques.

shots.narrative_descriptor: Vander enters Benzo's shop. They exchange friendly banter and laugh together.

shots.description: Benzo calls out to the back of the shop, 'Ekko! What's going on with that thing?' Ekko, a young boy with white hair, is working on a large clockwork mechanism. He replies, 'Give me a few seconds. The cat and pinion's still busted.' Benzo says, 'Finish it later. Vander and I need a word.' Ekko says, 'Right. Off you go.' Ekko grabs a box and leaves the room.

character_entities.description: A large man with mutton chops who runs a shop in the Undercity. He is a friend and informant to Vander.
A young boy with white hair who works in Benzo's shop, tinkering with mechanical devices.

place_entities.description: A shop in the Undercity run by Benzo, filled with various mechanical parts and antiques.

shots.narrative_descriptor: Benzo tells Ekko to stop working and leave the room so he and Vander can talk privately. Ekko agrees and leaves.

shots.description: As Ekko walks past, Vander greets him, 'Ekko. Hey, little man.' Ekko replies, 'Hey, Vander.' Benzo's demeanor becomes serious. He asks Vander, 'Why are you mucking about with this?' Vander replies, 'I assume you've heard.'

character_entities.description: A large, muscular man with a beard, wearing a brown jacket and gauntlets. He acts as a father figure to Vi, Powder, Mylo, and Claggor.
A large man with mutton chops who runs a shop in the Undercity. He is a friend and informant to Vander.
A young boy with white hair who works in Benzo's shop, tinkering with mechanical devices.

place_entities.description: A shop in the Undercity run by Benzo, filled with various mechanical parts and antiques.

shots.narrative_descriptor: Vander greets Ekko as he leaves. Benzo then turns serious and asks Vander why he is involved in the current mess.

shots.description: Benzo says, 'Yeah. Me and half the Undercity.' Vander sighs, 'How could they be so stupid?' Benzo replies, 'The younger folk think it's the right thing to do. Yeah. And Vi's one of them. Throws herself at trouble wherever she can find some. They're growing up, Vander. Looking to write their own stories.'

character_entities.description: A large, muscular man with a beard, wearing a brown jacket and gauntlets. He acts as a father figure to Vi, Powder, Mylo, and Claggor.
A large man with mutton chops who runs a shop in the Undercity. He is a friend and informant to Vander.

place_entities.description: The dark, polluted, and impoverished city beneath Piltover.
A shop in the Undercity run by Benzo, filled with various mechanical parts and antiques.

shots.narrative_descriptor: Benzo confirms he has heard about the incident in Piltover. They discuss the younger generation's actions, with Benzo noting that Vi and the others are growing up and trying to make their own mark.

axis_descriptions.entity: Extract every important person/character in the range once each, merging recurrences into one canonical entry. Include foreground people/characters, speakers, addressed people/characters, partially visible but important interactors, and entities needed to understand actions, conflicts, portrayals, or relationships. Do not skip an unknown-name entity if it is distinguishable or narratively important. Skip background extras with no interaction or story function. person = real individual, character = fictional/performed role; if both a real person and their portrayed character are identifiable, emit them separately. Appearance segmentation — entities are extracted segment by segment, so a lead who recurs in every segment can crowd out the people around them. Treat each NEW person/character's first appearance as the start of a new entity segment. When several people appear at once, split that window into shorter appearance intervals and emit EVERY person in it — including secondary, briefly-seen, or interacting-but-not-central people — not only the most prominent one.
axis_descriptions.shot: This is the 5th of 5 parts of the video, and it is 499 seconds long. Segment this part into granular, contiguous shots using visual and audio cues: cuts, major camera-motion changes, event/action beats, dialogue or speaker turns, subject/framing/setting changes, and entity entries/exits. When unsure, prefer shorter coherent shots. Cover this part end to end with no gaps: the FIRST shot must start at 0 and the LAST shot must end at 499 (the full length of this part). Do not omit or trim static or silent spans at the head or tail.
axis_descriptions.video: This is the 5th of 5 parts of the video. Emit exactly ONE segment covering this whole part, using chunk-relative times: `start_time=0`, `end_time=499`.

segment_definitions.description: Extract every important person/character in the range once each, merging recurrences into one canonical entry. Include foreground people/characters, speakers, addressed people/characters, partially visible but important interactors, and entities needed to understand actions, conflicts, portrayals, or relationships. Do not skip an unknown-name entity if it is distinguishable or narratively important. Skip background extras with no interaction or story function. person = real individual, character = fictional/performed role; if both a real person and their portrayed character are identifiable, emit them separately. Appearance segmentation — entities are extracted segment by segment, so a lead who recurs in every segment can crowd out the people around them. Treat each NEW person/character's first appearance as the start of a new entity segment. When several people appear at once, split that window into shorter appearance intervals and emit EVERY person in it — including secondary, briefly-seen, or interacting-but-not-central people — not only the most prominent one.

fields.name: canonical_name
fields.description: Name, in order of preference: (1) the entity's full name (a real person's real name, or a character's in-story name); (2) a name spoken or shown on screen; (3) a descriptive label built from stable, distinguishing traits (e.g. 'bald older guard', 'red-haired teen') — avoid unstable traits like clothing. These entities are later merged across videos by matching name, so the name must be specific enough that two DIFFERENT people never collide on the same name: never a bare generic ('man', 'woman', 'person'), which would wrongly merge unrelated people. One name per entity; the description elaborates further.

fields.name: entity_type
fields.description: Whether this entity is a person or a character.

fields.name: description
fields.description: Identifying description of this entity. Elaborate the entity to support its name and future cross-video merging: stable appearance, role, key relationships/interactions, and any grounded aliases or names heard. Prefer visible, spoken, OCR, or credited evidence over guesses.

fields.name: relationships
fields.description: Each relationship starting FROM this entity, output exactly as `dst_name;dst_type;rel_type;desc` (empty array if none). dst_name / dst_type must copy another emitted entity verbatim. rel_type is `actor_portrays_character` (person -> character only; desc must be `other`) or `has_social_relationship` (same type only). desc must be exactly one of family / friend / colleague / acquaintance / rival / other. Do not add evidence text, labels, colons, extra semicolons, or any value outside the enum in this field. Put relationship evidence in the entity description instead. Emit only clearly evidenced ties, not mere co-presence; emit symmetric social ties once.

fields.name: best_thumbnail
fields.description: Best, clearest, most front-facing frame of the entity. Output a list of 5 numbers `[time_sec, y_min, x_min, y_max, x_max]`: time_sec is seconds from the chunk start; the box is normalized to [0,1000] (top-left, bottom-right) and tightly encloses the entity. Output [] if not clearly visible.

fields.name: second_best_thumbnail
fields.description: A frame as different as possible from best_thumbnail — a different time, ideally a different shot, and a different pose / angle / expression. Output a list of 5 numbers `[time_sec, y_min, x_min, y_max, x_max]`: time_sec is seconds from the chunk start; the box is normalized to [0,1000] (top-left, bottom-right) and tightly encloses the entity. Output [] if not clearly visible.

fields.name: third_best_thumbnail
fields.description: A frame distinct from and far in time from both other thumbnails, so the three span the entity's varied appearances. Output a list of 5 numbers `[time_sec, y_min, x_min, y_max, x_max]`: time_sec is seconds from the chunk start; the box is normalized to [0,1000] (top-left, bottom-right) and tightly encloses the entity. Output [] if not clearly visible.

segment_definitions.description: This is the 5th of 5 parts of the video, and it is 499 seconds long. Segment this part into granular, contiguous shots using visual and audio cues: cuts, major camera-motion changes, event/action beats, dialogue or speaker turns, subject/framing/setting changes, and entity entries/exits. When unsure, prefer shorter coherent shots. Cover this part end to end with no gaps: the FIRST shot must start at 0 and the LAST shot must end at 499 (the full length of this part). Do not omit or trim static or silent spans at the head or tail.

fields.name: description
fields.description: Describe this shot in rich, verbose detail — cover every meaningful visual and non-visual detail: the actions and interactions, objects, setting, on-screen text, and any spoken dialogue quoted verbatim (with the speaker when certain). Refer to each person or character by the EXACT same name you give it in the `entities` field. Write enough that the shot stands on its own and, read in order, the shots reconstruct the whole story.

fields.name: shot_type
fields.description: The type of the shot.

fields.name: camera_motion
fields.description: The camera motion of the shot.

fields.name: camera_angle
fields.description: The camera angle of the shot.

fields.name: entities
fields.description: Each entity evidenced in this shot, output exactly as `name;entity_type;description`. Include only entities visible, audible, or OCR-referenced in this shot: foreground or speaking persons/characters, important addressed or interacted-with people/characters, and entities needed to understand the action, conflict, portrayal, or relationship; also central places, objects, concepts, or things when they drive the shot's meaning. entity_type is one of person / character / place / object / concept / thing, lowercase. Use exactly two semicolons as separators; do not put semicolons inside name, entity_type, or description (use commas or dashes). For the `name` element (person/character): Name, in order of preference: (1) the entity's full name (a real person's real name, or a character's in-story name); (2) a name spoken or shown on screen; (3) a descriptive label built from stable, distinguishing traits (e.g. 'bald older guard', 'red-haired teen') — avoid unstable traits like clothing. Decide from all available context, not this shot alone. The name must be specific enough that two DIFFERENT people never collide on the same name: never a bare generic ('man', 'woman', 'person'). **Use that entity's exact same name in every shot.** For the `description` element: Describe the entity for IDENTIFICATION — so that, from descriptions alone, one can tell which mentions across shots are the SAME person. Write SEVERAL sentences and pack in AS MANY stable identifying properties as you can: any name or alias heard, role, gender, approximate age, ethnicity, hair (color/length/style), facial features, build/height, distinctive marks, and especially their relationship or recurring interaction with other identified entities (e.g. 'the protagonist's mother', 'the man who repeatedly argues with the bald guard'), which strongly anchors identity. Do NOT describe clothing, location, or momentary physical action — they change and do not help identification.

segment_definitions.description: This is the 5th of 5 parts of the video. Emit exactly ONE segment covering this whole part, using chunk-relative times: `start_time=0`, `end_time=499`.

fields.name: title
fields.description: Title of the whole video; best guess from in-chunk evidence (titles / credits / dialogue).

fields.name: description
fields.description: Summarize this chunk using only evidence from the emitted shot segments — cover essential story beats, key people/characters, important relationships/conflicts, locations, objects, and short grounded dialogue/OCR facts; do not introduce unsupported facts. Integrate events, objects, story context, visual detail, and audio/OCR/transcription facts into one account: names, relationships, intentions, conflicts, interaction evidence, and production cues like 'Action!'/'Cut!' when evidenced. Quote only short exact phrases; do not reproduce the transcript or assert an uncertain speaker.

fields.name: work
fields.description: Optional work title inferred from this chunk (for root candidate materialization).

metadata.description: The end credits of the video, showing the names of the cast and crew.

metadata.title: Arcane: Part 5
metadata.description: This segment covers the final part of the video, beginning with a tense confrontation between Councilor Grayson and Vander in a dimly lit room, where Grayson demands action against the Undercity. The scene shifts to young Ekko and Powder in their hideout, where Ekko comforts Powder after a failed heist, giving her a toy and encouraging her. They later sit on a rooftop overlooking Zaun, where Ekko shares stories of their friends and their struggles. Powder discovers glowing blue crystals, which Ekko decides to keep secret. The narrative then moves to a dark laboratory where Silco interrogates a captured Grayson, while a scientist injects a purple substance into a rat, causing it to mutate and attack. The segment concludes with the credits rolling, listing the cast and production team for the League of Legends series.

gemini_result.title: Arcane - Vi and Powder on the Roof / Silco Interrogates Deckard
gemini_result.description: Grayson pressures Vander for a name to appease the Enforcers, but he refuses. Vi comforts a guilty Powder, showing her that everyone makes mistakes by pointing out Claggor and Mylo's past blunders through a spyglass. Powder reveals she kept the blue Hextech crystals. Meanwhile, in an underwater base, Silco interrogates Deckard about the explosion in Piltover. When Deckard reveals that Vander is in trouble with the Enforcers because of the kids, Silco realizes this is an opportunity.
