axis_descriptions.entity: Extract every important person/character in the range once each, merging recurrences into one canonical entry. Include foreground people/characters, speakers, addressed people/characters, partially visible but important interactors, and entities needed to understand actions, conflicts, portrayals, or relationships. Do not skip an unknown-name entity if it is distinguishable or narratively important. Skip background extras with no interaction or story function. person = real individual, character = fictional/performed role; if both a real person and their portrayed character are identifiable, emit them separately. Appearance segmentation — entities are extracted segment by segment, so a lead who recurs in every segment can crowd out the people around them. Treat each NEW person/character's first appearance as the start of a new entity segment. When several people appear at once, split that window into shorter appearance intervals and emit EVERY person in it — including secondary, briefly-seen, or interacting-but-not-central people — not only the most prominent one.
axis_descriptions.shot: This is the 1st of 4 parts of the video, and it is 579 seconds long. Segment this part into granular, contiguous shots using visual and audio cues: cuts, major camera-motion changes, event/action beats, dialogue or speaker turns, subject/framing/setting changes, and entity entries/exits. When unsure, prefer shorter coherent shots. Cover this part end to end with no gaps: the FIRST shot must start at 0 and the LAST shot must end at 579 (the full length of this part). Do not omit or trim static or silent spans at the head or tail.
axis_descriptions.video: This is the 1st of 4 parts of the video. Emit exactly ONE segment covering this whole part, using chunk-relative times: `start_time=0`, `end_time=579`.

segment_definitions.description: Extract every important person/character in the range once each, merging recurrences into one canonical entry. Include foreground people/characters, speakers, addressed people/characters, partially visible but important interactors, and entities needed to understand actions, conflicts, portrayals, or relationships. Do not skip an unknown-name entity if it is distinguishable or narratively important. Skip background extras with no interaction or story function. person = real individual, character = fictional/performed role; if both a real person and their portrayed character are identifiable, emit them separately. Appearance segmentation — entities are extracted segment by segment, so a lead who recurs in every segment can crowd out the people around them. Treat each NEW person/character's first appearance as the start of a new entity segment. When several people appear at once, split that window into shorter appearance intervals and emit EVERY person in it — including secondary, briefly-seen, or interacting-but-not-central people — not only the most prominent one.

fields.name: canonical_name
fields.description: Name, in order of preference: (1) the entity's full name (a real person's real name, or a character's in-story name); (2) a name spoken or shown on screen; (3) a descriptive label built from stable, distinguishing traits (e.g. 'bald older guard', 'red-haired teen') — avoid unstable traits like clothing. These entities are later merged across videos by matching name, so the name must be specific enough that two DIFFERENT people never collide on the same name: never a bare generic ('man', 'woman', 'person'), which would wrongly merge unrelated people. One name per entity; the description elaborates further.

fields.name: entity_type
fields.description: Whether this entity is a person or a character.

fields.name: description
fields.description: Identifying description of this entity. Elaborate the entity to support its name and future cross-video merging: stable appearance, role, key relationships/interactions, and any grounded aliases or names heard. Prefer visible, spoken, OCR, or credited evidence over guesses.

fields.name: relationships
fields.description: Each relationship starting FROM this entity, output exactly as `dst_name;dst_type;rel_type;desc` (empty array if none). dst_name / dst_type must copy another emitted entity verbatim. rel_type is `actor_portrays_character` (person -> character only; desc must be `other`) or `has_social_relationship` (same type only). desc must be exactly one of family / friend / colleague / acquaintance / rival / other. Do not add evidence text, labels, colons, extra semicolons, or any value outside the enum in this field. Put relationship evidence in the entity description instead. Emit only clearly evidenced ties, not mere co-presence; emit symmetric social ties once.

fields.name: best_thumbnail
fields.description: Best, clearest, most front-facing frame of the entity. Output a list of 5 numbers `[time_sec, y_min, x_min, y_max, x_max]`: time_sec is seconds from the chunk start; the box is normalized to [0,1000] (top-left, bottom-right) and tightly encloses the entity. Output [] if not clearly visible.

fields.name: second_best_thumbnail
fields.description: A frame as different as possible from best_thumbnail — a different time, ideally a different shot, and a different pose / angle / expression. Output a list of 5 numbers `[time_sec, y_min, x_min, y_max, x_max]`: time_sec is seconds from the chunk start; the box is normalized to [0,1000] (top-left, bottom-right) and tightly encloses the entity. Output [] if not clearly visible.

fields.name: third_best_thumbnail
fields.description: A frame distinct from and far in time from both other thumbnails, so the three span the entity's varied appearances. Output a list of 5 numbers `[time_sec, y_min, x_min, y_max, x_max]`: time_sec is seconds from the chunk start; the box is normalized to [0,1000] (top-left, bottom-right) and tightly encloses the entity. Output [] if not clearly visible.

segment_definitions.description: This is the 1st of 4 parts of the video, and it is 579 seconds long. Segment this part into granular, contiguous shots using visual and audio cues: cuts, major camera-motion changes, event/action beats, dialogue or speaker turns, subject/framing/setting changes, and entity entries/exits. When unsure, prefer shorter coherent shots. Cover this part end to end with no gaps: the FIRST shot must start at 0 and the LAST shot must end at 579 (the full length of this part). Do not omit or trim static or silent spans at the head or tail.

fields.name: description
fields.description: Describe this shot in rich, verbose detail — cover every meaningful visual and non-visual detail: the actions and interactions, objects, setting, on-screen text, and any spoken dialogue quoted verbatim (with the speaker when certain). Refer to each person or character by the EXACT same name you give it in the `entities` field. Write enough that the shot stands on its own and, read in order, the shots reconstruct the whole story.

fields.name: shot_type
fields.description: The type of the shot.

fields.name: camera_motion
fields.description: The camera motion of the shot.

fields.name: camera_angle
fields.description: The camera angle of the shot.

fields.name: entities
fields.description: Each entity evidenced in this shot, output exactly as `name;entity_type;description`. Include only entities visible, audible, or OCR-referenced in this shot: foreground or speaking persons/characters, important addressed or interacted-with people/characters, and entities needed to understand the action, conflict, portrayal, or relationship; also central places, objects, concepts, or things when they drive the shot's meaning. entity_type is one of person / character / place / object / concept / thing, lowercase. Use exactly two semicolons as separators; do not put semicolons inside name, entity_type, or description (use commas or dashes). For the `name` element (person/character): Name, in order of preference: (1) the entity's full name (a real person's real name, or a character's in-story name); (2) a name spoken or shown on screen; (3) a descriptive label built from stable, distinguishing traits (e.g. 'bald older guard', 'red-haired teen') — avoid unstable traits like clothing. Decide from all available context, not this shot alone. The name must be specific enough that two DIFFERENT people never collide on the same name: never a bare generic ('man', 'woman', 'person'). **Use that entity's exact same name in every shot.** For the `description` element: Describe the entity for IDENTIFICATION — so that, from descriptions alone, one can tell which mentions across shots are the SAME person. Write SEVERAL sentences and pack in AS MANY stable identifying properties as you can: any name or alias heard, role, gender, approximate age, ethnicity, hair (color/length/style), facial features, build/height, distinctive marks, and especially their relationship or recurring interaction with other identified entities (e.g. 'the protagonist's mother', 'the man who repeatedly argues with the bald guard'), which strongly anchors identity. Do NOT describe clothing, location, or momentary physical action — they change and do not help identification.

segment_definitions.description: This is the 1st of 4 parts of the video. Emit exactly ONE segment covering this whole part, using chunk-relative times: `start_time=0`, `end_time=579`.

fields.name: title
fields.description: Title of the whole video; best guess from in-chunk evidence (titles / credits / dialogue).

fields.name: description
fields.description: Summarize this chunk using only evidence from the emitted shot segments — cover essential story beats, key people/characters, important relationships/conflicts, locations, objects, and short grounded dialogue/OCR facts; do not introduce unsupported facts. Integrate events, objects, story context, visual detail, and audio/OCR/transcription facts into one account: names, relationships, intentions, conflicts, interaction evidence, and production cues like 'Action!'/'Cut!' when evidenced. Quote only short exact phrases; do not reproduce the transcript or assert an uncertain speaker.

fields.name: work
fields.description: Optional work title inferred from this chunk (for root candidate materialization).

params.summary_prompt: Summarize the video for segmentation context.

metadata.description: A young woman with dark hair and a determined expression, wearing a white shirt and carrying a wooden crate filled with mechanical devices. She is seen walking down a hallway with Jayce.
A young girl with dark hair, wearing a hooded winter coat and a scarf. She is seen in a snowy landscape, carrying an unconscious man and later looking up in fear.
A small, elderly man with a long white beard and a staff, wearing a hooded robe. He appears in the snowy landscape and uses magic to revive the unconscious man.
A woman with short dark hair, wearing a blue and gold uniform. She is seen in a damaged room, questioning Jayce about restricted items.
A small, elderly man with a long white beard and a staff, wearing a blue uniform. He is seen in a prison cell, talking to Jayce about the dangers of magic.
A young man with dark hair, wearing a white tank top and bandages on his arms. He is seen in a boxing ring, preparing for a fight.
A young man with dark hair, wearing goggles and a brown vest. He is seen in a boxing ring, watching Vi and Ekko.
A young girl with blue hair, wearing a dark shirt and a plaid skirt. She is seen in a boxing ring, holding a gun and looking determined.

The video opens with a black screen, transitioning to a close-up of a circular, metallic device with intricate engravings. The device is illuminated by a green light, and the words 'A NETFLIX SERIES' appear on its surface. The camera zooms in on the device, which features illustrations of two characters, a woman with pink hair and a man with blue hair.

metadata.title: Arcane: The Hextech Incident and Undercity Raid
metadata.description: This segment opens with a flashback to a snowy mountain where a young Jinx (Powder) and Vi are separated from their group. A mysterious figure, Silco, uses magic to save Vi, and Jinx is left behind before being rescued by Silco, who gives her a Hextech gem. The narrative then shifts to the present, where Professor Heimerdinger is interrogating a young Jayce Talis in his destroyed laboratory. Jayce admits to experimenting with Hextech, leading to his arrest and imprisonment. In the Undercity, Vi, Powder, and their friends are preparing for a heist. The segment concludes with the arrival of Enforcers, led by a masked officer, who raid the hideout and arrest the group.

gemini_result.title: Arcane - Explosion and Flashback
gemini_result.description: Jayce and Caitlyn arrive at Jayce's lab, only to find it locked. As Jayce tries to open the door, a Hextech crystal inside destabilizes and explodes, destroying the lab and throwing Jayce back. While dazed, Jayce experiences a flashback to his childhood, where he and his mother were caught in a deadly blizzard. They are saved by a mysterious Hooded Mage who uses a Hextech crystal and arcane magic to teleport them to a safe, flowery meadow. The mage leaves the crystal with Young Jayce. The scene then transitions to the opening credits, followed by the aftermath of the explosion in the present day, where Enforcer Grayson questions Jayce about the illegal items in his ruined lab.

axis_descriptions.entity: Extract every important person/character in the range once each, merging recurrences into one canonical entry. Include foreground people/characters, speakers, addressed people/characters, partially visible but important interactors, and entities needed to understand actions, conflicts, portrayals, or relationships. Do not skip an unknown-name entity if it is distinguishable or narratively important. Skip background extras with no interaction or story function. person = real individual, character = fictional/performed role; if both a real person and their portrayed character are identifiable, emit them separately. Appearance segmentation — entities are extracted segment by segment, so a lead who recurs in every segment can crowd out the people around them. Treat each NEW person/character's first appearance as the start of a new entity segment. When several people appear at once, split that window into shorter appearance intervals and emit EVERY person in it — including secondary, briefly-seen, or interacting-but-not-central people — not only the most prominent one.
axis_descriptions.shot: This is the 2nd of 4 parts of the video, and it is 578 seconds long. Segment this part into granular, contiguous shots using visual and audio cues: cuts, major camera-motion changes, event/action beats, dialogue or speaker turns, subject/framing/setting changes, and entity entries/exits. When unsure, prefer shorter coherent shots. Cover this part end to end with no gaps: the FIRST shot must start at 0 and the LAST shot must end at 578 (the full length of this part). Do not omit or trim static or silent spans at the head or tail.
axis_descriptions.video: This is the 2nd of 4 parts of the video. Emit exactly ONE segment covering this whole part, using chunk-relative times: `start_time=0`, `end_time=578`.

segment_definitions.description: Extract every important person/character in the range once each, merging recurrences into one canonical entry. Include foreground people/characters, speakers, addressed people/characters, partially visible but important interactors, and entities needed to understand actions, conflicts, portrayals, or relationships. Do not skip an unknown-name entity if it is distinguishable or narratively important. Skip background extras with no interaction or story function. person = real individual, character = fictional/performed role; if both a real person and their portrayed character are identifiable, emit them separately. Appearance segmentation — entities are extracted segment by segment, so a lead who recurs in every segment can crowd out the people around them. Treat each NEW person/character's first appearance as the start of a new entity segment. When several people appear at once, split that window into shorter appearance intervals and emit EVERY person in it — including secondary, briefly-seen, or interacting-but-not-central people — not only the most prominent one.

fields.name: canonical_name
fields.description: Name, in order of preference: (1) the entity's full name (a real person's real name, or a character's in-story name); (2) a name spoken or shown on screen; (3) a descriptive label built from stable, distinguishing traits (e.g. 'bald older guard', 'red-haired teen') — avoid unstable traits like clothing. These entities are later merged across videos by matching name, so the name must be specific enough that two DIFFERENT people never collide on the same name: never a bare generic ('man', 'woman', 'person'), which would wrongly merge unrelated people. One name per entity; the description elaborates further.

fields.name: entity_type
fields.description: Whether this entity is a person or a character.

fields.name: description
fields.description: Identifying description of this entity. Elaborate the entity to support its name and future cross-video merging: stable appearance, role, key relationships/interactions, and any grounded aliases or names heard. Prefer visible, spoken, OCR, or credited evidence over guesses.

fields.name: relationships
fields.description: Each relationship starting FROM this entity, output exactly as `dst_name;dst_type;rel_type;desc` (empty array if none). dst_name / dst_type must copy another emitted entity verbatim. rel_type is `actor_portrays_character` (person -> character only; desc must be `other`) or `has_social_relationship` (same type only). desc must be exactly one of family / friend / colleague / acquaintance / rival / other. Do not add evidence text, labels, colons, extra semicolons, or any value outside the enum in this field. Put relationship evidence in the entity description instead. Emit only clearly evidenced ties, not mere co-presence; emit symmetric social ties once.

fields.name: best_thumbnail
fields.description: Best, clearest, most front-facing frame of the entity. Output a list of 5 numbers `[time_sec, y_min, x_min, y_max, x_max]`: time_sec is seconds from the chunk start; the box is normalized to [0,1000] (top-left, bottom-right) and tightly encloses the entity. Output [] if not clearly visible.

fields.name: second_best_thumbnail
fields.description: A frame as different as possible from best_thumbnail — a different time, ideally a different shot, and a different pose / angle / expression. Output a list of 5 numbers `[time_sec, y_min, x_min, y_max, x_max]`: time_sec is seconds from the chunk start; the box is normalized to [0,1000] (top-left, bottom-right) and tightly encloses the entity. Output [] if not clearly visible.

fields.name: third_best_thumbnail
fields.description: A frame distinct from and far in time from both other thumbnails, so the three span the entity's varied appearances. Output a list of 5 numbers `[time_sec, y_min, x_min, y_max, x_max]`: time_sec is seconds from the chunk start; the box is normalized to [0,1000] (top-left, bottom-right) and tightly encloses the entity. Output [] if not clearly visible.

segment_definitions.description: This is the 2nd of 4 parts of the video, and it is 578 seconds long. Segment this part into granular, contiguous shots using visual and audio cues: cuts, major camera-motion changes, event/action beats, dialogue or speaker turns, subject/framing/setting changes, and entity entries/exits. When unsure, prefer shorter coherent shots. Cover this part end to end with no gaps: the FIRST shot must start at 0 and the LAST shot must end at 578 (the full length of this part). Do not omit or trim static or silent spans at the head or tail.

fields.name: description
fields.description: Describe this shot in rich, verbose detail — cover every meaningful visual and non-visual detail: the actions and interactions, objects, setting, on-screen text, and any spoken dialogue quoted verbatim (with the speaker when certain). Refer to each person or character by the EXACT same name you give it in the `entities` field. Write enough that the shot stands on its own and, read in order, the shots reconstruct the whole story.

fields.name: shot_type
fields.description: The type of the shot.

fields.name: camera_motion
fields.description: The camera motion of the shot.

fields.name: camera_angle
fields.description: The camera angle of the shot.

fields.name: entities
fields.description: Each entity evidenced in this shot, output exactly as `name;entity_type;description`. Include only entities visible, audible, or OCR-referenced in this shot: foreground or speaking persons/characters, important addressed or interacted-with people/characters, and entities needed to understand the action, conflict, portrayal, or relationship; also central places, objects, concepts, or things when they drive the shot's meaning. entity_type is one of person / character / place / object / concept / thing, lowercase. Use exactly two semicolons as separators; do not put semicolons inside name, entity_type, or description (use commas or dashes). For the `name` element (person/character): Name, in order of preference: (1) the entity's full name (a real person's real name, or a character's in-story name); (2) a name spoken or shown on screen; (3) a descriptive label built from stable, distinguishing traits (e.g. 'bald older guard', 'red-haired teen') — avoid unstable traits like clothing. Decide from all available context, not this shot alone. The name must be specific enough that two DIFFERENT people never collide on the same name: never a bare generic ('man', 'woman', 'person'). **Use that entity's exact same name in every shot.** For the `description` element: Describe the entity for IDENTIFICATION — so that, from descriptions alone, one can tell which mentions across shots are the SAME person. Write SEVERAL sentences and pack in AS MANY stable identifying properties as you can: any name or alias heard, role, gender, approximate age, ethnicity, hair (color/length/style), facial features, build/height, distinctive marks, and especially their relationship or recurring interaction with other identified entities (e.g. 'the protagonist's mother', 'the man who repeatedly argues with the bald guard'), which strongly anchors identity. Do NOT describe clothing, location, or momentary physical action — they change and do not help identification.

segment_definitions.description: This is the 2nd of 4 parts of the video. Emit exactly ONE segment covering this whole part, using chunk-relative times: `start_time=0`, `end_time=578`.

fields.name: title
fields.description: Title of the whole video; best guess from in-chunk evidence (titles / credits / dialogue).

fields.name: description
fields.description: Summarize this chunk using only evidence from the emitted shot segments — cover essential story beats, key people/characters, important relationships/conflicts, locations, objects, and short grounded dialogue/OCR facts; do not introduce unsupported facts. Integrate events, objects, story context, visual detail, and audio/OCR/transcription facts into one account: names, relationships, intentions, conflicts, interaction evidence, and production cues like 'Action!'/'Cut!' when evidenced. Quote only short exact phrases; do not reproduce the transcript or assert an uncertain speaker.

fields.name: work
fields.description: Optional work title inferred from this chunk (for root candidate materialization).

params.summary_prompt: Summarize the video for segmentation context.

metadata.title: Arcane - Part 2
metadata.description: This segment depicts the escalating conflict between Piltover and Zaun. It begins with a raid on a Zaunite hideout where Vi, Powder, and others are captured by Enforcers, leading to a chaotic escape. The narrative then shifts to the political sphere in Piltover, where Councilor Mel Medarda discusses a trial with a merchant, identifying the accused as Jayce Talis. In Zaun, Vander attempts to maintain peace, but Vi challenges his leadership, calling him 'weak' and inciting the group to fight back. The central event is Jayce's trial before the Council of Piltover, where he is accused of illegal experimentation. Despite his apology and his mother's plea, the Council, led by Heimerdinger, debates his fate. Heimerdinger warns of the dangers of magic, referencing a past catastrophe. Ultimately, Jayce is expelled from the Academy and remanded to his parents' care. The segment concludes with Jayce returning to the Academy to find it destroyed and overgrown, with a trail of purple blood leading into the darkness.

gemini_result.title: Arcane - The Explosion and The Trial
gemini_result.description: Powder uses a Hextech crystal to create an explosion, allowing her, Vi, Mylo, and Claggor to escape from the Enforcers. Meanwhile, in Piltover, Mel Medarda discusses Jayce Talis's upcoming trial. In the underground, Sevika confronts Vander about his leadership and his deal with the Enforcers. Finally, Jayce Talis stands trial before the Council in Piltover.

Skip background extras with no interaction or story function. person = real individual, character = fictional/performed role; if both a real person and their portrayed character are identifiable, emit them separately. Treat each NEW person/character's first appearance as the start of a new entity segment. fields.name: canonical_name
fields.description: Name, in order of preference: (1) the entity's full name (a real person's real name, or a character's in-story name); (2) a name spoken or shown on screen; (3) a descriptive label built from stable, distinguishing traits (e.g. 'bald older guard', 'red-haired teen') — avoid unstable traits like clothing. These entities are later merged across videos by matching name, so the name must be specific enough that two DIFFERENT people never collide on the same name: never a bare generic ('man', 'woman', 'person'), which would wrongly merge unrelated people. One name per entity; the description elaborates further.

fields.name: entity_type

fields.name: description
fields.description: Identifying description of this entity. Elaborate the entity to support its name and future cross-video merging: stable appearance, role, key relationships/interactions, and any grounded aliases or names heard. Prefer visible, spoken, OCR, or credited evidence over guesses.

fields.name: relationships
fields.description: Each relationship starting FROM this entity, output exactly as `dst_name;dst_type;rel_type;desc` (empty array if none). dst_name / dst_type must copy another emitted entity verbatim. rel_type is `actor_portrays_character` (person -> character only; desc must be `other`) or `has_social_relationship` (same type only). desc must be exactly one of family / friend / colleague / acquaintance / rival / other. Do not add evidence text, labels, colons, extra semicolons, or any value outside the enum in this field. Put relationship evidence in the entity description instead. Emit only clearly evidenced ties, not mere co-presence; emit symmetric social ties once.

metadata.title: Arcane: The Search for the Children
metadata.description: This segment follows multiple intersecting storylines in a steampunk fantasy setting. It opens with a confrontation between a masked officer and a pale, scarred man in a vine-choked room. A flashback reveals a young man, Jace, grieving over a wrist device while a woman urges him to abandon his dangerous magical research. Jace later consults a small, furry creature about a book containing complex diagrams. The narrative shifts to a dark, bioluminescent cavern where the scarred man, Silas, tempts a young woman with a vial of purple liquid, claiming 'real power' comes to those who will do anything. After she smashes the vial, Silas is consumed by a purple energy. The story returns to the present, showing Jace trapped behind a gate, separated from a girl named Kate in the rain. A council meeting follows, where a blonde woman demands the search for four missing children be intensified. The final act takes place in a bar called 'The Last Drop,' where the masked officer, Marcus, interrogates the bartender, who reveals knowledge of Marcus's past. The segment concludes with the bartender rescuing the four children from a hidden room as the officer's men search the premises.

gemini_result.title: Arcane - Episode 2 Excerpt
gemini_result.description: The video shows several interconnected scenes. Silco meets with Enforcer Marcus in a dilapidated room, revealing he knows about the four children Marcus is looking for. Meanwhile, Jayce is despondent in his room after his research is confiscated. Cassandra Kiramman visits him, urging him to let go of his magic research, but Jayce refuses. Jayce then visits Heimerdinger's lab, trying to convince him of the crystals' potential, but Heimerdinger dismisses it as too dangerous. In an underwater base, Silco manipulates a young man named Deckard into drinking a dangerous purple substance called Shimmer, which violently transforms him into a monster. Finally, Jayce stands outside the Kiramman estate gates in the rain, where a young Caitlyn tells him they can no longer be friends because he is considered a 'misfit'. Cassandra calls Caitlyn inside, leaving Jayce alone in the rain.

axis_descriptions.entity: Extract every important person/character in the range once each, merging recurrences into one canonical entry. Include foreground people/characters, speakers, addressed people/characters, partially visible but important interactors, and entities needed to understand actions, conflicts, portrayals, or relationships. Do not skip an unknown-name entity if it is distinguishable or narratively important. Skip background extras with no interaction or story function. person = real individual, character = fictional/performed role; if both a real person and their portrayed character are identifiable, emit them separately. Appearance segmentation — entities are extracted segment by segment, so a lead who recurs in every segment can crowd out the people around them. Treat each NEW person/character's first appearance as the start of a new entity segment. When several people appear at once, split that window into shorter appearance intervals and emit EVERY person in it — including secondary, briefly-seen, or interacting-but-not-central people — not only the most prominent one.
axis_descriptions.shot: This is the 4th of 4 parts of the video, and it is 578 seconds long. Segment this part into granular, contiguous shots using visual and audio cues: cuts, major camera-motion changes, event/action beats, dialogue or speaker turns, subject/framing/setting changes, and entity entries/exits. When unsure, prefer shorter coherent shots. Cover this part end to end with no gaps: the FIRST shot must start at 0 and the LAST shot must end at 578 (the full length of this part). Do not omit or trim static or silent spans at the head or tail.
axis_descriptions.video: This is the 4th of 4 parts of the video. Emit exactly ONE segment covering this whole part, using chunk-relative times: `start_time=0`, `end_time=578`.

segment_definitions.description: Extract every important person/character in the range once each, merging recurrences into one canonical entry. Include foreground people/characters, speakers, addressed people/characters, partially visible but important interactors, and entities needed to understand actions, conflicts, portrayals, or relationships. Do not skip an unknown-name entity if it is distinguishable or narratively important. Skip background extras with no interaction or story function. person = real individual, character = fictional/performed role; if both a real person and their portrayed character are identifiable, emit them separately. Appearance segmentation — entities are extracted segment by segment, so a lead who recurs in every segment can crowd out the people around them. Treat each NEW person/character's first appearance as the start of a new entity segment. When several people appear at once, split that window into shorter appearance intervals and emit EVERY person in it — including secondary, briefly-seen, or interacting-but-not-central people — not only the most prominent one.

fields.name: canonical_name
fields.description: Name, in order of preference: (1) the entity's full name (a real person's real name, or a character's in-story name); (2) a name spoken or shown on screen; (3) a descriptive label built from stable, distinguishing traits (e.g. 'bald older guard', 'red-haired teen') — avoid unstable traits like clothing. These entities are later merged across videos by matching name, so the name must be specific enough that two DIFFERENT people never collide on the same name: never a bare generic ('man', 'woman', 'person'), which would wrongly merge unrelated people. One name per entity; the description elaborates further.

fields.name: entity_type
fields.description: Whether this entity is a person or a character.

fields.name: description
fields.description: Identifying description of this entity. Elaborate the entity to support its name and future cross-video merging: stable appearance, role, key relationships/interactions, and any grounded aliases or names heard. Prefer visible, spoken, OCR, or credited evidence over guesses.

fields.name: relationships
fields.description: Each relationship starting FROM this entity, output exactly as `dst_name;dst_type;rel_type;desc` (empty array if none). dst_name / dst_type must copy another emitted entity verbatim. rel_type is `actor_portrays_character` (person -> character only; desc must be `other`) or `has_social_relationship` (same type only). desc must be exactly one of family / friend / colleague / acquaintance / rival / other. Do not add evidence text, labels, colons, extra semicolons, or any value outside the enum in this field. Put relationship evidence in the entity description instead. Emit only clearly evidenced ties, not mere co-presence; emit symmetric social ties once.

fields.name: best_thumbnail
fields.description: Best, clearest, most front-facing frame of the entity. Output a list of 5 numbers `[time_sec, y_min, x_min, y_max, x_max]`: time_sec is seconds from the chunk start; the box is normalized to [0,1000] (top-left, bottom-right) and tightly encloses the entity. Output [] if not clearly visible.

fields.name: second_best_thumbnail
fields.description: A frame as different as possible from best_thumbnail — a different time, ideally a different shot, and a different pose / angle / expression. Output a list of 5 numbers `[time_sec, y_min, x_min, y_max, x_max]`: time_sec is seconds from the chunk start; the box is normalized to [0,1000] (top-left, bottom-right) and tightly encloses the entity. Output [] if not clearly visible.

fields.name: third_best_thumbnail
fields.description: A frame distinct from and far in time from both other thumbnails, so the three span the entity's varied appearances. Output a list of 5 numbers `[time_sec, y_min, x_min, y_max, x_max]`: time_sec is seconds from the chunk start; the box is normalized to [0,1000] (top-left, bottom-right) and tightly encloses the entity. Output [] if not clearly visible.

segment_definitions.description: This is the 4th of 4 parts of the video, and it is 578 seconds long. Segment this part into granular, contiguous shots using visual and audio cues: cuts, major camera-motion changes, event/action beats, dialogue or speaker turns, subject/framing/setting changes, and entity entries/exits. When unsure, prefer shorter coherent shots. Cover this part end to end with no gaps: the FIRST shot must start at 0 and the LAST shot must end at 578 (the full length of this part). Do not omit or trim static or silent spans at the head or tail.

fields.name: description
fields.description: Describe this shot in rich, verbose detail — cover every meaningful visual and non-visual detail: the actions and interactions, objects, setting, on-screen text, and any spoken dialogue quoted verbatim (with the speaker when certain). Refer to each person or character by the EXACT same name you give it in the `entities` field. Write enough that the shot stands on its own and, read in order, the shots reconstruct the whole story.

fields.name: shot_type
fields.description: The type of the shot.

fields.name: camera_motion
fields.description: The camera motion of the shot.

fields.name: camera_angle
fields.description: The camera angle of the shot.

fields.name: entities
fields.description: Each entity evidenced in this shot, output exactly as `name;entity_type;description`. Include only entities visible, audible, or OCR-referenced in this shot: foreground or speaking persons/characters, important addressed or interacted-with people/characters, and entities needed to understand the action, conflict, portrayal, or relationship; also central places, objects, concepts, or things when they drive the shot's meaning. entity_type is one of person / character / place / object / concept / thing, lowercase. Use exactly two semicolons as separators; do not put semicolons inside name, entity_type, or description (use commas or dashes). For the `name` element (person/character): Name, in order of preference: (1) the entity's full name (a real person's real name, or a character's in-story name); (2) a name spoken or shown on screen; (3) a descriptive label built from stable, distinguishing traits (e.g. 'bald older guard', 'red-haired teen') — avoid unstable traits like clothing. Decide from all available context, not this shot alone. The name must be specific enough that two DIFFERENT people never collide on the same name: never a bare generic ('man', 'woman', 'person'). **Use that entity's exact same name in every shot.** For the `description` element: Describe the entity for IDENTIFICATION — so that, from descriptions alone, one can tell which mentions across shots are the SAME person. Write SEVERAL sentences and pack in AS MANY stable identifying properties as you can: any name or alias heard, role, gender, approximate age, ethnicity, hair (color/length/style), facial features, build/height, distinctive marks, and especially their relationship or recurring interaction with other identified entities (e.g. 'the protagonist's mother', 'the man who repeatedly argues with the bald guard'), which strongly anchors identity. Do NOT describe clothing, location, or momentary physical action — they change and do not help identification.

segment_definitions.description: This is the 4th of 4 parts of the video. Emit exactly ONE segment covering this whole part, using chunk-relative times: `start_time=0`, `end_time=578`.

fields.name: title
fields.description: Title of the whole video; best guess from in-chunk evidence (titles / credits / dialogue).

fields.name: description
fields.description: Summarize this chunk using only evidence from the emitted shot segments — cover essential story beats, key people/characters, important relationships/conflicts, locations, objects, and short grounded dialogue/OCR facts; do not introduce unsupported facts. Integrate events, objects, story context, visual detail, and audio/OCR/transcription facts into one account: names, relationships, intentions, conflicts, interaction evidence, and production cues like 'Action!'/'Cut!' when evidenced. Quote only short exact phrases; do not reproduce the transcript or assert an uncertain speaker.

fields.name: work
fields.description: Optional work title inferred from this chunk (for root candidate materialization).

metadata.title: Arcane - Episode 1 Part 4
metadata.description: This segment covers the final part of the video, beginning with Vi confronting Vander on a bridge about fighting back against the Enforcers. Vander warns her that 'nobody wins in war' and reveals that his past actions led to the death of her parents. The scene shifts to a flashback where a young Jayce is approached by Viktor in a ruined building; Viktor offers to help Jayce with his Hextech research, giving him a wrist device. Back in the present, Vi comforts a distraught Powder, who is playing with a toy rabbit, telling her that 'what makes you different makes you strong.' The narrative then cuts to a confrontation between Councilor Heimerdinger and Councilor Mel Medarda regarding the dangers of Hextech. The segment concludes with a montage of the aftermath of the tragedy: Powder is alone in a bar with Vander, who gives her a drink, while Enforcers break down the door. Vi is shown sitting alone in the dark, her eyes filled with determination as the credits roll.

gemini_result.title: Arcane - Vi and Vander / Jayce and Viktor
gemini_result.description: Vi argues with Vander about fighting back against the enforcers. Vander takes her to the bridge to explain the cost of war, revealing that her parents died the last time they fought. Meanwhile, Jayce contemplates suicide in his ruined apartment but is interrupted by Viktor, who believes in his Hextech research and returns his crystal, offering to help him.

axis_descriptions.entity: Extract every important person/character in the range once each, merging recurrences into one canonical entry. Include foreground people/characters, speakers, addressed people/characters, partially visible but important interactors, and entities needed to understand actions, conflicts, portrayals, or relationships. Do not skip an unknown-name entity if it is distinguishable or narratively important. Skip background extras with no interaction or story function. person = real individual, character = fictional/performed role; if both a real person and their portrayed character are identifiable, emit them separately. Appearance segmentation — entities are extracted segment by segment, so a lead who recurs in every segment can crowd out the people around them. Treat each NEW person/character's first appearance as the start of a new entity segment. When several people appear at once, split that window into shorter appearance intervals and emit EVERY person in it — including secondary, briefly-seen, or interacting-but-not-central people — not only the most prominent one.
axis_descriptions.shot: This is the 1st of 7 parts of the video, and it is 528 seconds long. Segment this part into granular, contiguous shots using visual and audio cues: cuts, major camera-motion changes, event/action beats, dialogue or speaker turns, subject/framing/setting changes, and entity entries/exits. When unsure, prefer shorter coherent shots. Cover this part end to end with no gaps: the FIRST shot must start at 0 and the LAST shot must end at 528 (the full length of this part). Do not omit or trim static or silent spans at the head or tail.
axis_descriptions.video: This is the 1st of 7 parts of the video. Emit exactly ONE segment covering this whole part, using chunk-relative times: `start_time=0`, `end_time=528`.

segment_definitions.description: Extract every important person/character in the range once each, merging recurrences into one canonical entry. Include foreground people/characters, speakers, addressed people/characters, partially visible but important interactors, and entities needed to understand actions, conflicts, portrayals, or relationships. Do not skip an unknown-name entity if it is distinguishable or narratively important. Skip background extras with no interaction or story function. person = real individual, character = fictional/performed role; if both a real person and their portrayed character are identifiable, emit them separately. Appearance segmentation — entities are extracted segment by segment, so a lead who recurs in every segment can crowd out the people around them. Treat each NEW person/character's first appearance as the start of a new entity segment. When several people appear at once, split that window into shorter appearance intervals and emit EVERY person in it — including secondary, briefly-seen, or interacting-but-not-central people — not only the most prominent one.

fields.name: canonical_name
fields.description: Name, in order of preference: (1) the entity's full name (a real person's real name, or a character's in-story name); (2) a name spoken or shown on screen; (3) a descriptive label built from stable, distinguishing traits (e.g. 'bald older guard', 'red-haired teen') — avoid unstable traits like clothing. These entities are later merged across videos by matching name, so the name must be specific enough that two DIFFERENT people never collide on the same name: never a bare generic ('man', 'woman', 'person'), which would wrongly merge unrelated people. One name per entity; the description elaborates further.

fields.name: entity_type
fields.description: Whether this entity is a person or a character.

fields.name: description
fields.description: Identifying description of this entity. Elaborate the entity to support its name and future cross-video merging: stable appearance, role, key relationships/interactions, and any grounded aliases or names heard. Prefer visible, spoken, OCR, or credited evidence over guesses.

fields.name: relationships
fields.description: Each relationship starting FROM this entity, output exactly as `dst_name;dst_type;rel_type;desc` (empty array if none). dst_name / dst_type must copy another emitted entity verbatim. rel_type is `actor_portrays_character` (person -> character only; desc must be `other`) or `has_social_relationship` (same type only). desc must be exactly one of family / friend / colleague / acquaintance / rival / other. Do not add evidence text, labels, colons, extra semicolons, or any value outside the enum in this field. Put relationship evidence in the entity description instead. Emit only clearly evidenced ties, not mere co-presence; emit symmetric social ties once.

fields.name: best_thumbnail
fields.description: Best, clearest, most front-facing frame of the entity. Output a list of 5 numbers `[time_sec, y_min, x_min, y_max, x_max]`: time_sec is seconds from the chunk start; the box is normalized to [0,1000] (top-left, bottom-right) and tightly encloses the entity. Output [] if not clearly visible.

fields.name: second_best_thumbnail
fields.description: A frame as different as possible from best_thumbnail — a different time, ideally a different shot, and a different pose / angle / expression. Output a list of 5 numbers `[time_sec, y_min, x_min, y_max, x_max]`: time_sec is seconds from the chunk start; the box is normalized to [0,1000] (top-left, bottom-right) and tightly encloses the entity. Output [] if not clearly visible.

fields.name: third_best_thumbnail
fields.description: A frame distinct from and far in time from both other thumbnails, so the three span the entity's varied appearances. Output a list of 5 numbers `[time_sec, y_min, x_min, y_max, x_max]`: time_sec is seconds from the chunk start; the box is normalized to [0,1000] (top-left, bottom-right) and tightly encloses the entity. Output [] if not clearly visible.

segment_definitions.description: This is the 1st of 7 parts of the video, and it is 528 seconds long. Segment this part into granular, contiguous shots using visual and audio cues: cuts, major camera-motion changes, event/action beats, dialogue or speaker turns, subject/framing/setting changes, and entity entries/exits. When unsure, prefer shorter coherent shots. Cover this part end to end with no gaps: the FIRST shot must start at 0 and the LAST shot must end at 528 (the full length of this part). Do not omit or trim static or silent spans at the head or tail.

fields.name: description
fields.description: Describe this shot in rich, verbose detail — cover every meaningful visual and non-visual detail: the actions and interactions, objects, setting, on-screen text, and any spoken dialogue quoted verbatim (with the speaker when certain). Refer to each person or character by the EXACT same name you give it in the `entities` field. Write enough that the shot stands on its own and, read in order, the shots reconstruct the whole story.

fields.name: shot_type
fields.description: The type of the shot.

fields.name: camera_motion
fields.description: The camera motion of the shot.

fields.name: camera_angle
fields.description: The camera angle of the shot.

fields.name: entities
fields.description: Each entity evidenced in this shot, output exactly as `name;entity_type;description`. Include only entities visible, audible, or OCR-referenced in this shot: foreground or speaking persons/characters, important addressed or interacted-with people/characters, and entities needed to understand the action, conflict, portrayal, or relationship; also central places, objects, concepts, or things when they drive the shot's meaning. entity_type is one of person / character / place / object / concept / thing, lowercase. Use exactly two semicolons as separators; do not put semicolons inside name, entity_type, or description (use commas or dashes). For the `name` element (person/character): Name, in order of preference: (1) the entity's full name (a real person's real name, or a character's in-story name); (2) a name spoken or shown on screen; (3) a descriptive label built from stable, distinguishing traits (e.g. 'bald older guard', 'red-haired teen') — avoid unstable traits like clothing. Decide from all available context, not this shot alone. The name must be specific enough that two DIFFERENT people never collide on the same name: never a bare generic ('man', 'woman', 'person'). **Use that entity's exact same name in every shot.** For the `description` element: Describe the entity for IDENTIFICATION — so that, from descriptions alone, one can tell which mentions across shots are the SAME person. Write SEVERAL sentences and pack in AS MANY stable identifying properties as you can: any name or alias heard, role, gender, approximate age, ethnicity, hair (color/length/style), facial features, build/height, distinctive marks, and especially their relationship or recurring interaction with other identified entities (e.g. 'the protagonist's mother', 'the man who repeatedly argues with the bald guard'), which strongly anchors identity. Do NOT describe clothing, location, or momentary physical action — they change and do not help identification.

segment_definitions.description: This is the 1st of 7 parts of the video. Emit exactly ONE segment covering this whole part, using chunk-relative times: `start_time=0`, `end_time=528`.

fields.name: title
fields.description: Title of the whole video; best guess from in-chunk evidence (titles / credits / dialogue).

fields.name: description
fields.description: Summarize this chunk using only evidence from the emitted shot segments — cover essential story beats, key people/characters, important relationships/conflicts, locations, objects, and short grounded dialogue/OCR facts; do not introduce unsupported facts. Integrate events, objects, story context, visual detail, and audio/OCR/transcription facts into one account: names, relationships, intentions, conflicts, interaction evidence, and production cues like 'Action!'/'Cut!' when evidenced. Quote only short exact phrases; do not reproduce the transcript or assert an uncertain speaker.

fields.name: work
fields.description: Optional work title inferred from this chunk (for root candidate materialization).

params.summary_prompt: Summarize the video for segmentation context.

metadata.title: Game of Thrones - The Night Lands
metadata.description: This segment follows a group of men, including a leader in a black fur cloak, as they ride horses through a snowy landscape and a dark tunnel, emerging from the base of a massive ice wall. They venture into a snowy forest where one man discovers a circle of dead bodies arranged on the ground. A young girl with pale skin and blue eyes appears, causing the man to flee in terror. The group discusses the danger, with one man suggesting they return, while the leader insists on continuing. The segment ends with the man who fled being chased and killed by a wight, followed by the show's title sequence featuring a map of Westeros and the title 'Game of Thrones'.

gemini_result.title: Game of Thrones - Prologue
gemini_result.description: Three rangers of the Night's Watch ride out from the Wall into the Haunted Forest. One of them, Will, discovers a gruesome scene of dismembered wildling bodies arranged in a pattern. He reports back to his companions, but their leader, Waymar Royce, insists on investigating further. When they return to the spot, the bodies have vanished.

axis_descriptions.entity: Extract every important person/character in the range once each, merging recurrences into one canonical entry. Include foreground people/characters, speakers, addressed people/characters, partially visible but important interactors, and entities needed to understand actions, conflicts, portrayals, or relationships. Do not skip an unknown-name entity if it is distinguishable or narratively important. Skip background extras with no interaction or story function. person = real individual, character = fictional/performed role; if both a real person and their portrayed character are identifiable, emit them separately. Appearance segmentation — entities are extracted segment by segment, so a lead who recurs in every segment can crowd out the people around them. Treat each NEW person/character's first appearance as the start of a new entity segment. When several people appear at once, split that window into shorter appearance intervals and emit EVERY person in it — including secondary, briefly-seen, or interacting-but-not-central people — not only the most prominent one.

fields.name: canonical_name
fields.description: Name, in order of preference: (1) the entity's full name (a real person's real name, or a character's in-story name); (2) a name spoken or shown on screen; (3) a descriptive label built from stable, distinguishing traits (e.g. 'bald older guard', 'red-haired teen') — avoid unstable traits like clothing. These entities are later merged across videos by matching name, so the name must be specific enough that two DIFFERENT people never collide on the same name: never a bare generic ('man', 'woman', 'person'), which would wrongly merge unrelated people. One name per entity; the description elaborates further.

fields.name: entity_type
fields.description: Whether this entity is a person or a character.

fields.name: description
fields.description: Identifying description of this entity. Elaborate the entity to support its name and future cross-video merging: stable appearance, role, key relationships/interactions, and any grounded aliases or names heard. Prefer visible, spoken, OCR, or credited evidence over guesses.

fields.name: relationships
fields.description: Each relationship starting FROM this entity, output exactly as `dst_name;dst_type;rel_type;desc` (empty array if none). dst_name / dst_type must copy another emitted entity verbatim. rel_type is `actor_portrays_character` (person -> character only; desc must be `other`) or `has_social_relationship` (same type only). desc must be exactly one of family / friend / colleague / acquaintance / rival / other. Do not add evidence text, labels, colons, extra semicolons, or any value outside the enum in this field. Put relationship evidence in the entity description instead. Emit only clearly evidenced ties, not mere co-presence; emit symmetric social ties once.

Use exactly two semicolons as separators; do not put semicolons inside name, entity_type, or description (use commas or dashes). For the `name` element (person/character): Name, in order of preference: (1) the entity's full name (a real person's real name, or a character's in-story name); (2) a name spoken or shown on screen; (3) a descriptive label built from stable, distinguishing traits (e.g. 'bald older guard', 'red-haired teen') — avoid unstable traits like clothing. Decide from all available context, not this shot alone. The name must be specific enough that two DIFFERENT people never collide on the same name: never a bare generic ('man', 'woman', 'person'). **Use that entity's exact same name in every shot.** For the `description` element: Describe the entity for IDENTIFICATION — so that, from descriptions alone, one can tell which mentions across shots are the SAME person. Write SEVERAL sentences and pack in AS MANY stable identifying properties as you can: any name or alias heard, role, gender, approximate age, ethnicity, hair (color/length/style), facial features, build/height, distinctive marks, and especially their relationship or recurring interaction with other identified entities (e.g. 'the protagonist's mother', 'the man who repeatedly argues with the bald guard'), which strongly anchors identity. Do NOT describe clothing, location, or momentary physical action — they change and do not help identification.

metadata.title: Game of Thrones - Cripples, Bastards, and Broken Things
metadata.description: The episode opens with the 'Game of Thrones' title card and credits for writers David Benioff & D.B. Weiss and director Tim Van Patten. A deserter from the Night's Watch is captured and brought to Winterfell, where he frantically warns of 'monsters' and 'white walkers' beyond the Wall. Eddard Stark, accompanied by his sons Robb, Bran, and Jon Snow, executes the deserter, fulfilling the ancient law that the judge must be the executioner. Bran is visibly shaken by the event. Later, during a hunt in the Wolfswood, the Stark family discovers a dead direwolf and its five pups. Despite Eddard's initial decision to kill the pups, Maester Luwin persuades him to let the children raise them, noting the direwolf is the Stark sigil. The children each claim a pup, beginning their lifelong bond with their direwolves.

gemini_result.title: Game of Thrones - Bran's Archery Practice and the Deserter's Execution
gemini_result.description: The video begins with the Game of Thrones title sequence and credits. It then transitions to a foggy landscape where a deserter from the Night's Watch, Will, is captured by riders. The scene shifts to Winterfell, where young Bran Stark is practicing archery in the courtyard, watched by his brothers Robb and Jon, and his parents Ned and Catelyn Stark from a balcony. Bran struggles to hit the target, receiving advice from his brothers. Meanwhile, inside, Sansa Stark is praised for her embroidery by Septa Mordane, while Arya Stark struggles with hers. Back outside, Arya shows up Bran by hitting a bullseye, leading to a playful chase. The lighthearted moment is interrupted when Rodrik Cassel informs Ned about the captured deserter. Ned decides he must execute the man and insists that Bran accompany them to witness it, despite Catelyn's objections. The final scene takes place at an execution block in the hills. Will, the deserter, frantically claims he saw White Walkers before Ned Stark executes him with his greatsword, Ice, as Bran watches without looking away.

axis_descriptions.entity: Extract every important person/character in the range once each, merging recurrences into one canonical entry. Include foreground people/characters, speakers, addressed people/characters, partially visible but important interactors, and entities needed to understand actions, conflicts, portrayals, or relationships. Do not skip an unknown-name entity if it is distinguishable or narratively important. Skip background extras with no interaction or story function. person = real individual, character = fictional/performed role; if both a real person and their portrayed character are identifiable, emit them separately. Appearance segmentation — entities are extracted segment by segment, so a lead who recurs in every segment can crowd out the people around them. Treat each NEW person/character's first appearance as the start of a new entity segment. When several people appear at once, split that window into shorter appearance intervals and emit EVERY person in it — including secondary, briefly-seen, or interacting-but-not-central people — not only the most prominent one.
axis_descriptions.shot: This is the 3rd of 7 parts of the video, and it is 529 seconds long. Segment this part into granular, contiguous shots using visual and audio cues: cuts, major camera-motion changes, event/action beats, dialogue or speaker turns, subject/framing/setting changes, and entity entries/exits. When unsure, prefer shorter coherent shots. Cover this part end to end with no gaps: the FIRST shot must start at 0 and the LAST shot must end at 529 (the full length of this part). Do not omit or trim static or silent spans at the head or tail.
axis_descriptions.video: This is the 3rd of 7 parts of the video. Emit exactly ONE segment covering this whole part, using chunk-relative times: `start_time=0`, `end_time=529`.

segment_definitions.description: Extract every important person/character in the range once each, merging recurrences into one canonical entry. Include foreground people/characters, speakers, addressed people/characters, partially visible but important interactors, and entities needed to understand actions, conflicts, portrayals, or relationships. Do not skip an unknown-name entity if it is distinguishable or narratively important. Skip background extras with no interaction or story function. person = real individual, character = fictional/performed role; if both a real person and their portrayed character are identifiable, emit them separately. Appearance segmentation — entities are extracted segment by segment, so a lead who recurs in every segment can crowd out the people around them. Treat each NEW person/character's first appearance as the start of a new entity segment. When several people appear at once, split that window into shorter appearance intervals and emit EVERY person in it — including secondary, briefly-seen, or interacting-but-not-central people — not only the most prominent one.

fields.name: canonical_name
fields.description: Name, in order of preference: (1) the entity's full name (a real person's real name, or a character's in-story name); (2) a name spoken or shown on screen; (3) a descriptive label built from stable, distinguishing traits (e.g. 'bald older guard', 'red-haired teen') — avoid unstable traits like clothing. These entities are later merged across videos by matching name, so the name must be specific enough that two DIFFERENT people never collide on the same name: never a bare generic ('man', 'woman', 'person'), which would wrongly merge unrelated people. One name per entity; the description elaborates further.

fields.name: entity_type
fields.description: Whether this entity is a person or a character.

fields.name: description
fields.description: Identifying description of this entity. Elaborate the entity to support its name and future cross-video merging: stable appearance, role, key relationships/interactions, and any grounded aliases or names heard. Prefer visible, spoken, OCR, or credited evidence over guesses.

fields.name: relationships
fields.description: Each relationship starting FROM this entity, output exactly as `dst_name;dst_type;rel_type;desc` (empty array if none). dst_name / dst_type must copy another emitted entity verbatim. rel_type is `actor_portrays_character` (person -> character only; desc must be `other`) or `has_social_relationship` (same type only). desc must be exactly one of family / friend / colleague / acquaintance / rival / other. Do not add evidence text, labels, colons, extra semicolons, or any value outside the enum in this field. Put relationship evidence in the entity description instead. Emit only clearly evidenced ties, not mere co-presence; emit symmetric social ties once.

fields.name: best_thumbnail
fields.description: Best, clearest, most front-facing frame of the entity. Output a list of 5 numbers `[time_sec, y_min, x_min, y_max, x_max]`: time_sec is seconds from the chunk start; the box is normalized to [0,1000] (top-left, bottom-right) and tightly encloses the entity. Output [] if not clearly visible.

fields.name: second_best_thumbnail
fields.description: A frame as different as possible from best_thumbnail — a different time, ideally a different shot, and a different pose / angle / expression. Output a list of 5 numbers `[time_sec, y_min, x_min, y_max, x_max]`: time_sec is seconds from the chunk start; the box is normalized to [0,1000] (top-left, bottom-right) and tightly encloses the entity. Output [] if not clearly visible.

fields.name: third_best_thumbnail
fields.description: A frame distinct from and far in time from both other thumbnails, so the three span the entity's varied appearances. Output a list of 5 numbers `[time_sec, y_min, x_min, y_max, x_max]`: time_sec is seconds from the chunk start; the box is normalized to [0,1000] (top-left, bottom-right) and tightly encloses the entity. Output [] if not clearly visible.

segment_definitions.description: This is the 3rd of 7 parts of the video, and it is 529 seconds long. Segment this part into granular, contiguous shots using visual and audio cues: cuts, major camera-motion changes, event/action beats, dialogue or speaker turns, subject/framing/setting changes, and entity entries/exits. When unsure, prefer shorter coherent shots. Cover this part end to end with no gaps: the FIRST shot must start at 0 and the LAST shot must end at 529 (the full length of this part). Do not omit or trim static or silent spans at the head or tail.

fields.name: description
fields.description: Describe this shot in rich, verbose detail — cover every meaningful visual and non-visual detail: the actions and interactions, objects, setting, on-screen text, and any spoken dialogue quoted verbatim (with the speaker when certain). Refer to each person or character by the EXACT same name you give it in the `entities` field. Write enough that the shot stands on its own and, read in order, the shots reconstruct the whole story.

fields.name: shot_type
fields.description: The type of the shot.

fields.name: camera_motion
fields.description: The camera motion of the shot.

fields.name: camera_angle
fields.description: The camera angle of the shot.

fields.name: entities
fields.description: Each entity evidenced in this shot, output exactly as `name;entity_type;description`. Include only entities visible, audible, or OCR-referenced in this shot: foreground or speaking persons/characters, important addressed or interacted-with people/characters, and entities needed to understand the action, conflict, portrayal, or relationship; also central places, objects, concepts, or things when they drive the shot's meaning. entity_type is one of person / character / place / object / concept / thing, lowercase. Use exactly two semicolons as separators; do not put semicolons inside name, entity_type, or description (use commas or dashes). For the `name` element (person/character): Name, in order of preference: (1) the entity's full name (a real person's real name, or a character's in-story name); (2) a name spoken or shown on screen; (3) a descriptive label built from stable, distinguishing traits (e.g. 'bald older guard', 'red-haired teen') — avoid unstable traits like clothing. Decide from all available context, not this shot alone. The name must be specific enough that two DIFFERENT people never collide on the same name: never a bare generic ('man', 'woman', 'person'). **Use that entity's exact same name in every shot.** For the `description` element: Describe the entity for IDENTIFICATION — so that, from descriptions alone, one can tell which mentions across shots are the SAME person. Write SEVERAL sentences and pack in AS MANY stable identifying properties as you can: any name or alias heard, role, gender, approximate age, ethnicity, hair (color/length/style), facial features, build/height, distinctive marks, and especially their relationship or recurring interaction with other identified entities (e.g. 'the protagonist's mother', 'the man who repeatedly argues with the bald guard'), which strongly anchors identity. Do NOT describe clothing, location, or momentary physical action — they change and do not help identification.

segment_definitions.description: This is the 3rd of 7 parts of the video. Emit exactly ONE segment covering this whole part, using chunk-relative times: `start_time=0`, `end_time=529`.

fields.name: title
fields.description: Title of the whole video; best guess from in-chunk evidence (titles / credits / dialogue).

fields.name: description
fields.description: Summarize this chunk using only evidence from the emitted shot segments — cover essential story beats, key people/characters, important relationships/conflicts, locations, objects, and short grounded dialogue/OCR facts; do not introduce unsupported facts. Integrate events, objects, story context, visual detail, and audio/OCR/transcription facts into one account: names, relationships, intentions, conflicts, interaction evidence, and production cues like 'Action!'/'Cut!' when evidenced. Quote only short exact phrases; do not reproduce the transcript or assert an uncertain speaker.

fields.name: work
fields.description: Optional work title inferred from this chunk (for root candidate materialization).

A young boy with blond hair, wearing a dark cloak. He is seen riding a horse in a castle courtyard.

In a dimly lit forest, Bran Stark, Robb Stark, and Jon Snow stand near a dead direwolf. Robb holds a direwolf pup, and Jon holds another. Bran looks at the pups with curiosity. The scene is somber, with the dead direwolf in the foreground.

metadata.title: The Stark Children and the Royal Arrival
metadata.description: This segment follows multiple storylines: the Stark children (Robb, Jon, Bran, Rickon) bond with direwolf pups in a forest; in King's Landing, the funeral of Jon Arryn is held, where Cersei and Jaime Lannister discuss the political implications of his death and the upcoming arrival of King Robert Baratheon. At Winterfell, Catelyn Stark informs Eddard Stark of Jon Arryn's death and the King's visit, leading to preparations for the royal arrival. The segment concludes with the royal procession, including Joffrey, Cersei, and Tyrion Lannister, entering Winterfell, where they are greeted by the Stark family.

gemini_result.title: Game of Thrones - The Direwolves and The King's Arrival
gemini_result.description: The Stark children find a litter of direwolf pups in the forest, and Jon Snow finds an albino runt for himself. In King's Landing, Cersei and Jaime Lannister discuss the death of Jon Arryn, the Hand of the King, and the secret he might have known. At Winterfell, Catelyn Stark informs Ned Stark of Jon Arryn's death and that King Robert Baratheon is riding north to Winterfell, prompting preparations for a royal visit.

shots.description: Close up of Jaime Lannister. He continues, "...you worry too much. It's starting to show."
shots.narrative_descriptor: Jaime Lannister continues, "...you worry too much. It's starting to show."

shots.narrative_descriptor: Cersei Lannister replies, "And you never worry about anything."

shots.narrative_descriptor: Jaime Lannister says, "There was nothing to be afraid of until you told Father."

shots.description: Close up of Cersei Lannister. She says, "We're Lannisters. Lannisters don't act like fools."
shots.narrative_descriptor: Cersei Lannister says, "We're Lannisters. Lannisters don't act like fools."

shots.description: Wide shot of Jon Arryn's body on the bier. Cersei's voiceover says, "What if Jon Arryn told someone?"

shots.narrative_descriptor: Cersei Lannister's voiceover says, "What if Jon Arryn told someone?" over a shot of Jon Arryn's body.

shots.description: Close up of Jaime Lannister. He asks, "But who would he tell?"
shots.narrative_descriptor: Jaime Lannister asks, "But who would he tell?"

shots.description: Close up of Cersei Lannister. She answers, "My husband."
shots.narrative_descriptor: Cersei Lannister answers, "My husband."

shots.description: Close up of Jaime Lannister. He says, "If he told the King, our heads would be decorating the city gates by now."
shots.narrative_descriptor: Jaime Lannister says, "If he told the King, our heads would be decorating the city gates by now."

shots.description: Close up of Cersei Lannister. She says, "Whatever Jon Arryn knew or didn't know, it died with him." Jaime's voiceover continues, "And Robert will choose a new Hand of the King, someone to do his job while he's off boar hunting and whoring."
shots.narrative_descriptor: Cersei Lannister says, "Whatever Jon Arryn knew or didn't know, it died with him." Jaime continues, "And Robert will choose a new Hand of the King, someone to do his job while he's off boar hunting and whoring."

shots.narrative_descriptor: Cersei Lannister says, "Or whatever."
