axis_descriptions.entity: Extract every important person/character in the range once each, merging recurrences into one canonical entry. Include foreground people/characters, speakers, addressed people/characters, partially visible but important interactors, and entities needed to understand actions, conflicts, portrayals, or relationships. Do not skip an unknown-name entity if it is distinguishable or narratively important. Skip background extras with no interaction or story function. person = real individual, character = fictional/performed role; if both a real person and their portrayed character are identifiable, emit them separately. Appearance segmentation — entities are extracted segment by segment, so a lead who recurs in every segment can crowd out the people around them. Treat each NEW person/character's first appearance as the start of a new entity segment. When several people appear at once, split that window into shorter appearance intervals and emit EVERY person in it — including secondary, briefly-seen, or interacting-but-not-central people — not only the most prominent one.
axis_descriptions.shot: This is the 4th of 7 parts of the video, and it is 528 seconds long. Segment this part into granular, contiguous shots using visual and audio cues: cuts, major camera-motion changes, event/action beats, dialogue or speaker turns, subject/framing/setting changes, and entity entries/exits. When unsure, prefer shorter coherent shots. Cover this part end to end with no gaps: the FIRST shot must start at 0 and the LAST shot must end at 528 (the full length of this part). Do not omit or trim static or silent spans at the head or tail.
axis_descriptions.video: This is the 4th of 7 parts of the video. Emit exactly ONE segment covering this whole part, using chunk-relative times: `start_time=0`, `end_time=528`.

segment_definitions.description: Extract every important person/character in the range once each, merging recurrences into one canonical entry. Include foreground people/characters, speakers, addressed people/characters, partially visible but important interactors, and entities needed to understand actions, conflicts, portrayals, or relationships. Do not skip an unknown-name entity if it is distinguishable or narratively important. Skip background extras with no interaction or story function. person = real individual, character = fictional/performed role; if both a real person and their portrayed character are identifiable, emit them separately. Appearance segmentation — entities are extracted segment by segment, so a lead who recurs in every segment can crowd out the people around them. Treat each NEW person/character's first appearance as the start of a new entity segment. When several people appear at once, split that window into shorter appearance intervals and emit EVERY person in it — including secondary, briefly-seen, or interacting-but-not-central people — not only the most prominent one.

fields.name: canonical_name
fields.description: Name, in order of preference: (1) the entity's full name (a real person's real name, or a character's in-story name); (2) a name spoken or shown on screen; (3) a descriptive label built from stable, distinguishing traits (e.g. 'bald older guard', 'red-haired teen') — avoid unstable traits like clothing. These entities are later merged across videos by matching name, so the name must be specific enough that two DIFFERENT people never collide on the same name: never a bare generic ('man', 'woman', 'person'), which would wrongly merge unrelated people. One name per entity; the description elaborates further.

fields.name: entity_type
fields.description: Whether this entity is a person or a character.

fields.name: description
fields.description: Identifying description of this entity. Elaborate the entity to support its name and future cross-video merging: stable appearance, role, key relationships/interactions, and any grounded aliases or names heard. Prefer visible, spoken, OCR, or credited evidence over guesses.

fields.name: relationships
fields.description: Each relationship starting FROM this entity, output exactly as `dst_name;dst_type;rel_type;desc` (empty array if none). dst_name / dst_type must copy another emitted entity verbatim. rel_type is `actor_portrays_character` (person -> character only; desc must be `other`) or `has_social_relationship` (same type only). desc must be exactly one of family / friend / colleague / acquaintance / rival / other. Do not add evidence text, labels, colons, extra semicolons, or any value outside the enum in this field. Put relationship evidence in the entity description instead. Emit only clearly evidenced ties, not mere co-presence; emit symmetric social ties once.

fields.name: best_thumbnail
fields.description: Best, clearest, most front-facing frame of the entity. Output a list of 5 numbers `[time_sec, y_min, x_min, y_max, x_max]`: time_sec is seconds from the chunk start; the box is normalized to [0,1000] (top-left, bottom-right) and tightly encloses the entity. Output [] if not clearly visible.

fields.name: second_best_thumbnail
fields.description: A frame as different as possible from best_thumbnail — a different time, ideally a different shot, and a different pose / angle / expression. Output a list of 5 numbers `[time_sec, y_min, x_min, y_max, x_max]`: time_sec is seconds from the chunk start; the box is normalized to [0,1000] (top-left, bottom-right) and tightly encloses the entity. Output [] if not clearly visible.

fields.name: third_best_thumbnail
fields.description: A frame distinct from and far in time from both other thumbnails, so the three span the entity's varied appearances. Output a list of 5 numbers `[time_sec, y_min, x_min, y_max, x_max]`: time_sec is seconds from the chunk start; the box is normalized to [0,1000] (top-left, bottom-right) and tightly encloses the entity. Output [] if not clearly visible.

segment_definitions.description: This is the 4th of 7 parts of the video, and it is 528 seconds long. Segment this part into granular, contiguous shots using visual and audio cues: cuts, major camera-motion changes, event/action beats, dialogue or speaker turns, subject/framing/setting changes, and entity entries/exits. When unsure, prefer shorter coherent shots. Cover this part end to end with no gaps: the FIRST shot must start at 0 and the LAST shot must end at 528 (the full length of this part). Do not omit or trim static or silent spans at the head or tail.

fields.name: description
fields.description: Describe this shot in rich, verbose detail — cover every meaningful visual and non-visual detail: the actions and interactions, objects, setting, on-screen text, and any spoken dialogue quoted verbatim (with the speaker when certain). Refer to each person or character by the EXACT same name you give it in the `entities` field. Write enough that the shot stands on its own and, read in order, the shots reconstruct the whole story.

fields.name: shot_type
fields.description: The type of the shot.

fields.name: camera_motion
fields.description: The camera motion of the shot.

fields.name: camera_angle
fields.description: The camera angle of the shot.

fields.name: entities
fields.description: Each entity evidenced in this shot, output exactly as `name;entity_type;description`. Include only entities visible, audible, or OCR-referenced in this shot: foreground or speaking persons/characters, important addressed or interacted-with people/characters, and entities needed to understand the action, conflict, portrayal, or relationship; also central places, objects, concepts, or things when they drive the shot's meaning. entity_type is one of person / character / place / object / concept / thing, lowercase. Use exactly two semicolons as separators; do not put semicolons inside name, entity_type, or description (use commas or dashes). For the `name` element (person/character): Name, in order of preference: (1) the entity's full name (a real person's real name, or a character's in-story name); (2) a name spoken or shown on screen; (3) a descriptive label built from stable, distinguishing traits (e.g. 'bald older guard', 'red-haired teen') — avoid unstable traits like clothing. Decide from all available context, not this shot alone. The name must be specific enough that two DIFFERENT people never collide on the same name: never a bare generic ('man', 'woman', 'person'). **Use that entity's exact same name in every shot.** For the `description` element: Describe the entity for IDENTIFICATION — so that, from descriptions alone, one can tell which mentions across shots are the SAME person. Write SEVERAL sentences and pack in AS MANY stable identifying properties as you can: any name or alias heard, role, gender, approximate age, ethnicity, hair (color/length/style), facial features, build/height, distinctive marks, and especially their relationship or recurring interaction with other identified entities (e.g. 'the protagonist's mother', 'the man who repeatedly argues with the bald guard'), which strongly anchors identity. Do NOT describe clothing, location, or momentary physical action — they change and do not help identification.

segment_definitions.description: This is the 4th of 7 parts of the video. Emit exactly ONE segment covering this whole part, using chunk-relative times: `start_time=0`, `end_time=528`.

fields.name: title
fields.description: Title of the whole video; best guess from in-chunk evidence (titles / credits / dialogue).

fields.name: description
fields.description: Summarize this chunk using only evidence from the emitted shot segments — cover essential story beats, key people/characters, important relationships/conflicts, locations, objects, and short grounded dialogue/OCR facts; do not introduce unsupported facts. Integrate events, objects, story context, visual detail, and audio/OCR/transcription facts into one account: names, relationships, intentions, conflicts, interaction evidence, and production cues like 'Action!'/'Cut!' when evidenced. Quote only short exact phrases; do not reproduce the transcript or assert an uncertain speaker.

fields.name: work
fields.description: Optional work title inferred from this chunk (for root candidate materialization).

params.summary_prompt: Summarize the video for segmentation context.

metadata.description: King Robert Baratheon, a large, bearded man with long dark hair, wearing a black fur cloak and leather armor. He is the new king of the Seven Kingdoms, arriving at Winterfell.
Lady Catelyn Stark, a woman with long red hair, wearing a fur cloak. She is Eddard Stark's wife and the mother of his children.
Queen Cersei Lannister, a woman with long blonde hair, wearing a fur cloak and a red dress. She is Robert Baratheon's wife and the mother of his children.
Ser Jaime Lannister, a man with long blonde hair, wearing golden armor. He is Cersei Lannister's twin brother and the Kingslayer.
Prince Joffrey Baratheon, a young boy with blonde hair, wearing a red cloak. He is Robert Baratheon's eldest son.

metadata.title: The Kingsroad
metadata.description: King Robert Baratheon arrives at Winterfell, where he is greeted by Eddard Stark and his family. Robert appoints Eddard as his new Hand of the King. Meanwhile, Cersei Lannister arrives with her brothers, Jaime and Tyrion. Tyrion is seen enjoying himself in a brothel before being summoned by Cersei. In a separate location, Viserys Targaryen is seen with his sister Daenerys, whom he is preparing to marry off to a Dothraki leader.

axis_descriptions.entity: Extract every important person/character in the range once each, merging recurrences into one canonical entry. Include foreground people/characters, speakers, addressed people/characters, partially visible but important interactors, and entities needed to understand actions, conflicts, portrayals, or relationships. Do not skip an unknown-name entity if it is distinguishable or narratively important. Skip background extras with no interaction or story function. person = real individual, character = fictional/performed role; if both a real person and their portrayed character are identifiable, emit them separately. Appearance segmentation — entities are extracted segment by segment, so a lead who recurs in every segment can crowd out the people around them. Treat each NEW person/character's first appearance as the start of a new entity segment. When several people appear at once, split that window into shorter appearance intervals and emit EVERY person in it — including secondary, briefly-seen, or interacting-but-not-central people — not only the most prominent one.
axis_descriptions.shot: This is the 5th of 7 parts of the video, and it is 528 seconds long. Segment this part into granular, contiguous shots using visual and audio cues: cuts, major camera-motion changes, event/action beats, dialogue or speaker turns, subject/framing/setting changes, and entity entries/exits. When unsure, prefer shorter coherent shots. Cover this part end to end with no gaps: the FIRST shot must start at 0 and the LAST shot must end at 528 (the full length of this part). Do not omit or trim static or silent spans at the head or tail.
axis_descriptions.video: This is the 5th of 7 parts of the video. Emit exactly ONE segment covering this whole part, using chunk-relative times: `start_time=0`, `end_time=528`.

segment_definitions.description: Extract every important person/character in the range once each, merging recurrences into one canonical entry. Include foreground people/characters, speakers, addressed people/characters, partially visible but important interactors, and entities needed to understand actions, conflicts, portrayals, or relationships. Do not skip an unknown-name entity if it is distinguishable or narratively important. Skip background extras with no interaction or story function. person = real individual, character = fictional/performed role; if both a real person and their portrayed character are identifiable, emit them separately. Appearance segmentation — entities are extracted segment by segment, so a lead who recurs in every segment can crowd out the people around them. Treat each NEW person/character's first appearance as the start of a new entity segment. When several people appear at once, split that window into shorter appearance intervals and emit EVERY person in it — including secondary, briefly-seen, or interacting-but-not-central people — not only the most prominent one.

fields.name: canonical_name
fields.description: Name, in order of preference: (1) the entity's full name (a real person's real name, or a character's in-story name); (2) a name spoken or shown on screen; (3) a descriptive label built from stable, distinguishing traits (e.g. 'bald older guard', 'red-haired teen') — avoid unstable traits like clothing. These entities are later merged across videos by matching name, so the name must be specific enough that two DIFFERENT people never collide on the same name: never a bare generic ('man', 'woman', 'person'), which would wrongly merge unrelated people. One name per entity; the description elaborates further.

fields.name: entity_type
fields.description: Whether this entity is a person or a character.

fields.name: description
fields.description: Identifying description of this entity. Elaborate the entity to support its name and future cross-video merging: stable appearance, role, key relationships/interactions, and any grounded aliases or names heard. Prefer visible, spoken, OCR, or credited evidence over guesses.

fields.name: relationships
fields.description: Each relationship starting FROM this entity, output exactly as `dst_name;dst_type;rel_type;desc` (empty array if none). dst_name / dst_type must copy another emitted entity verbatim. rel_type is `actor_portrays_character` (person -> character only; desc must be `other`) or `has_social_relationship` (same type only). desc must be exactly one of family / friend / colleague / acquaintance / rival / other. Do not add evidence text, labels, colons, extra semicolons, or any value outside the enum in this field. Put relationship evidence in the entity description instead. Emit only clearly evidenced ties, not mere co-presence; emit symmetric social ties once.

fields.name: best_thumbnail
fields.description: Best, clearest, most front-facing frame of the entity. Output a list of 5 numbers `[time_sec, y_min, x_min, y_max, x_max]`: time_sec is seconds from the chunk start; the box is normalized to [0,1000] (top-left, bottom-right) and tightly encloses the entity. Output [] if not clearly visible.

fields.name: second_best_thumbnail
fields.description: A frame as different as possible from best_thumbnail — a different time, ideally a different shot, and a different pose / angle / expression. Output a list of 5 numbers `[time_sec, y_min, x_min, y_max, x_max]`: time_sec is seconds from the chunk start; the box is normalized to [0,1000] (top-left, bottom-right) and tightly encloses the entity. Output [] if not clearly visible.

fields.name: third_best_thumbnail
fields.description: A frame distinct from and far in time from both other thumbnails, so the three span the entity's varied appearances. Output a list of 5 numbers `[time_sec, y_min, x_min, y_max, x_max]`: time_sec is seconds from the chunk start; the box is normalized to [0,1000] (top-left, bottom-right) and tightly encloses the entity. Output [] if not clearly visible.

segment_definitions.description: This is the 5th of 7 parts of the video, and it is 528 seconds long. Segment this part into granular, contiguous shots using visual and audio cues: cuts, major camera-motion changes, event/action beats, dialogue or speaker turns, subject/framing/setting changes, and entity entries/exits. When unsure, prefer shorter coherent shots. Cover this part end to end with no gaps: the FIRST shot must start at 0 and the LAST shot must end at 528 (the full length of this part). Do not omit or trim static or silent spans at the head or tail.

fields.name: description
fields.description: Describe this shot in rich, verbose detail — cover every meaningful visual and non-visual detail: the actions and interactions, objects, setting, on-screen text, and any spoken dialogue quoted verbatim (with the speaker when certain). Refer to each person or character by the EXACT same name you give it in the `entities` field. Write enough that the shot stands on its own and, read in order, the shots reconstruct the whole story.

fields.name: shot_type
fields.description: The type of the shot.

fields.name: camera_motion
fields.description: The camera motion of the shot.

fields.name: camera_angle
fields.description: The camera angle of the shot.

fields.name: entities
fields.description: Each entity evidenced in this shot, output exactly as `name;entity_type;description`. Include only entities visible, audible, or OCR-referenced in this shot: foreground or speaking persons/characters, important addressed or interacted-with people/characters, and entities needed to understand the action, conflict, portrayal, or relationship; also central places, objects, concepts, or things when they drive the shot's meaning. entity_type is one of person / character / place / object / concept / thing, lowercase. Use exactly two semicolons as separators; do not put semicolons inside name, entity_type, or description (use commas or dashes). For the `name` element (person/character): Name, in order of preference: (1) the entity's full name (a real person's real name, or a character's in-story name); (2) a name spoken or shown on screen; (3) a descriptive label built from stable, distinguishing traits (e.g. 'bald older guard', 'red-haired teen') — avoid unstable traits like clothing. Decide from all available context, not this shot alone. The name must be specific enough that two DIFFERENT people never collide on the same name: never a bare generic ('man', 'woman', 'person'). **Use that entity's exact same name in every shot.** For the `description` element: Describe the entity for IDENTIFICATION — so that, from descriptions alone, one can tell which mentions across shots are the SAME person. Write SEVERAL sentences and pack in AS MANY stable identifying properties as you can: any name or alias heard, role, gender, approximate age, ethnicity, hair (color/length/style), facial features, build/height, distinctive marks, and especially their relationship or recurring interaction with other identified entities (e.g. 'the protagonist's mother', 'the man who repeatedly argues with the bald guard'), which strongly anchors identity. Do NOT describe clothing, location, or momentary physical action — they change and do not help identification.

segment_definitions.description: This is the 5th of 7 parts of the video. Emit exactly ONE segment covering this whole part, using chunk-relative times: `start_time=0`, `end_time=528`.

fields.name: title
fields.description: Title of the whole video; best guess from in-chunk evidence (titles / credits / dialogue).

fields.name: description
fields.description: Summarize this chunk using only evidence from the emitted shot segments — cover essential story beats, key people/characters, important relationships/conflicts, locations, objects, and short grounded dialogue/OCR facts; do not introduce unsupported facts. Integrate events, objects, story context, visual detail, and audio/OCR/transcription facts into one account: names, relationships, intentions, conflicts, interaction evidence, and production cues like 'Action!'/'Cut!' when evidenced. Quote only short exact phrases; do not reproduce the transcript or assert an uncertain speaker.

fields.name: work
fields.description: Optional work title inferred from this chunk (for root candidate materialization).

metadata.title: Game of Thrones - Season 1, Episode 1
metadata.description: This segment follows multiple storylines. Daenerys Targaryen is introduced to Khal Drogo by her brother Viserys and Illyrio Mopatis in Pentos, where Drogo is impressed by her beauty. Viserys reassures a reluctant Daenerys that they will return to Westeros with an army to reclaim the Iron Throne. In King's Landing, Sansa Stark expresses her infatuation with Joffrey to Catelyn, who warns her of the political implications. At Winterfell, Jon Snow is approached by Benjen Stark, who invites him to join the Night's Watch, and later encounters Tyrion Lannister, who taunts him about his status as a bastard. The segment concludes with scenes of feasting and revelry at Winterfell, featuring Ned Stark and Benjen.

gemini_result.title: Game of Thrones - Daenerys Meets Khal Drogo
gemini_result.description: Viserys Targaryen and Illyrio Mopatis arrange a marriage between Daenerys Targaryen and Khal Drogo in Pentos. Daenerys is reluctant but Viserys forces her to comply. Meanwhile, in Winterfell, Catelyn Stark brushes Sansa Stark's hair as they discuss Sansa's potential marriage to Prince Joffrey.

axis_descriptions.entity: Extract every important person/character in the range once each, merging recurrences into one canonical entry. Include foreground people/characters, speakers, addressed people/characters, partially visible but important interactors, and entities needed to understand actions, conflicts, portrayals, or relationships. Do not skip an unknown-name entity if it is distinguishable or narratively important. Skip background extras with no interaction or story function. person = real individual, character = fictional/performed role; if both a real person and their portrayed character are identifiable, emit them separately. Appearance segmentation — entities are extracted segment by segment, so a lead who recurs in every segment can crowd out the people around them. Treat each NEW person/character's first appearance as the start of a new entity segment. When several people appear at once, split that window into shorter appearance intervals and emit EVERY person in it — including secondary, briefly-seen, or interacting-but-not-central people — not only the most prominent one.
axis_descriptions.shot: This is the 6th of 7 parts of the video, and it is 528 seconds long. Segment this part into granular, contiguous shots using visual and audio cues: cuts, major camera-motion changes, event/action beats, dialogue or speaker turns, subject/framing/setting changes, and entity entries/exits. When unsure, prefer shorter coherent shots. Cover this part end to end with no gaps: the FIRST shot must start at 0 and the LAST shot must end at 528 (the full length of this part). Do not omit or trim static or silent spans at the head or tail.
axis_descriptions.video: This is the 6th of 7 parts of the video. Emit exactly ONE segment covering this whole part, using chunk-relative times: `start_time=0`, `end_time=528`.

segment_definitions.description: Extract every important person/character in the range once each, merging recurrences into one canonical entry. Include foreground people/characters, speakers, addressed people/characters, partially visible but important interactors, and entities needed to understand actions, conflicts, portrayals, or relationships. Do not skip an unknown-name entity if it is distinguishable or narratively important. Skip background extras with no interaction or story function. person = real individual, character = fictional/performed role; if both a real person and their portrayed character are identifiable, emit them separately. Appearance segmentation — entities are extracted segment by segment, so a lead who recurs in every segment can crowd out the people around them. Treat each NEW person/character's first appearance as the start of a new entity segment. When several people appear at once, split that window into shorter appearance intervals and emit EVERY person in it — including secondary, briefly-seen, or interacting-but-not-central people — not only the most prominent one.

fields.name: canonical_name
fields.description: Name, in order of preference: (1) the entity's full name (a real person's real name, or a character's in-story name); (2) a name spoken or shown on screen; (3) a descriptive label built from stable, distinguishing traits (e.g. 'bald older guard', 'red-haired teen') — avoid unstable traits like clothing. These entities are later merged across videos by matching name, so the name must be specific enough that two DIFFERENT people never collide on the same name: never a bare generic ('man', 'woman', 'person'), which would wrongly merge unrelated people. One name per entity; the description elaborates further.

fields.name: entity_type
fields.description: Whether this entity is a person or a character.

fields.name: description
fields.description: Identifying description of this entity. Elaborate the entity to support its name and future cross-video merging: stable appearance, role, key relationships/interactions, and any grounded aliases or names heard. Prefer visible, spoken, OCR, or credited evidence over guesses.

fields.name: relationships
fields.description: Each relationship starting FROM this entity, output exactly as `dst_name;dst_type;rel_type;desc` (empty array if none). dst_name / dst_type must copy another emitted entity verbatim. rel_type is `actor_portrays_character` (person -> character only; desc must be `other`) or `has_social_relationship` (same type only). desc must be exactly one of family / friend / colleague / acquaintance / rival / other. Do not add evidence text, labels, colons, extra semicolons, or any value outside the enum in this field. Put relationship evidence in the entity description instead. Emit only clearly evidenced ties, not mere co-presence; emit symmetric social ties once.

fields.name: best_thumbnail
fields.description: Best, clearest, most front-facing frame of the entity. Output a list of 5 numbers `[time_sec, y_min, x_min, y_max, x_max]`: time_sec is seconds from the chunk start; the box is normalized to [0,1000] (top-left, bottom-right) and tightly encloses the entity. Output [] if not clearly visible.

fields.name: second_best_thumbnail
fields.description: A frame as different as possible from best_thumbnail — a different time, ideally a different shot, and a different pose / angle / expression. Output a list of 5 numbers `[time_sec, y_min, x_min, y_max, x_max]`: time_sec is seconds from the chunk start; the box is normalized to [0,1000] (top-left, bottom-right) and tightly encloses the entity. Output [] if not clearly visible.

fields.name: third_best_thumbnail
fields.description: A frame distinct from and far in time from both other thumbnails, so the three span the entity's varied appearances. Output a list of 5 numbers `[time_sec, y_min, x_min, y_max, x_max]`: time_sec is seconds from the chunk start; the box is normalized to [0,1000] (top-left, bottom-right) and tightly encloses the entity. Output [] if not clearly visible.

segment_definitions.description: This is the 6th of 7 parts of the video, and it is 528 seconds long. Segment this part into granular, contiguous shots using visual and audio cues: cuts, major camera-motion changes, event/action beats, dialogue or speaker turns, subject/framing/setting changes, and entity entries/exits. When unsure, prefer shorter coherent shots. Cover this part end to end with no gaps: the FIRST shot must start at 0 and the LAST shot must end at 528 (the full length of this part). Do not omit or trim static or silent spans at the head or tail.

fields.name: description
fields.description: Describe this shot in rich, verbose detail — cover every meaningful visual and non-visual detail: the actions and interactions, objects, setting, on-screen text, and any spoken dialogue quoted verbatim (with the speaker when certain). Refer to each person or character by the EXACT same name you give it in the `entities` field. Write enough that the shot stands on its own and, read in order, the shots reconstruct the whole story.

fields.name: shot_type
fields.description: The type of the shot.

fields.name: camera_motion
fields.description: The camera motion of the shot.

fields.name: camera_angle
fields.description: The camera angle of the shot.

fields.name: entities
fields.description: Each entity evidenced in this shot, output exactly as `name;entity_type;description`. Include only entities visible, audible, or OCR-referenced in this shot: foreground or speaking persons/characters, important addressed or interacted-with people/characters, and entities needed to understand the action, conflict, portrayal, or relationship; also central places, objects, concepts, or things when they drive the shot's meaning. entity_type is one of person / character / place / object / concept / thing, lowercase. Use exactly two semicolons as separators; do not put semicolons inside name, entity_type, or description (use commas or dashes). For the `name` element (person/character): Name, in order of preference: (1) the entity's full name (a real person's real name, or a character's in-story name); (2) a name spoken or shown on screen; (3) a descriptive label built from stable, distinguishing traits (e.g. 'bald older guard', 'red-haired teen') — avoid unstable traits like clothing. Decide from all available context, not this shot alone. The name must be specific enough that two DIFFERENT people never collide on the same name: never a bare generic ('man', 'woman', 'person'). **Use that entity's exact same name in every shot.** For the `description` element: Describe the entity for IDENTIFICATION — so that, from descriptions alone, one can tell which mentions across shots are the SAME person. Write SEVERAL sentences and pack in AS MANY stable identifying properties as you can: any name or alias heard, role, gender, approximate age, ethnicity, hair (color/length/style), facial features, build/height, distinctive marks, and especially their relationship or recurring interaction with other identified entities (e.g. 'the protagonist's mother', 'the man who repeatedly argues with the bald guard'), which strongly anchors identity. Do NOT describe clothing, location, or momentary physical action — they change and do not help identification.

segment_definitions.description: This is the 6th of 7 parts of the video. Emit exactly ONE segment covering this whole part, using chunk-relative times: `start_time=0`, `end_time=528`.

fields.name: title
fields.description: Title of the whole video; best guess from in-chunk evidence (titles / credits / dialogue).

fields.name: description
fields.description: Summarize this chunk using only evidence from the emitted shot segments — cover essential story beats, key people/characters, important relationships/conflicts, locations, objects, and short grounded dialogue/OCR facts; do not introduce unsupported facts. Integrate events, objects, story context, visual detail, and audio/OCR/transcription facts into one account: names, relationships, intentions, conflicts, interaction evidence, and production cues like 'Action!'/'Cut!' when evidenced. Quote only short exact phrases; do not reproduce the transcript or assert an uncertain speaker.

fields.name: work
fields.description: Optional work title inferred from this chunk (for root candidate materialization).

params.summary_prompt: Summarize the video for segmentation context.

metadata.description: Ned Stark is the Lord of Winterfell, characterized by his long brown hair, beard, and serious demeanor. He is seen in a dimly lit room, wearing dark clothing, and is engaged in a conversation with Benjen Stark.
Robb Stark is the eldest son of Ned Stark, characterized by his curly brown hair and youthful appearance. He is seen hugging Benjen Stark in a dimly lit room.
Catelyn Stark is the wife of Ned Stark and mother of his children. She has long brown hair and is seen sitting at a table with Cersei Lannister, wearing a dark dress.
Sansa Stark is the eldest daughter of Ned Stark, characterized by her long brown hair and youthful appearance. She is seen sitting at a table with Arya Stark, wearing a dark dress.

Myrcella Baratheon is the daughter of Cersei Lannister and King Robert Baratheon, characterized by her long red hair and youthful appearance. She is seen standing in a dimly lit room, wearing a dark dress.

metadata.title: Game of Thrones - Winterfell and Pentos
metadata.description: This segment captures several key storylines. At Winterfell, Ned Stark discusses the dangers beyond the Wall with Benjen Stark, while Catelyn Stark converses with Cersei Lannister about Sansa. A tense encounter occurs between Ned and Jaime Lannister regarding a potential tournament. Later, Catelyn receives a letter from her sister Lysa Arryn, who claims the Lannisters murdered Jon Arryn and that King Robert is in danger. Ned is visibly shaken by the news. Meanwhile, in Pentos, Daenerys Targaryen and Viserys Targaryen are guests of Khal Drogo. Daenerys witnesses a brutal Dothraki bloodriders' fight, which ends in death, and is later presented with a stack of books by Jorah Mormont.

gemini_result.title: Game of Thrones - Winterfell Feast and The Letter
gemini_result.description: During the feast at Winterfell, Ned Stark and his brother Benjen discuss the deserter and the ominous signs beyond the Wall. Queen Cersei Lannister converses with Catelyn Stark and takes an interest in Sansa Stark, while Jaime Lannister subtly challenges Ned. Later that night, Ned and Catelyn are interrupted in their bedchamber by Maester Luwin, who brings a secret letter from Catelyn's sister, Lysa Arryn. The letter claims that the Lannisters murdered her husband, Jon Arryn, the former Hand of the King.

Ned dismisses the deserter's claims of monsters killing wildlings as nonsense.

The Queen of Westeros, with blonde hair styled in intricate braids.

place_entities.description: The large, dimly lit hall where the feast is held.

shots.narrative_descriptor: Catelyn asks Cersei if it's her first time in the North. Cersei says yes and that it's lovely.

shots.description: Sansa Stark is shown looking admiringly towards the high table. Catelyn remarks that the North is very gloomy compared to the capital and recalls how scared she was when Ned first brought her to Winterfell.

character_entities.description: Ned's wife, Lady of Winterfell, with long auburn hair.
Ned and Catelyn's eldest daughter, a young girl with auburn hair.

place_entities.description: The large, dimly lit hall where the feast is held.

shots.narrative_descriptor: Sansa looks at Cersei while Catelyn talks about how gloomy the North is.

shots.description: Catelyn finishes her sentence. Cersei notices Sansa staring at her and looks back at the young girl with a slight smile.

character_entities.description: Ned's wife, Lady of Winterfell, with long auburn hair.
The Queen of Westeros, with blonde hair styled in intricate braids.

place_entities.description: The large, dimly lit hall where the feast is held.

shots.narrative_descriptor: Cersei notices Sansa looking at her.

shots.description: Sansa approaches the high table. Cersei warmly calls her a 'little dove' and praises her beauty, asking her age. Sansa proudly replies that she is thirteen and tall. Cersei asks if she is still growing, and Sansa says she probably is.

character_entities.description: The Queen of Westeros, with blonde hair styled in intricate braids.
Ned and Catelyn's eldest daughter, a young girl with auburn hair.

place_entities.description: The large, dimly lit hall where the feast is held.

shots.narrative_descriptor: Sansa approaches Cersei. Cersei calls her a 'little dove' and asks her age. Sansa says she is thirteen.

shots.description: Cersei continues to question Sansa, asking if she has had her moon blood yet, to which Sansa replies no. Cersei then asks if Sansa made her dress herself. When Sansa confirms, Cersei compliments the dress and asks Sansa to make her one like it.

character_entities.description: The Queen of Westeros, with blonde hair styled in intricate braids.
Ned and Catelyn's eldest daughter, a young girl with auburn hair.

place_entities.description: The large, dimly lit hall where the feast is held.

shots.narrative_descriptor: Cersei asks Sansa if she has had her moon blood and compliments her dress.

shots.description: Catelyn, observing the interaction, remarks to Cersei that they might have grandchildren someday. Cersei agrees, stating that Sansa will do well in the capital and that such a beauty shouldn't be hidden in the North forever.

character_entities.description: Ned's wife, Lady of Winterfell, with long auburn hair.
The Queen of Westeros, with blonde hair styled in intricate braids.

place_entities.description: The large, dimly lit hall where the feast is held.

shots.narrative_descriptor: Catelyn and Cersei discuss the possibility of having grandchildren together.

shots.description: Sansa walks back to her seat at the lower tables. Prince Joffrey Baratheon catches her eye and smiles at her. Sansa, clearly smitten, smiles back at him.

character_entities.description: Ned and Catelyn's eldest daughter, a young girl with auburn hair.
The Crown Prince, a young blonde boy.

place_entities.description: The large, dimly lit hall where the feast is held.

shots.narrative_descriptor: Sansa and Joffrey exchange smiles across the hall.

shots.description: Jaime Lannister approaches Ned Stark in the hall. Jaime expresses hope that they will be neighbors and suggests celebrating Ned's appointment with a tournament, eager to cross swords with him. Ned firmly declines, stating he doesn't fight in tournaments so his enemies won't know his capabilities in a real battle. Jaime praises this strategy.

character_entities.description: Lord of Winterfell, a serious and honorable man with shoulder-length brown hair and a beard.
A knight of the Kingsguard, Cersei's twin brother, with blonde hair.

place_entities.description: The large, dimly lit hall where the feast is held.

shots.narrative_descriptor: Jaime Lannister suggests a tournament to Ned Stark. Ned declines, saying he doesn't fight in tournaments so his enemies don't know what he can do.

shots.description: Back at the feast table, Arya Stark playfully uses her spoon to fling a piece of food, which hits Sansa squarely in the face. Sansa is outraged. Catelyn looks over with a stern, disapproving expression. Robb laughs at the incident and then escorts Arya away, telling her it's time for bed.

character_entities.description: Ned's wife, Lady of Winterfell, with long auburn hair.
Ned and Catelyn's eldest daughter, a young girl with auburn hair.
Ned and Catelyn's youngest daughter, a tomboyish girl.
Ned's eldest son, with curly reddish-brown hair.

place_entities.description: The large, dimly lit hall where the feast is held.

shots.narrative_descriptor: Arya throws food at Sansa. Robb laughs and takes Arya to bed.

shots.description: The scene cuts to a quiet, dark bedchamber in Winterfell. The camera slowly pans across the room, showing a lit fireplace and candles, eventually revealing Ned and Catelyn lying in bed together.

character_entities.description: Lord of Winterfell, a serious and honorable man with shoulder-length brown hair and a beard.
Ned's wife, Lady of Winterfell, with long auburn hair.

place_entities.description: Ned and Catelyn's private room, lit by candles and a fireplace.

shots.narrative_descriptor: Establishing shot of Ned and Catelyn's bedchamber.

shots.description: Ned and Catelyn converse intimately in bed. Ned expresses his reluctance to go south, calling the capital a 'nest of ugly southern rats' and stating his place is in the North with her. Catelyn playfully asserts she won't let the King take him. Ned jokes about King Robert's weight gain, noting he only eats when it's time to drink.

character_entities.description: Lord of Winterfell, a serious and honorable man with shoulder-length brown hair and a beard.
Ned's wife, Lady of Winterfell, with long auburn hair.

place_entities.description: Ned and Catelyn's private room, lit by candles and a fireplace.

shots.narrative_descriptor: Ned and Catelyn talk in bed. Ned says he doesn't want to go south. Catelyn says she won't let the King take him.

shots.description: Their private moment is interrupted by Maester Luwin, who appears at the chamber door. He apologizes for the intrusion and informs them that a rider has arrived in the night with a message from Catelyn's sister.

character_entities.description: Lord of Winterfell, a serious and honorable man with shoulder-length brown hair and a beard.
Ned's wife, Lady of Winterfell, with long auburn hair.
The Maester of Winterfell, an older bald man wearing a grey robe and a chain.

place_entities.description: Ned and Catelyn's private room, lit by candles and a fireplace.

shots.narrative_descriptor: Maester Luwin interrupts Ned and Catelyn to deliver a message from Catelyn's sister.

shots.description: Alarmed by the late-night message, Catelyn quickly gets out of bed, wraps a robe around herself, and walks over to Maester Luwin to take the small, folded letter from his hands.

character_entities.description: Lord of Winterfell, a serious and honorable man with shoulder-length brown hair and a beard.
Ned's wife, Lady of Winterfell, with long auburn hair.
The Maester of Winterfell, an older bald man wearing a grey robe and a chain.

place_entities.description: Ned and Catelyn's private room, lit by candles and a fireplace.

thing_entities.description: A small folded parchment sealed with black wax, sent by Lysa Arryn.

shots.narrative_descriptor: Catelyn gets out of bed and takes the letter from Maester Luwin.

shots.description: Catelyn examines the letter, noting the black wax seal. She breaks the seal and reads the contents, her expression growing increasingly concerned. Maester Luwin remarks that the letter was not sent by raven, indicating its secrecy.

character_entities.description: Ned's wife, Lady of Winterfell, with long auburn hair.
The Maester of Winterfell, an older bald man wearing a grey robe and a chain.

place_entities.description: Ned and Catelyn's private room, lit by candles and a fireplace.

thing_entities.description: A small folded parchment sealed with black wax, sent by Lysa Arryn.

shots.narrative_descriptor: Catelyn opens and reads the letter with the black seal.

shots.description: After reading the letter, Catelyn walks over to the fireplace and tosses the parchment into the flames, ensuring the dangerous message is destroyed.

character_entities.description: Ned's wife, Lady of Winterfell, with long auburn hair.

place_entities.description: Ned and Catelyn's private room, lit by candles and a fireplace.

thing_entities.description: A small folded parchment sealed with black wax, sent by Lysa Arryn.

shots.narrative_descriptor: Catelyn burns the letter in the fireplace.

shots.description: Catelyn turns back to Ned and Maester Luwin, revealing the letter's shocking contents: her sister Lysa has fled the capital and claims that the Lannisters murdered her husband, Jon Arryn. She also warns that the King is in danger. Ned tries to dismiss it as the ramblings of a grieving widow, but Catelyn insists Lysa wouldn't risk her life and her son's life to send such a message without cause.

character_entities.description: Lord of Winterfell, a serious and honorable man with shoulder-length brown hair and a beard.
Ned's wife, Lady of Winterfell, with long auburn hair.
The Maester of Winterfell, an older bald man wearing a grey robe and a chain.

place_entities.description: Ned and Catelyn's private room, lit by candles and a fireplace.

shots.narrative_descriptor: Catelyn tells Ned that Lysa claims the Lannisters murdered Jon Arryn and that the King is in danger.

axis_descriptions.entity: Extract every important person/character in the range once each, merging recurrences into one canonical entry. Include foreground people/characters, speakers, addressed people/characters, partially visible but important interactors, and entities needed to understand actions, conflicts, portrayals, or relationships. Do not skip an unknown-name entity if it is distinguishable or narratively important. Skip background extras with no interaction or story function. person = real individual, character = fictional/performed role; if both a real person and their portrayed character are identifiable, emit them separately. Appearance segmentation — entities are extracted segment by segment, so a lead who recurs in every segment can crowd out the people around them. Treat each NEW person/character's first appearance as the start of a new entity segment. When several people appear at once, split that window into shorter appearance intervals and emit EVERY person in it — including secondary, briefly-seen, or interacting-but-not-central people — not only the most prominent one.
axis_descriptions.shot: This is the 7th of 7 parts of the video, and it is 528 seconds long. Segment this part into granular, contiguous shots using visual and audio cues: cuts, major camera-motion changes, event/action beats, dialogue or speaker turns, subject/framing/setting changes, and entity entries/exits. When unsure, prefer shorter coherent shots. Cover this part end to end with no gaps: the FIRST shot must start at 0 and the LAST shot must end at 528 (the full length of this part). Do not omit or trim static or silent spans at the head or tail.
axis_descriptions.video: This is the 7th of 7 parts of the video. Emit exactly ONE segment covering this whole part, using chunk-relative times: `start_time=0`, `end_time=528`.

segment_definitions.description: Extract every important person/character in the range once each, merging recurrences into one canonical entry. Include foreground people/characters, speakers, addressed people/characters, partially visible but important interactors, and entities needed to understand actions, conflicts, portrayals, or relationships. Do not skip an unknown-name entity if it is distinguishable or narratively important. Skip background extras with no interaction or story function. person = real individual, character = fictional/performed role; if both a real person and their portrayed character are identifiable, emit them separately. Appearance segmentation — entities are extracted segment by segment, so a lead who recurs in every segment can crowd out the people around them. Treat each NEW person/character's first appearance as the start of a new entity segment. When several people appear at once, split that window into shorter appearance intervals and emit EVERY person in it — including secondary, briefly-seen, or interacting-but-not-central people — not only the most prominent one.

fields.name: canonical_name
fields.description: Name, in order of preference: (1) the entity's full name (a real person's real name, or a character's in-story name); (2) a name spoken or shown on screen; (3) a descriptive label built from stable, distinguishing traits (e.g. 'bald older guard', 'red-haired teen') — avoid unstable traits like clothing. These entities are later merged across videos by matching name, so the name must be specific enough that two DIFFERENT people never collide on the same name: never a bare generic ('man', 'woman', 'person'), which would wrongly merge unrelated people. One name per entity; the description elaborates further.

fields.name: entity_type
fields.description: Whether this entity is a person or a character.

fields.name: description
fields.description: Identifying description of this entity. Elaborate the entity to support its name and future cross-video merging: stable appearance, role, key relationships/interactions, and any grounded aliases or names heard. Prefer visible, spoken, OCR, or credited evidence over guesses.

fields.name: relationships
fields.description: Each relationship starting FROM this entity, output exactly as `dst_name;dst_type;rel_type;desc` (empty array if none). dst_name / dst_type must copy another emitted entity verbatim. rel_type is `actor_portrays_character` (person -> character only; desc must be `other`) or `has_social_relationship` (same type only). desc must be exactly one of family / friend / colleague / acquaintance / rival / other. Do not add evidence text, labels, colons, extra semicolons, or any value outside the enum in this field. Put relationship evidence in the entity description instead. Emit only clearly evidenced ties, not mere co-presence; emit symmetric social ties once.

fields.name: best_thumbnail
fields.description: Best, clearest, most front-facing frame of the entity. Output a list of 5 numbers `[time_sec, y_min, x_min, y_max, x_max]`: time_sec is seconds from the chunk start; the box is normalized to [0,1000] (top-left, bottom-right) and tightly encloses the entity. Output [] if not clearly visible.

fields.name: second_best_thumbnail
fields.description: A frame as different as possible from best_thumbnail — a different time, ideally a different shot, and a different pose / angle / expression. Output a list of 5 numbers `[time_sec, y_min, x_min, y_max, x_max]`: time_sec is seconds from the chunk start; the box is normalized to [0,1000] (top-left, bottom-right) and tightly encloses the entity. Output [] if not clearly visible.

fields.name: third_best_thumbnail
fields.description: A frame distinct from and far in time from both other thumbnails, so the three span the entity's varied appearances. Output a list of 5 numbers `[time_sec, y_min, x_min, y_max, x_max]`: time_sec is seconds from the chunk start; the box is normalized to [0,1000] (top-left, bottom-right) and tightly encloses the entity. Output [] if not clearly visible.

segment_definitions.description: This is the 7th of 7 parts of the video, and it is 528 seconds long. Segment this part into granular, contiguous shots using visual and audio cues: cuts, major camera-motion changes, event/action beats, dialogue or speaker turns, subject/framing/setting changes, and entity entries/exits. When unsure, prefer shorter coherent shots. Cover this part end to end with no gaps: the FIRST shot must start at 0 and the LAST shot must end at 528 (the full length of this part). Do not omit or trim static or silent spans at the head or tail.

fields.name: description
fields.description: Describe this shot in rich, verbose detail — cover every meaningful visual and non-visual detail: the actions and interactions, objects, setting, on-screen text, and any spoken dialogue quoted verbatim (with the speaker when certain). Refer to each person or character by the EXACT same name you give it in the `entities` field. Write enough that the shot stands on its own and, read in order, the shots reconstruct the whole story.

fields.name: shot_type
fields.description: The type of the shot.

fields.name: camera_motion
fields.description: The camera motion of the shot.

fields.name: camera_angle
fields.description: The camera angle of the shot.

fields.name: entities
fields.description: Each entity evidenced in this shot, output exactly as `name;entity_type;description`. Include only entities visible, audible, or OCR-referenced in this shot: foreground or speaking persons/characters, important addressed or interacted-with people/characters, and entities needed to understand the action, conflict, portrayal, or relationship; also central places, objects, concepts, or things when they drive the shot's meaning. entity_type is one of person / character / place / object / concept / thing, lowercase. Use exactly two semicolons as separators; do not put semicolons inside name, entity_type, or description (use commas or dashes). For the `name` element (person/character): Name, in order of preference: (1) the entity's full name (a real person's real name, or a character's in-story name); (2) a name spoken or shown on screen; (3) a descriptive label built from stable, distinguishing traits (e.g. 'bald older guard', 'red-haired teen') — avoid unstable traits like clothing. Decide from all available context, not this shot alone. The name must be specific enough that two DIFFERENT people never collide on the same name: never a bare generic ('man', 'woman', 'person'). **Use that entity's exact same name in every shot.** For the `description` element: Describe the entity for IDENTIFICATION — so that, from descriptions alone, one can tell which mentions across shots are the SAME person. Write SEVERAL sentences and pack in AS MANY stable identifying properties as you can: any name or alias heard, role, gender, approximate age, ethnicity, hair (color/length/style), facial features, build/height, distinctive marks, and especially their relationship or recurring interaction with other identified entities (e.g. 'the protagonist's mother', 'the man who repeatedly argues with the bald guard'), which strongly anchors identity. Do NOT describe clothing, location, or momentary physical action — they change and do not help identification.

segment_definitions.description: This is the 7th of 7 parts of the video. Emit exactly ONE segment covering this whole part, using chunk-relative times: `start_time=0`, `end_time=528`.

fields.name: title
fields.description: Title of the whole video; best guess from in-chunk evidence (titles / credits / dialogue).

fields.name: description
fields.description: Summarize this chunk using only evidence from the emitted shot segments — cover essential story beats, key people/characters, important relationships/conflicts, locations, objects, and short grounded dialogue/OCR facts; do not introduce unsupported facts. Integrate events, objects, story context, visual detail, and audio/OCR/transcription facts into one account: names, relationships, intentions, conflicts, interaction evidence, and production cues like 'Action!'/'Cut!' when evidenced. Quote only short exact phrases; do not reproduce the transcript or assert an uncertain speaker.

fields.name: work
fields.description: Optional work title inferred from this chunk (for root candidate materialization).

metadata.title: Game of Thrones - Season 1, Episode 2
metadata.description: This segment covers the final part of the video, featuring multiple storylines. Daenerys Targaryen is gifted dragon eggs by a Maester and a white horse by Khal Drogo, whom she later joins on a cliff at sunset. In another storyline, Tyrion Lannister and Sandor Clegane are seen in a courtyard, followed by Ned Stark and Ser Rodrik Cassel discussing a mission. The segment concludes with Bran Stark climbing a castle wall, where he discovers his father, Ned Stark, and his mother, Catelyn Stark, in a compromising situation, leading to a tense confrontation.

gemini_result.title: Game of Thrones - Daenerys Wedding and Winterfell
gemini_result.description: Daenerys Targaryen receives wedding gifts, including books from Ser Jorah Mormont and three dragon eggs from Magister Illyrio Mopatis. Khal Drogo gives her a white horse. Later, at sunset, Daenerys and Drogo consummate their marriage by the sea. The scene then cuts to Winterfell, where Tyrion Lannister and Sandor Clegane are seen, followed by Ned Stark and King Robert Baratheon preparing for a hunt.

axis_descriptions.entity: Extract every important person/character in the range once each, merging recurrences into one canonical entry. Include foreground people/characters, speakers, addressed people/characters, partially visible but important interactors, and entities needed to understand actions, conflicts, portrayals, or relationships. Do not skip an unknown-name entity if it is distinguishable or narratively important. Skip background extras with no interaction or story function. person = real individual, character = fictional/performed role; if both a real person and their portrayed character are identifiable, emit them separately. Appearance segmentation — entities are extracted segment by segment, so a lead who recurs in every segment can crowd out the people around them. Treat each NEW person/character's first appearance as the start of a new entity segment. When several people appear at once, split that window into shorter appearance intervals and emit EVERY person in it — including secondary, briefly-seen, or interacting-but-not-central people — not only the most prominent one.

When unsure, prefer shorter coherent shots. Cover this part end to end with no gaps: the FIRST shot must start at 0 and the LAST shot must end at 553 (the full length of this part). Do not omit or trim static or silent spans at the head or tail.

segment_definitions.description: Extract every important person/character in the range once each, merging recurrences into one canonical entry. Include foreground people/characters, speakers, addressed people/characters, partially visible but important interactors, and entities needed to understand actions, conflicts, portrayals, or relationships. Do not skip an unknown-name entity if it is distinguishable or narratively important. Skip background extras with no interaction or story function. person = real individual, character = fictional/performed role; if both a real person and their portrayed character are identifiable, emit them separately. Appearance segmentation — entities are extracted segment by segment, so a lead who recurs in every segment can crowd out the people around them. Treat each NEW person/character's first appearance as the start of a new entity segment. When several people appear at once, split that window into shorter appearance intervals and emit EVERY person in it — including secondary, briefly-seen, or interacting-but-not-central people — not only the most prominent one.

fields.name: canonical_name
fields.description: Name, in order of preference: (1) the entity's full name (a real person's real name, or a character's in-story name); (2) a name spoken or shown on screen; (3) a descriptive label built from stable, distinguishing traits (e.g. 'bald older guard', 'red-haired teen') — avoid unstable traits like clothing. These entities are later merged across videos by matching name, so the name must be specific enough that two DIFFERENT people never collide on the same name: never a bare generic ('man', 'woman', 'person'), which would wrongly merge unrelated people. One name per entity; the description elaborates further.

fields.name: entity_type
fields.description: Whether this entity is a person or a character.

fields.name: description
fields.description: Identifying description of this entity. Elaborate the entity to support its name and future cross-video merging: stable appearance, role, key relationships/interactions, and any grounded aliases or names heard. Prefer visible, spoken, OCR, or credited evidence over guesses.

fields.name: relationships
fields.description: Each relationship starting FROM this entity, output exactly as `dst_name;dst_type;rel_type;desc` (empty array if none). dst_name / dst_type must copy another emitted entity verbatim. rel_type is `actor_portrays_character` (person -> character only; desc must be `other`) or `has_social_relationship` (same type only). desc must be exactly one of family / friend / colleague / acquaintance / rival / other. Do not add evidence text, labels, colons, extra semicolons, or any value outside the enum in this field. Put relationship evidence in the entity description instead. Emit only clearly evidenced ties, not mere co-presence; emit symmetric social ties once.

fields.name: best_thumbnail
fields.description: Best, clearest, most front-facing frame of the entity. Output a list of 5 numbers `[time_sec, y_min, x_min, y_max, x_max]`: time_sec is seconds from the chunk start; the box is normalized to [0,1000] (top-left, bottom-right) and tightly encloses the entity. Output [] if not clearly visible.

fields.name: second_best_thumbnail
fields.description: A frame as different as possible from best_thumbnail — a different time, ideally a different shot, and a different pose / angle / expression. Output a list of 5 numbers `[time_sec, y_min, x_min, y_max, x_max]`: time_sec is seconds from the chunk start; the box is normalized to [0,1000] (top-left, bottom-right) and tightly encloses the entity. Output [] if not clearly visible.

fields.name: third_best_thumbnail
fields.description: A frame distinct from and far in time from both other thumbnails, so the three span the entity's varied appearances. Output a list of 5 numbers `[time_sec, y_min, x_min, y_max, x_max]`: time_sec is seconds from the chunk start; the box is normalized to [0,1000] (top-left, bottom-right) and tightly encloses the entity. Output [] if not clearly visible.

segment_definitions.description: This is the 1st of 6 parts of the video, and it is 553 seconds long. Segment this part into granular, contiguous shots using visual and audio cues: cuts, major camera-motion changes, event/action beats, dialogue or speaker turns, subject/framing/setting changes, and entity entries/exits. When unsure, prefer shorter coherent shots. Cover this part end to end with no gaps: the FIRST shot must start at 0 and the LAST shot must end at 553 (the full length of this part). Do not omit or trim static or silent spans at the head or tail.

fields.name: description
fields.description: Describe this shot in rich, verbose detail — cover every meaningful visual and non-visual detail: the actions and interactions, objects, setting, on-screen text, and any spoken dialogue quoted verbatim (with the speaker when certain). Refer to each person or character by the EXACT same name you give it in the `entities` field. Write enough that the shot stands on its own and, read in order, the shots reconstruct the whole story.

fields.name: shot_type
fields.description: The type of the shot.

fields.name: camera_motion
fields.description: The camera motion of the shot.

fields.name: camera_angle
fields.description: The camera angle of the shot.

fields.name: entities
fields.description: Each entity evidenced in this shot, output exactly as `name;entity_type;description`. Include only entities visible, audible, or OCR-referenced in this shot: foreground or speaking persons/characters, important addressed or interacted-with people/characters, and entities needed to understand the action, conflict, portrayal, or relationship; also central places, objects, concepts, or things when they drive the shot's meaning. entity_type is one of person / character / place / object / concept / thing, lowercase. Use exactly two semicolons as separators; do not put semicolons inside name, entity_type, or description (use commas or dashes). For the `name` element (person/character): Name, in order of preference: (1) the entity's full name (a real person's real name, or a character's in-story name); (2) a name spoken or shown on screen; (3) a descriptive label built from stable, distinguishing traits (e.g. 'bald older guard', 'red-haired teen') — avoid unstable traits like clothing. Decide from all available context, not this shot alone. The name must be specific enough that two DIFFERENT people never collide on the same name: never a bare generic ('man', 'woman', 'person'). **Use that entity's exact same name in every shot.** For the `description` element: Describe the entity for IDENTIFICATION — so that, from descriptions alone, one can tell which mentions across shots are the SAME person. Write SEVERAL sentences and pack in AS MANY stable identifying properties as you can: any name or alias heard, role, gender, approximate age, ethnicity, hair (color/length/style), facial features, build/height, distinctive marks, and especially their relationship or recurring interaction with other identified entities (e.g. 'the protagonist's mother', 'the man who repeatedly argues with the bald guard'), which strongly anchors identity. Do NOT describe clothing, location, or momentary physical action — they change and do not help identification.

segment_definitions.description: This is the 1st of 6 parts of the video. Emit exactly ONE segment covering this whole part, using chunk-relative times: `start_time=0`, `end_time=553`.

fields.name: title
fields.description: Title of the whole video; best guess from in-chunk evidence (titles / credits / dialogue).

fields.name: description
fields.description: Summarize this chunk using only evidence from the emitted shot segments — cover essential story beats, key people/characters, important relationships/conflicts, locations, objects, and short grounded dialogue/OCR facts; do not introduce unsupported facts. Integrate events, objects, story context, visual detail, and audio/OCR/transcription facts into one account: names, relationships, intentions, conflicts, interaction evidence, and production cues like 'Action!'/'Cut!' when evidenced. Quote only short exact phrases; do not reproduce the transcript or assert an uncertain speaker.

fields.name: work
fields.description: Optional work title inferred from this chunk (for root candidate materialization).

params.summary_prompt: Summarize the video for segmentation context.

metadata.title: Game of Thrones - Cripples, Bastards, and Broken Things
metadata.description: The video opens with the HBO Entertainment logo and the iconic 'Game of Thrones' title sequence, featuring a miniature map of Westeros and Essos with growing castles and trees. The episode begins with Daenerys Targaryen and Jorah Mormont riding through the Dothraki Sea, discussing the dangers of the Shadow Lands and the Dothraki belief in the end of the world. They arrive at a Dothraki camp where Daenerys is greeted by her people. Viserys Targaryen confronts Jorah, demanding his crown and threatening to stay on the road until he gets it. Meanwhile, at Winterfell, Tyrion Lannister is found sleeping in a dog kennel by Prince Joffrey, who orders him to apologize to the Starks. Tyrion joins the Stark family for a meal, where he discusses his upcoming journey to the Wall with Catelyn Stark and Jory Cassel. The scene shifts to Bran Stark lying unconscious in bed, tended to by Old Nan, as Catelyn enters and expresses her grief over losing her firstborn.

gemini_result.title: Game of Thrones - Opening Credits and Dothraki Sea
gemini_result.description: The video begins with the Game of Thrones opening credits sequence, showing a 3D map of Westeros and Essos, including King's Landing, Winterfell, The Wall, and Vaes Dothrak. Following the credits, the scene shifts to the Dothraki Sea, where a massive horde is traveling. Daenerys Targaryen is riding a horse, looking exhausted. Jorah Mormont rides alongside her, offering her dried meat and explaining Dothraki beliefs about ghost grass. Viserys Targaryen is also seen riding, looking uncomfortable. Khal Drogo rides ahead. Later, the horde sets up camp. Daenerys is helped off her horse by Jorah and her handmaids, as she is very weak. Viserys then approaches Jorah, questioning his loyalty and past, to which Jorah responds stoically. The clip ends with a shot of dogs in a cage.

shots.description: Jorah Mormont approaches Daenerys and helps her dismount from her horse. She is visibly weak and her legs give out. Her handmaids quickly rush over to support her and lead her away.

character_entities.description: A young woman with long silver-blonde hair, being helped off her horse.
An older, bearded man in armor, helping Daenerys.
Women who assist Daenerys.

thing_entities.description: The white horse Daenerys was riding.

shots.narrative_descriptor: Jorah Mormont helps Daenerys dismount. She is very weak and her legs give out. Her handmaids rush to support her and lead her away.

shots.description: Jorah Mormont stands watching Daenerys being led away. Viserys Targaryen walks up behind him and starts a conversation.

character_entities.description: An older, bearded man in armor.
A young man with silver-blonde hair, walking up to Jorah.

shots.narrative_descriptor: Viserys Targaryen walks up to Jorah Mormont, who is watching Daenerys leave.

shots.description: An over-the-shoulder shot focusing on Viserys as he talks to Jorah, expressing his impatience and disdain for their current situation.

character_entities.description: A young man with silver-blonde hair.
An older, bearded man in armor.

shots.narrative_descriptor: Viserys Targaryen says to Jorah, 'Ми все ще недалеко від Пентоса. Пан Ілліріо гостинно приймав нас. Там буде спокійно.' Jorah replies, 'Мене не цікавлять ані гостинність, ані спокій. Я залишуся з дрого, доки він не виконає свою частину угоди, і я не отримаю корону.'

shots.description: A close-up of Jorah Mormont as he replies to Viserys.

character_entities.description: An older, bearded man in armor.

shots.narrative_descriptor: Viserys Targaryen says, 'Як забажаєте. Хоча яке жорстоке це життя, воно краще за втрату голови.'

shots.description: A close-up of Viserys Targaryen, looking arrogant, as he questions Jorah's loyalty and asks why Ned Stark exiled him.

character_entities.description: A young man with silver-blonde hair.

shots.narrative_descriptor: Viserys Targaryen asks Jorah, 'За що Старк тебе шукає? Рабів купував?'

shots.description: A close-up of Jorah Mormont, looking serious, as he answers Viserys, admitting he sold poachers into slavery.

character_entities.description: An older, bearded man in armor.

shots.narrative_descriptor: Jorah Mormont replies, 'Продавав. Упіймав кількох браконьєрів.'

shots.description: A close-up of Viserys Targaryen, smiling smugly, as he tells Jorah that he won't be punished for such things when Viserys is king.

character_entities.description: A young man with silver-blonde hair.

shots.narrative_descriptor: Viserys Targaryen smiles and says, 'За таке не каратимуть, коли я стану королем. Можеш бути певний.'

shots.description: A medium shot shows Viserys turning and walking away. Jorah stands and watches him go, his expression thoughtful.

character_entities.description: A young man with silver-blonde hair, walking away.
An older, bearded man in armor, watching Viserys.

shots.narrative_descriptor: Viserys Targaryen turns and walks away. Jorah Mormont watches him.

axis_descriptions.entity: Extract every important person/character in the range once each, merging recurrences into one canonical entry. Include foreground people/characters, speakers, addressed people/characters, partially visible but important interactors, and entities needed to understand actions, conflicts, portrayals, or relationships. Do not skip an unknown-name entity if it is distinguishable or narratively important. Skip background extras with no interaction or story function. person = real individual, character = fictional/performed role; if both a real person and their portrayed character are identifiable, emit them separately. Appearance segmentation — entities are extracted segment by segment, so a lead who recurs in every segment can crowd out the people around them. Treat each NEW person/character's first appearance as the start of a new entity segment. When several people appear at once, split that window into shorter appearance intervals and emit EVERY person in it — including secondary, briefly-seen, or interacting-but-not-central people — not only the most prominent one.

fields.name: canonical_name
fields.description: Name, in order of preference: (1) the entity's full name (a real person's real name, or a character's in-story name); (2) a name spoken or shown on screen; (3) a descriptive label built from stable, distinguishing traits (e.g. 'bald older guard', 'red-haired teen') — avoid unstable traits like clothing. These entities are later merged across videos by matching name, so the name must be specific enough that two DIFFERENT people never collide on the same name: never a bare generic ('man', 'woman', 'person'), which would wrongly merge unrelated people. One name per entity; the description elaborates further.

fields.name: entity_type
fields.description: Whether this entity is a person or a character.

fields.name: description
fields.description: Identifying description of this entity. Elaborate the entity to support its name and future cross-video merging: stable appearance, role, key relationships/interactions, and any grounded aliases or names heard. Prefer visible, spoken, OCR, or credited evidence over guesses.

fields.name: relationships
fields.description: Each relationship starting FROM this entity, output exactly as `dst_name;dst_type;rel_type;desc` (empty array if none). dst_name / dst_type must copy another emitted entity verbatim. rel_type is `actor_portrays_character` (person -> character only; desc must be `other`) or `has_social_relationship` (same type only). desc must be exactly one of family / friend / colleague / acquaintance / rival / other. Do not add evidence text, labels, colons, extra semicolons, or any value outside the enum in this field. Put relationship evidence in the entity description instead. Emit only clearly evidenced ties, not mere co-presence; emit symmetric social ties once.

fields.name: best_thumbnail
fields.description: Best, clearest, most front-facing frame of the entity. Output a list of 5 numbers `[time_sec, y_min, x_min, y_max, x_max]`: time_sec is seconds from the chunk start; the box is normalized to [0,1000] (top-left, bottom-right) and tightly encloses the entity. Output [] if not clearly visible.

fields.name: second_best_thumbnail
fields.description: A frame as different as possible from best_thumbnail — a different time, ideally a different shot, and a different pose / angle / expression. Output a list of 5 numbers `[time_sec, y_min, x_min, y_max, x_max]`: time_sec is seconds from the chunk start; the box is normalized to [0,1000] (top-left, bottom-right) and tightly encloses the entity. Output [] if not clearly visible.

fields.name: third_best_thumbnail
fields.description: A frame distinct from and far in time from both other thumbnails, so the three span the entity's varied appearances. Output a list of 5 numbers `[time_sec, y_min, x_min, y_max, x_max]`: time_sec is seconds from the chunk start; the box is normalized to [0,1000] (top-left, bottom-right) and tightly encloses the entity. fields.name: description
fields.description: Describe this shot in rich, verbose detail — cover every meaningful visual and non-visual detail: the actions and interactions, objects, setting, on-screen text, and any spoken dialogue quoted verbatim (with the speaker when certain). Refer to each person or character by the EXACT same name you give it in the `entities` field. Write enough that the shot stands on its own and, read in order, the shots reconstruct the whole story.

fields.name: entities
fields.description: Each entity evidenced in this shot, output exactly as `name;entity_type;description`. Include only entities visible, audible, or OCR-referenced in this shot: foreground or speaking persons/characters, important addressed or interacted-with people/characters, and entities needed to understand the action, conflict, portrayal, or relationship; also central places, objects, concepts, or things when they drive the shot's meaning. entity_type is one of person / character / place / object / concept / thing, lowercase. Use exactly two semicolons as separators; do not put semicolons inside name, entity_type, or description (use commas or dashes). For the `name` element (person/character): Name, in order of preference: (1) the entity's full name (a real person's real name, or a character's in-story name); (2) a name spoken or shown on screen; (3) a descriptive label built from stable, distinguishing traits (e.g. 'bald older guard', 'red-haired teen') — avoid unstable traits like clothing. Decide from all available context, not this shot alone. The name must be specific enough that two DIFFERENT people never collide on the same name: never a bare generic ('man', 'woman', 'person'). **Use that entity's exact same name in every shot.** For the `description` element: Describe the entity for IDENTIFICATION — so that, from descriptions alone, one can tell which mentions across shots are the SAME person. Write SEVERAL sentences and pack in AS MANY stable identifying properties as you can: any name or alias heard, role, gender, approximate age, ethnicity, hair (color/length/style), facial features, build/height, distinctive marks, and especially their relationship or recurring interaction with other identified entities (e.g. 'the protagonist's mother', 'the man who repeatedly argues with the bald guard'), which strongly anchors identity. Do NOT describe clothing, location, or momentary physical action — they change and do not help identification.
