metadata.title: Jon Snow's Departure
metadata.description: This segment captures Jon Snow's emotional departure from Winterfell. It begins with a somber conversation between Catelyn Stark and Cersei Lannister, where Cersei recounts the death of her son, Joffrey, and her grief. The scene shifts to the forge, where Jon speaks with Ser Rodrik Cassel about joining the Night's Watch, acknowledging the dangers beyond the Wall. Jon then visits his sister Arya, giving her a small sword and teaching her how to hold it, sharing a tender moment before she leaves. Finally, Jon says goodbye to his comatose brother Bran and his parents, Ned and Catelyn. Ned Stark expresses his sorrow at Jon's decision, but Catelyn acknowledges that Jon has made his own choice. The segment highlights the Stark family's bonds and the weight of duty as Jon prepares to leave for the Wall.

gemini_result.title: Game of Thrones - Cersei Comforts Catelyn, Jon Snow's Farewells
gemini_result.description: Cersei Lannister visits Catelyn Stark, who is keeping vigil over the unconscious Bran Stark. Cersei shares a story about losing her own firstborn child to a fever, attempting to comfort Catelyn. Later, in the Winterfell courtyard, Jon Snow watches a blacksmith work. Jaime Lannister approaches him and mockingly congratulates him on joining the Night's Watch, shaking his hand. Finally, Jon visits Arya Stark in her room as she packs. He gifts her a small, thin sword, which he had specially made for her.

axis_descriptions.entity: Extract every important person/character in the range once each, merging recurrences into one canonical entry. Include foreground people/characters, speakers, addressed people/characters, partially visible but important interactors, and entities needed to understand actions, conflicts, portrayals, or relationships. Do not skip an unknown-name entity if it is distinguishable or narratively important. Skip background extras with no interaction or story function. person = real individual, character = fictional/performed role; if both a real person and their portrayed character are identifiable, emit them separately. Appearance segmentation — entities are extracted segment by segment, so a lead who recurs in every segment can crowd out the people around them. Treat each NEW person/character's first appearance as the start of a new entity segment. When several people appear at once, split that window into shorter appearance intervals and emit EVERY person in it — including secondary, briefly-seen, or interacting-but-not-central people — not only the most prominent one.
axis_descriptions.shot: This is the 3rd of 6 parts of the video, and it is 553 seconds long. Segment this part into granular, contiguous shots using visual and audio cues: cuts, major camera-motion changes, event/action beats, dialogue or speaker turns, subject/framing/setting changes, and entity entries/exits. When unsure, prefer shorter coherent shots. Cover this part end to end with no gaps: the FIRST shot must start at 0 and the LAST shot must end at 553 (the full length of this part). Do not omit or trim static or silent spans at the head or tail.
axis_descriptions.video: This is the 3rd of 6 parts of the video. Emit exactly ONE segment covering this whole part, using chunk-relative times: `start_time=0`, `end_time=553`.

segment_definitions.description: Extract every important person/character in the range once each, merging recurrences into one canonical entry. Include foreground people/characters, speakers, addressed people/characters, partially visible but important interactors, and entities needed to understand actions, conflicts, portrayals, or relationships. Do not skip an unknown-name entity if it is distinguishable or narratively important. Skip background extras with no interaction or story function. person = real individual, character = fictional/performed role; if both a real person and their portrayed character are identifiable, emit them separately. Appearance segmentation — entities are extracted segment by segment, so a lead who recurs in every segment can crowd out the people around them. Treat each NEW person/character's first appearance as the start of a new entity segment. When several people appear at once, split that window into shorter appearance intervals and emit EVERY person in it — including secondary, briefly-seen, or interacting-but-not-central people — not only the most prominent one.

fields.name: canonical_name
fields.description: Name, in order of preference: (1) the entity's full name (a real person's real name, or a character's in-story name); (2) a name spoken or shown on screen; (3) a descriptive label built from stable, distinguishing traits (e.g. 'bald older guard', 'red-haired teen') — avoid unstable traits like clothing. These entities are later merged across videos by matching name, so the name must be specific enough that two DIFFERENT people never collide on the same name: never a bare generic ('man', 'woman', 'person'), which would wrongly merge unrelated people. One name per entity; the description elaborates further.

fields.name: entity_type
fields.description: Whether this entity is a person or a character.

fields.name: description
fields.description: Identifying description of this entity. Elaborate the entity to support its name and future cross-video merging: stable appearance, role, key relationships/interactions, and any grounded aliases or names heard. Prefer visible, spoken, OCR, or credited evidence over guesses.

fields.name: relationships
fields.description: Each relationship starting FROM this entity, output exactly as `dst_name;dst_type;rel_type;desc` (empty array if none). dst_name / dst_type must copy another emitted entity verbatim. rel_type is `actor_portrays_character` (person -> character only; desc must be `other`) or `has_social_relationship` (same type only). desc must be exactly one of family / friend / colleague / acquaintance / rival / other. Do not add evidence text, labels, colons, extra semicolons, or any value outside the enum in this field. Put relationship evidence in the entity description instead. Emit only clearly evidenced ties, not mere co-presence; emit symmetric social ties once.

fields.name: best_thumbnail
fields.description: Best, clearest, most front-facing frame of the entity. Output a list of 5 numbers `[time_sec, y_min, x_min, y_max, x_max]`: time_sec is seconds from the chunk start; the box is normalized to [0,1000] (top-left, bottom-right) and tightly encloses the entity. Output [] if not clearly visible.

fields.name: second_best_thumbnail
fields.description: A frame as different as possible from best_thumbnail — a different time, ideally a different shot, and a different pose / angle / expression. Output a list of 5 numbers `[time_sec, y_min, x_min, y_max, x_max]`: time_sec is seconds from the chunk start; the box is normalized to [0,1000] (top-left, bottom-right) and tightly encloses the entity. Output [] if not clearly visible.

fields.name: third_best_thumbnail
fields.description: A frame distinct from and far in time from both other thumbnails, so the three span the entity's varied appearances. Output a list of 5 numbers `[time_sec, y_min, x_min, y_max, x_max]`: time_sec is seconds from the chunk start; the box is normalized to [0,1000] (top-left, bottom-right) and tightly encloses the entity. Output [] if not clearly visible.

segment_definitions.description: This is the 3rd of 6 parts of the video, and it is 553 seconds long. Segment this part into granular, contiguous shots using visual and audio cues: cuts, major camera-motion changes, event/action beats, dialogue or speaker turns, subject/framing/setting changes, and entity entries/exits. When unsure, prefer shorter coherent shots. Cover this part end to end with no gaps: the FIRST shot must start at 0 and the LAST shot must end at 553 (the full length of this part). Do not omit or trim static or silent spans at the head or tail.

fields.name: description
fields.description: Describe this shot in rich, verbose detail — cover every meaningful visual and non-visual detail: the actions and interactions, objects, setting, on-screen text, and any spoken dialogue quoted verbatim (with the speaker when certain). Refer to each person or character by the EXACT same name you give it in the `entities` field. Write enough that the shot stands on its own and, read in order, the shots reconstruct the whole story.

fields.name: shot_type
fields.description: The type of the shot.

fields.name: camera_motion
fields.description: The camera motion of the shot.

fields.name: camera_angle
fields.description: The camera angle of the shot.

fields.name: entities
fields.description: Each entity evidenced in this shot, output exactly as `name;entity_type;description`. Include only entities visible, audible, or OCR-referenced in this shot: foreground or speaking persons/characters, important addressed or interacted-with people/characters, and entities needed to understand the action, conflict, portrayal, or relationship; also central places, objects, concepts, or things when they drive the shot's meaning. entity_type is one of person / character / place / object / concept / thing, lowercase. Use exactly two semicolons as separators; do not put semicolons inside name, entity_type, or description (use commas or dashes). For the `name` element (person/character): Name, in order of preference: (1) the entity's full name (a real person's real name, or a character's in-story name); (2) a name spoken or shown on screen; (3) a descriptive label built from stable, distinguishing traits (e.g. 'bald older guard', 'red-haired teen') — avoid unstable traits like clothing. Decide from all available context, not this shot alone. The name must be specific enough that two DIFFERENT people never collide on the same name: never a bare generic ('man', 'woman', 'person'). **Use that entity's exact same name in every shot.** For the `description` element: Describe the entity for IDENTIFICATION — so that, from descriptions alone, one can tell which mentions across shots are the SAME person. Write SEVERAL sentences and pack in AS MANY stable identifying properties as you can: any name or alias heard, role, gender, approximate age, ethnicity, hair (color/length/style), facial features, build/height, distinctive marks, and especially their relationship or recurring interaction with other identified entities (e.g. 'the protagonist's mother', 'the man who repeatedly argues with the bald guard'), which strongly anchors identity. Do NOT describe clothing, location, or momentary physical action — they change and do not help identification.

segment_definitions.description: This is the 3rd of 6 parts of the video. Emit exactly ONE segment covering this whole part, using chunk-relative times: `start_time=0`, `end_time=553`.

fields.name: title
fields.description: Title of the whole video; best guess from in-chunk evidence (titles / credits / dialogue).

fields.name: description
fields.description: Summarize this chunk using only evidence from the emitted shot segments — cover essential story beats, key people/characters, important relationships/conflicts, locations, objects, and short grounded dialogue/OCR facts; do not introduce unsupported facts. Integrate events, objects, story context, visual detail, and audio/OCR/transcription facts into one account: names, relationships, intentions, conflicts, interaction evidence, and production cues like 'Action!'/'Cut!' when evidenced. Quote only short exact phrases; do not reproduce the transcript or assert an uncertain speaker.

fields.name: work
fields.description: Optional work title inferred from this chunk (for root candidate materialization).

params.summary_prompt: Summarize the video for segmentation context.

metadata.description: Ned Stark is a nobleman with long brown hair and a beard, wearing dark clothing. He is seen in a dimly lit room, looking down with a somber expression.
Catelyn Stark is a woman with long reddish-brown hair, wearing a green dress. She is seen crying in a dimly lit room, looking distressed.
Jon Snow is a young man with dark curly hair, wearing a fur-collared cloak. He is seen carrying a saddle and talking to Robb Stark in a courtyard.
Ned Stark is seen riding a horse with Jon Snow, wearing a fur-collared cloak. He is talking to Jon in a grassy field.
Robert Baratheon is a large man with a beard, wearing a fur-collared cloak. He is seen sitting at a table outdoors, talking to Ned Stark.
Daenerys Targaryen is a woman with long blonde hair, seen in a dimly lit tent with Khal Drogo. She is lying on a bed, looking distressed.
Jon Snow is seen in a dark forest, wearing a fur-collared cloak. He is sitting by a fire, looking somber.

In a dimly lit stone room, Eddard Stark sits beside his wife, Catelyn Stark, who is visibly distraught and crying. Eddard looks at her with a pained expression, then turns away and stands up to leave the room.

metadata.title: Game of Thrones - Part 3
metadata.description: This segment follows multiple storylines: Ned Stark comforts a grieving woman in a dimly lit room before departing. Jon Snow bids farewell to Robb Stark in a bustling courtyard, carrying a saddle, and later rides with Ned across a green field, discussing his mother and Ned's past. King Robert Baratheon and Ned share a meal outdoors, discussing past loves and the threat of Daenerys Targaryen, with Robert expressing his intent to destroy the Targaryens. Daenerys is shown in a Dothraki camp, first in a communal gathering, then in a tense intimate scene with Drogo, with dragon eggs visible nearby. Finally, Jon Snow is in a dark forest with Tyrion Lannister and new recruits, where Tyrion reads from a book and explains the Night's Watch oath, emphasizing that 'Ours is the fury' and 'We are the shield that guards the realms of men.'

gemini_result.title: Game of Thrones - Farewells and Journeys
gemini_result.description: Ned Stark bids a difficult farewell to his wife Catelyn, who is grieving over their comatose son Bran. In the courtyard of Winterfell, Jon Snow and Robb Stark share a brotherly goodbye before Jon departs for the Wall. A large party rides out from Winterfell, eventually splitting as Ned heads south to King's Landing and Jon heads north with his uncle Benjen. Before parting, Jon asks Ned about his mother, and Ned promises to tell him the truth the next time they meet. Later, on the Kingsroad, King Robert Baratheon halts his procession for a picnic with Ned. They reminisce about their youth, past loves like Bessie and Wylla, and the war. Their conversation is interrupted by a messenger bearing a scroll, which reveals that Daenerys Targaryen has married a Dothraki warlord.

axis_descriptions.entity: Extract every important person/character in the range once each, merging recurrences into one canonical entry. Include foreground people/characters, speakers, addressed people/characters, partially visible but important interactors, and entities needed to understand actions, conflicts, portrayals, or relationships. Do not skip an unknown-name entity if it is distinguishable or narratively important. Skip background extras with no interaction or story function. person = real individual, character = fictional/performed role; if both a real person and their portrayed character are identifiable, emit them separately. Appearance segmentation — entities are extracted segment by segment, so a lead who recurs in every segment can crowd out the people around them. Treat each NEW person/character's first appearance as the start of a new entity segment. When several people appear at once, split that window into shorter appearance intervals and emit EVERY person in it — including secondary, briefly-seen, or interacting-but-not-central people — not only the most prominent one.
axis_descriptions.shot: This is the 4th of 6 parts of the video, and it is 553 seconds long. Segment this part into granular, contiguous shots using visual and audio cues: cuts, major camera-motion changes, event/action beats, dialogue or speaker turns, subject/framing/setting changes, and entity entries/exits. When unsure, prefer shorter coherent shots. Cover this part end to end with no gaps: the FIRST shot must start at 0 and the LAST shot must end at 553 (the full length of this part). Do not omit or trim static or silent spans at the head or tail.
axis_descriptions.video: This is the 4th of 6 parts of the video. Emit exactly ONE segment covering this whole part, using chunk-relative times: `start_time=0`, `end_time=553`.

segment_definitions.description: Extract every important person/character in the range once each, merging recurrences into one canonical entry. Include foreground people/characters, speakers, addressed people/characters, partially visible but important interactors, and entities needed to understand actions, conflicts, portrayals, or relationships. Do not skip an unknown-name entity if it is distinguishable or narratively important. Skip background extras with no interaction or story function. person = real individual, character = fictional/performed role; if both a real person and their portrayed character are identifiable, emit them separately. Appearance segmentation — entities are extracted segment by segment, so a lead who recurs in every segment can crowd out the people around them. Treat each NEW person/character's first appearance as the start of a new entity segment. When several people appear at once, split that window into shorter appearance intervals and emit EVERY person in it — including secondary, briefly-seen, or interacting-but-not-central people — not only the most prominent one.

fields.name: canonical_name
fields.description: Name, in order of preference: (1) the entity's full name (a real person's real name, or a character's in-story name); (2) a name spoken or shown on screen; (3) a descriptive label built from stable, distinguishing traits (e.g. 'bald older guard', 'red-haired teen') — avoid unstable traits like clothing. These entities are later merged across videos by matching name, so the name must be specific enough that two DIFFERENT people never collide on the same name: never a bare generic ('man', 'woman', 'person'), which would wrongly merge unrelated people. One name per entity; the description elaborates further.

fields.name: entity_type
fields.description: Whether this entity is a person or a character.

fields.name: description
fields.description: Identifying description of this entity. Elaborate the entity to support its name and future cross-video merging: stable appearance, role, key relationships/interactions, and any grounded aliases or names heard. Prefer visible, spoken, OCR, or credited evidence over guesses.

fields.name: relationships
fields.description: Each relationship starting FROM this entity, output exactly as `dst_name;dst_type;rel_type;desc` (empty array if none). dst_name / dst_type must copy another emitted entity verbatim. rel_type is `actor_portrays_character` (person -> character only; desc must be `other`) or `has_social_relationship` (same type only). desc must be exactly one of family / friend / colleague / acquaintance / rival / other. Do not add evidence text, labels, colons, extra semicolons, or any value outside the enum in this field. Put relationship evidence in the entity description instead. Emit only clearly evidenced ties, not mere co-presence; emit symmetric social ties once.

fields.name: best_thumbnail
fields.description: Best, clearest, most front-facing frame of the entity. Output a list of 5 numbers `[time_sec, y_min, x_min, y_max, x_max]`: time_sec is seconds from the chunk start; the box is normalized to [0,1000] (top-left, bottom-right) and tightly encloses the entity. Output [] if not clearly visible.

fields.name: second_best_thumbnail
fields.description: A frame as different as possible from best_thumbnail — a different time, ideally a different shot, and a different pose / angle / expression. Output a list of 5 numbers `[time_sec, y_min, x_min, y_max, x_max]`: time_sec is seconds from the chunk start; the box is normalized to [0,1000] (top-left, bottom-right) and tightly encloses the entity. Output [] if not clearly visible.

fields.name: third_best_thumbnail
fields.description: A frame distinct from and far in time from both other thumbnails, so the three span the entity's varied appearances. Output a list of 5 numbers `[time_sec, y_min, x_min, y_max, x_max]`: time_sec is seconds from the chunk start; the box is normalized to [0,1000] (top-left, bottom-right) and tightly encloses the entity. Output [] if not clearly visible.

segment_definitions.description: This is the 4th of 6 parts of the video, and it is 553 seconds long. Segment this part into granular, contiguous shots using visual and audio cues: cuts, major camera-motion changes, event/action beats, dialogue or speaker turns, subject/framing/setting changes, and entity entries/exits. When unsure, prefer shorter coherent shots. Cover this part end to end with no gaps: the FIRST shot must start at 0 and the LAST shot must end at 553 (the full length of this part). Do not omit or trim static or silent spans at the head or tail.

fields.name: description
fields.description: Describe this shot in rich, verbose detail — cover every meaningful visual and non-visual detail: the actions and interactions, objects, setting, on-screen text, and any spoken dialogue quoted verbatim (with the speaker when certain). Refer to each person or character by the EXACT same name you give it in the `entities` field. Write enough that the shot stands on its own and, read in order, the shots reconstruct the whole story.

fields.name: shot_type
fields.description: The type of the shot.

fields.name: camera_motion
fields.description: The camera motion of the shot.

fields.name: camera_angle
fields.description: The camera angle of the shot.

fields.name: entities
fields.description: Each entity evidenced in this shot, output exactly as `name;entity_type;description`. Include only entities visible, audible, or OCR-referenced in this shot: foreground or speaking persons/characters, important addressed or interacted-with people/characters, and entities needed to understand the action, conflict, portrayal, or relationship; also central places, objects, concepts, or things when they drive the shot's meaning. entity_type is one of person / character / place / object / concept / thing, lowercase. Use exactly two semicolons as separators; do not put semicolons inside name, entity_type, or description (use commas or dashes). For the `name` element (person/character): Name, in order of preference: (1) the entity's full name (a real person's real name, or a character's in-story name); (2) a name spoken or shown on screen; (3) a descriptive label built from stable, distinguishing traits (e.g. 'bald older guard', 'red-haired teen') — avoid unstable traits like clothing. Decide from all available context, not this shot alone. The name must be specific enough that two DIFFERENT people never collide on the same name: never a bare generic ('man', 'woman', 'person'). **Use that entity's exact same name in every shot.** For the `description` element: Describe the entity for IDENTIFICATION — so that, from descriptions alone, one can tell which mentions across shots are the SAME person. Write SEVERAL sentences and pack in AS MANY stable identifying properties as you can: any name or alias heard, role, gender, approximate age, ethnicity, hair (color/length/style), facial features, build/height, distinctive marks, and especially their relationship or recurring interaction with other identified entities (e.g. 'the protagonist's mother', 'the man who repeatedly argues with the bald guard'), which strongly anchors identity. Do NOT describe clothing, location, or momentary physical action — they change and do not help identification.

segment_definitions.description: This is the 4th of 6 parts of the video. Emit exactly ONE segment covering this whole part, using chunk-relative times: `start_time=0`, `end_time=553`.

fields.name: title
fields.description: Title of the whole video; best guess from in-chunk evidence (titles / credits / dialogue).

fields.name: description
fields.description: Summarize this chunk using only evidence from the emitted shot segments — cover essential story beats, key people/characters, important relationships/conflicts, locations, objects, and short grounded dialogue/OCR facts; do not introduce unsupported facts. Integrate events, objects, story context, visual detail, and audio/OCR/transcription facts into one account: names, relationships, intentions, conflicts, interaction evidence, and production cues like 'Action!'/'Cut!' when evidenced. Quote only short exact phrases; do not reproduce the transcript or assert an uncertain speaker.

fields.name: work
fields.description: Optional work title inferred from this chunk (for root candidate materialization).

metadata.title: Game of Thrones - Catelyn's Revelation
metadata.description: This segment follows multiple storylines: Jon Snow and Tyrion Lannister discuss Jon's heritage and the Night's Watch in a forest setting. In Winterfell, Catelyn Stark tends to her injured son Bran while Robb Stark and Maester Luwin discuss the costs of hosting King Robert. A mysterious assassin attacks Catelyn, but she defends herself and her son, killing the attacker. Meanwhile, Daenerys Targaryen is tended to by her handmaidens in a tent, where she learns about dragons and the moon from Jhiqui. The segment concludes with Catelyn Stark confronting Robb, Tyrion, and Lord Hoster Tully at the Godswood, revealing that Bran was pushed from the tower and that the assassin's dagger belonged to the Lannisters, sparking a potential conflict.

gemini_result.title: Game of Thrones - The Kingsroad
gemini_result.description: Jon Snow and Tyrion Lannister converse by a campfire on their way to the Wall. Tyrion mocks the Night's Watch, and Jon drinks from Tyrion's wineskin. In Winterfell, Catelyn Stark keeps a vigil over the comatose Bran Stark. Maester Luwin and Robb Stark attempt to persuade her to leave the room and attend to her duties, but she refuses. A fire breaks out outside, drawing Robb away. An assassin enters the room to kill Bran. Catelyn fights him off, grabbing his Valyrian steel dagger with her bare hands. Bran's direwolf, Summer, enters and kills the assassin, saving them. In the Dothraki camp, Daenerys Targaryen is bathed by her handmaidens. She asks them about dragons, and Doreah tells her a myth about the moon being a dragon's egg.

axis_descriptions.entity: Extract every important person/character in the range once each, merging recurrences into one canonical entry. Include foreground people/characters, speakers, addressed people/characters, partially visible but important interactors, and entities needed to understand actions, conflicts, portrayals, or relationships. Do not skip an unknown-name entity if it is distinguishable or narratively important. Skip background extras with no interaction or story function. person = real individual, character = fictional/performed role; if both a real person and their portrayed character are identifiable, emit them separately. Appearance segmentation — entities are extracted segment by segment, so a lead who recurs in every segment can crowd out the people around them. Treat each NEW person/character's first appearance as the start of a new entity segment. When several people appear at once, split that window into shorter appearance intervals and emit EVERY person in it — including secondary, briefly-seen, or interacting-but-not-central people — not only the most prominent one.
axis_descriptions.shot: This is the 5th of 6 parts of the video, and it is 552 seconds long. Segment this part into granular, contiguous shots using visual and audio cues: cuts, major camera-motion changes, event/action beats, dialogue or speaker turns, subject/framing/setting changes, and entity entries/exits. When unsure, prefer shorter coherent shots. Cover this part end to end with no gaps: the FIRST shot must start at 0 and the LAST shot must end at 552 (the full length of this part). Do not omit or trim static or silent spans at the head or tail.
axis_descriptions.video: This is the 5th of 6 parts of the video. Emit exactly ONE segment covering this whole part, using chunk-relative times: `start_time=0`, `end_time=552`.

Skip background extras with no interaction or story function. person = real individual, character = fictional/performed role; if both a real person and their portrayed character are identifiable, emit them separately. Appearance segmentation — entities are extracted segment by segment, so a lead who recurs in every segment can crowd out the people around them. Treat each NEW person/character's first appearance as the start of a new entity segment. When several people appear at once, split that window into shorter appearance intervals and emit EVERY person in it — including secondary, briefly-seen, or interacting-but-not-central people — not only the most prominent one.

fields.name: canonical_name
fields.description: Name, in order of preference: (1) the entity's full name (a real person's real name, or a character's in-story name); (2) a name spoken or shown on screen; (3) a descriptive label built from stable, distinguishing traits (e.g. 'bald older guard', 'red-haired teen') — avoid unstable traits like clothing. These entities are later merged across videos by matching name, so the name must be specific enough that two DIFFERENT people never collide on the same name: never a bare generic ('man', 'woman', 'person'), which would wrongly merge unrelated people. One name per entity; the description elaborates further.

fields.name: entity_type
fields.description: Whether this entity is a person or a character.

fields.name: description
fields.description: Identifying description of this entity. Elaborate the entity to support its name and future cross-video merging: stable appearance, role, key relationships/interactions, and any grounded aliases or names heard. Prefer visible, spoken, OCR, or credited evidence over guesses.

fields.name: relationships
fields.description: Each relationship starting FROM this entity, output exactly as `dst_name;dst_type;rel_type;desc` (empty array if none). dst_name / dst_type must copy another emitted entity verbatim. rel_type is `actor_portrays_character` (person -> character only; desc must be `other`) or `has_social_relationship` (same type only). desc must be exactly one of family / friend / colleague / acquaintance / rival / other. Do not add evidence text, labels, colons, extra semicolons, or any value outside the enum in this field. Put relationship evidence in the entity description instead. Emit only clearly evidenced ties, not mere co-presence; emit symmetric social ties once.

fields.name: best_thumbnail
fields.description: Best, clearest, most front-facing frame of the entity. Output a list of 5 numbers `[time_sec, y_min, x_min, y_max, x_max]`: time_sec is seconds from the chunk start; the box is normalized to [0,1000] (top-left, bottom-right) and tightly encloses the entity. Output [] if not clearly visible.

fields.name: second_best_thumbnail
fields.description: A frame as different as possible from best_thumbnail — a different time, ideally a different shot, and a different pose / angle / expression. Output a list of 5 numbers `[time_sec, y_min, x_min, y_max, x_max]`: time_sec is seconds from the chunk start; the box is normalized to [0,1000] (top-left, bottom-right) and tightly encloses the entity. Output [] if not clearly visible.

fields.name: third_best_thumbnail
fields.description: A frame distinct from and far in time from both other thumbnails, so the three span the entity's varied appearances. Output a list of 5 numbers `[time_sec, y_min, x_min, y_max, x_max]`: time_sec is seconds from the chunk start; the box is normalized to [0,1000] (top-left, bottom-right) and tightly encloses the entity. Output [] if not clearly visible.

Segment this part into granular, contiguous shots using visual and audio cues: cuts, major camera-motion changes, event/action beats, dialogue or speaker turns, subject/framing/setting changes, and entity entries/exits. When unsure, prefer shorter coherent shots. Cover this part end to end with no gaps: the FIRST shot must start at 0 and the LAST shot must end at 552 (the full length of this part). Do not omit or trim static or silent spans at the head or tail.

fields.name: description
fields.description: Describe this shot in rich, verbose detail — cover every meaningful visual and non-visual detail: the actions and interactions, objects, setting, on-screen text, and any spoken dialogue quoted verbatim (with the speaker when certain). Refer to each person or character by the EXACT same name you give it in the `entities` field. Write enough that the shot stands on its own and, read in order, the shots reconstruct the whole story.

fields.name: shot_type
fields.description: The type of the shot.

fields.name: camera_motion
fields.description: The camera motion of the shot.

fields.name: camera_angle
fields.description: The camera angle of the shot.

fields.name: entities
fields.description: Each entity evidenced in this shot, output exactly as `name;entity_type;description`. Include only entities visible, audible, or OCR-referenced in this shot: foreground or speaking persons/characters, important addressed or interacted-with people/characters, and entities needed to understand the action, conflict, portrayal, or relationship; also central places, objects, concepts, or things when they drive the shot's meaning. entity_type is one of person / character / place / object / concept / thing, lowercase. Use exactly two semicolons as separators; do not put semicolons inside name, entity_type, or description (use commas or dashes). For the `name` element (person/character): Name, in order of preference: (1) the entity's full name (a real person's real name, or a character's in-story name); (2) a name spoken or shown on screen; (3) a descriptive label built from stable, distinguishing traits (e.g. 'bald older guard', 'red-haired teen') — avoid unstable traits like clothing. Decide from all available context, not this shot alone. The name must be specific enough that two DIFFERENT people never collide on the same name: never a bare generic ('man', 'woman', 'person'). **Use that entity's exact same name in every shot.** For the `description` element: Describe the entity for IDENTIFICATION — so that, from descriptions alone, one can tell which mentions across shots are the SAME person. Write SEVERAL sentences and pack in AS MANY stable identifying properties as you can: any name or alias heard, role, gender, approximate age, ethnicity, hair (color/length/style), facial features, build/height, distinctive marks, and especially their relationship or recurring interaction with other identified entities (e.g. 'the protagonist's mother', 'the man who repeatedly argues with the bald guard'), which strongly anchors identity. Do NOT describe clothing, location, or momentary physical action — they change and do not help identification.

segment_definitions.description: This is the 5th of 6 parts of the video. Emit exactly ONE segment covering this whole part, using chunk-relative times: `start_time=0`, `end_time=552`.

fields.name: title
fields.description: Title of the whole video; best guess from in-chunk evidence (titles / credits / dialogue).

fields.name: description
fields.description: Summarize this chunk using only evidence from the emitted shot segments — cover essential story beats, key people/characters, important relationships/conflicts, locations, objects, and short grounded dialogue/OCR facts; do not introduce unsupported facts. Integrate events, objects, story context, visual detail, and audio/OCR/transcription facts into one account: names, relationships, intentions, conflicts, interaction evidence, and production cues like 'Action!'/'Cut!' when evidenced. Quote only short exact phrases; do not reproduce the transcript or assert an uncertain speaker.

metadata.title: Game of Thrones - Part 5
metadata.description: This segment follows several intertwined storylines. In a dark forest, Catelyn Stark discusses Bran's safety with Robb and Tyrion, expressing her fear for his life. She later places a protective scarecrow by Bran's bedside and kisses his forehead. In a candlelit tent, Jorah Mormont instructs Daenerys Targaryen on how to please her husband, Drogo, before their intimate encounter. Meanwhile, Sansa Stark arrives at a bustling camp with her direwolf, Lady, and is introduced to the intimidating Vargo Hoat and the charming Joffrey Baratheon. The segment concludes with Joffrey bullying a butcher's son, leading to Arya Stark intervening and a fight breaking out, which Sansa desperately tries to stop.

gemini_result.title: Game of Thrones - Catelyn's Decision and Daenerys's Lesson
gemini_result.description: Catelyn Stark decides to travel to King's Landing to inform her husband about the attempt on Bran's life. She leaves Bran in the care of Robb. Meanwhile, across the Narrow Sea, Doreah teaches Daenerys Targaryen how to please Khal Drogo and take control in their relationship. Daenerys then applies these lessons when Drogo enters her tent.

axis_descriptions.entity: Extract every important person/character in the range once each, merging recurrences into one canonical entry. Include foreground people/characters, speakers, addressed people/characters, partially visible but important interactors, and entities needed to understand actions, conflicts, portrayals, or relationships. Do not skip an unknown-name entity if it is distinguishable or narratively important. Skip background extras with no interaction or story function. person = real individual, character = fictional/performed role; if both a real person and their portrayed character are identifiable, emit them separately. Appearance segmentation — entities are extracted segment by segment, so a lead who recurs in every segment can crowd out the people around them. Treat each NEW person/character's first appearance as the start of a new entity segment. When several people appear at once, split that window into shorter appearance intervals and emit EVERY person in it — including secondary, briefly-seen, or interacting-but-not-central people — not only the most prominent one.
axis_descriptions.shot: This is the 6th of 6 parts of the video, and it is 553 seconds long. Segment this part into granular, contiguous shots using visual and audio cues: cuts, major camera-motion changes, event/action beats, dialogue or speaker turns, subject/framing/setting changes, and entity entries/exits. When unsure, prefer shorter coherent shots. Cover this part end to end with no gaps: the FIRST shot must start at 0 and the LAST shot must end at 553 (the full length of this part). Do not omit or trim static or silent spans at the head or tail.
axis_descriptions.video: This is the 6th of 6 parts of the video. Emit exactly ONE segment covering this whole part, using chunk-relative times: `start_time=0`, `end_time=553`.

segment_definitions.description: Extract every important person/character in the range once each, merging recurrences into one canonical entry. Include foreground people/characters, speakers, addressed people/characters, partially visible but important interactors, and entities needed to understand actions, conflicts, portrayals, or relationships. Do not skip an unknown-name entity if it is distinguishable or narratively important. Skip background extras with no interaction or story function. person = real individual, character = fictional/performed role; if both a real person and their portrayed character are identifiable, emit them separately. Appearance segmentation — entities are extracted segment by segment, so a lead who recurs in every segment can crowd out the people around them. Treat each NEW person/character's first appearance as the start of a new entity segment. When several people appear at once, split that window into shorter appearance intervals and emit EVERY person in it — including secondary, briefly-seen, or interacting-but-not-central people — not only the most prominent one.

fields.name: canonical_name
fields.description: Name, in order of preference: (1) the entity's full name (a real person's real name, or a character's in-story name); (2) a name spoken or shown on screen; (3) a descriptive label built from stable, distinguishing traits (e.g. 'bald older guard', 'red-haired teen') — avoid unstable traits like clothing. These entities are later merged across videos by matching name, so the name must be specific enough that two DIFFERENT people never collide on the same name: never a bare generic ('man', 'woman', 'person'), which would wrongly merge unrelated people. One name per entity; the description elaborates further.

fields.name: entity_type
fields.description: Whether this entity is a person or a character.

fields.name: description
fields.description: Identifying description of this entity. Elaborate the entity to support its name and future cross-video merging: stable appearance, role, key relationships/interactions, and any grounded aliases or names heard. Prefer visible, spoken, OCR, or credited evidence over guesses.

fields.name: relationships
fields.description: Each relationship starting FROM this entity, output exactly as `dst_name;dst_type;rel_type;desc` (empty array if none). dst_name / dst_type must copy another emitted entity verbatim. rel_type is `actor_portrays_character` (person -> character only; desc must be `other`) or `has_social_relationship` (same type only). desc must be exactly one of family / friend / colleague / acquaintance / rival / other. Do not add evidence text, labels, colons, extra semicolons, or any value outside the enum in this field. Put relationship evidence in the entity description instead. Emit only clearly evidenced ties, not mere co-presence; emit symmetric social ties once.

fields.name: best_thumbnail
fields.description: Best, clearest, most front-facing frame of the entity. Output a list of 5 numbers `[time_sec, y_min, x_min, y_max, x_max]`: time_sec is seconds from the chunk start; the box is normalized to [0,1000] (top-left, bottom-right) and tightly encloses the entity. Output [] if not clearly visible.

fields.name: second_best_thumbnail
fields.description: A frame as different as possible from best_thumbnail — a different time, ideally a different shot, and a different pose / angle / expression. Output a list of 5 numbers `[time_sec, y_min, x_min, y_max, x_max]`: time_sec is seconds from the chunk start; the box is normalized to [0,1000] (top-left, bottom-right) and tightly encloses the entity. Output [] if not clearly visible.

fields.name: third_best_thumbnail
fields.description: A frame distinct from and far in time from both other thumbnails, so the three span the entity's varied appearances. Output a list of 5 numbers `[time_sec, y_min, x_min, y_max, x_max]`: time_sec is seconds from the chunk start; the box is normalized to [0,1000] (top-left, bottom-right) and tightly encloses the entity. Output [] if not clearly visible.

segment_definitions.description: This is the 6th of 6 parts of the video, and it is 553 seconds long. Segment this part into granular, contiguous shots using visual and audio cues: cuts, major camera-motion changes, event/action beats, dialogue or speaker turns, subject/framing/setting changes, and entity entries/exits. When unsure, prefer shorter coherent shots. Cover this part end to end with no gaps: the FIRST shot must start at 0 and the LAST shot must end at 553 (the full length of this part). Do not omit or trim static or silent spans at the head or tail.

fields.name: description
fields.description: Describe this shot in rich, verbose detail — cover every meaningful visual and non-visual detail: the actions and interactions, objects, setting, on-screen text, and any spoken dialogue quoted verbatim (with the speaker when certain). Refer to each person or character by the EXACT same name you give it in the `entities` field. Write enough that the shot stands on its own and, read in order, the shots reconstruct the whole story.

fields.name: shot_type
fields.description: The type of the shot.

fields.name: camera_motion
fields.description: The camera motion of the shot.

fields.name: camera_angle
fields.description: The camera angle of the shot.

fields.name: entities
fields.description: Each entity evidenced in this shot, output exactly as `name;entity_type;description`. Include only entities visible, audible, or OCR-referenced in this shot: foreground or speaking persons/characters, important addressed or interacted-with people/characters, and entities needed to understand the action, conflict, portrayal, or relationship; also central places, objects, concepts, or things when they drive the shot's meaning. entity_type is one of person / character / place / object / concept / thing, lowercase. Use exactly two semicolons as separators; do not put semicolons inside name, entity_type, or description (use commas or dashes). For the `name` element (person/character): Name, in order of preference: (1) the entity's full name (a real person's real name, or a character's in-story name); (2) a name spoken or shown on screen; (3) a descriptive label built from stable, distinguishing traits (e.g. 'bald older guard', 'red-haired teen') — avoid unstable traits like clothing. Decide from all available context, not this shot alone. The name must be specific enough that two DIFFERENT people never collide on the same name: never a bare generic ('man', 'woman', 'person'). **Use that entity's exact same name in every shot.** For the `description` element: Describe the entity for IDENTIFICATION — so that, from descriptions alone, one can tell which mentions across shots are the SAME person. Write SEVERAL sentences and pack in AS MANY stable identifying properties as you can: any name or alias heard, role, gender, approximate age, ethnicity, hair (color/length/style), facial features, build/height, distinctive marks, and especially their relationship or recurring interaction with other identified entities (e.g. 'the protagonist's mother', 'the man who repeatedly argues with the bald guard'), which strongly anchors identity. Do NOT describe clothing, location, or momentary physical action — they change and do not help identification.

segment_definitions.description: This is the 6th of 6 parts of the video. Emit exactly ONE segment covering this whole part, using chunk-relative times: `start_time=0`, `end_time=553`.

fields.name: title
fields.description: Title of the whole video; best guess from in-chunk evidence (titles / credits / dialogue).

fields.name: description
fields.description: Summarize this chunk using only evidence from the emitted shot segments — cover essential story beats, key people/characters, important relationships/conflicts, locations, objects, and short grounded dialogue/OCR facts; do not introduce unsupported facts. Integrate events, objects, story context, visual detail, and audio/OCR/transcription facts into one account: names, relationships, intentions, conflicts, interaction evidence, and production cues like 'Action!'/'Cut!' when evidenced. Quote only short exact phrases; do not reproduce the transcript or assert an uncertain speaker.

fields.name: work
fields.description: Optional work title inferred from this chunk (for root candidate materialization).

params.summary_prompt: Summarize the video for segmentation context.

metadata.description: A young girl with brown hair in braids, wearing a grey dress. She is seen holding a sword and interacting with Joffrey and Sansa.
A young boy with blond hair, wearing a maroon tunic. He is seen lying on the grass with a bloody hand after being bitten by a direwolf.
A young girl with long red hair, wearing a light blue dress. She is seen standing near Arya and Joffrey, and later tending to Joffrey's wound.
A large grey and white direwolf. She is seen interacting with Arya in the woods and later running away.
A man with long brown hair and a beard, wearing a brown tunic. He is seen leading a search party through the woods at night with torches.
A woman with long blonde hair, wearing a gold and red dress. She is seen standing next to Robert Baratheon and Joffrey, accusing Arya.
A small white direwolf. She is seen standing next to Sansa Stark in the room with Robert Baratheon.
A large man with a scarred face, wearing dark armor. He is seen walking through a camp at night and later killing Lady.
A small white direwolf, chained to a post at night. She is seen being petted by Ned Stark before being killed.
A young girl with brown hair, lying in bed under a fur blanket. She is seen waking up in a dimly lit room.
A young girl, Arya, stands in a grassy area with trees in the background, holding a sword. She looks determined and slightly angry.

metadata.title: Arya's Confrontation and Nymeria's Fate
metadata.description: This segment depicts the immediate aftermath of a confrontation between Arya Stark and Joffrey Baratheon. Arya, holding a sword, stands over an injured Joffrey by a river, while Sansa Stark looks on in shock. Arya throws the sword into the water and flees into the woods, where she is confronted by her direwolf, Nymeria, who warns her of the danger she is in. Later, at night, King Robert Baratheon's men find Arya and bring her to the royal tent. A heated argument ensues between Robert, Cersei Lannister, and Sansa regarding the incident. Cersei and Sansa accuse Arya of attacking Joffrey, while Robert defends her, dismissing it as a childish fight. Despite Robert's initial reluctance, he is pressured into ordering the execution of Nymeria. Arya pleads for her wolf's life, but Robert ultimately agrees to the demand. The segment concludes with Robert visiting the chained Nymeria, releasing her, and telling her to run and never return, while Arya is shown sleeping peacefully in her bed.

gemini_result.title: Game of Thrones - Arya defends Mycah
gemini_result.description: Arya Stark disarms Joffrey Baratheon after he attacks her friend Mycah. She throws his sword in the river and runs away into the woods with her direwolf, Nymeria. Knowing Nymeria will be killed for biting Joffrey, Arya forces the wolf to leave. Later, Arya is found and brought before King Robert Baratheon and Queen Cersei Lannister at the inn. A dispute ensues over what happened, with Joffrey lying and Sansa refusing to tell the truth. Cersei demands punishment, and when it is revealed Nymeria is gone, she demands Sansa's direwolf, Lady, be killed instead.
